name: Artisan
startingSkills: A Good Defense - Basic Martial Weapons (1), Short Weapons (3)
Protective Equipment - Basic Armor (2)
Productive Equipment - Choose one Apprentice Crafting: [Alchemy, (Ritual Magic AND Ritual Lore), Enchanting, or Tinkering] (3)
The Land Provides - Choose a Gathering Skill: Forage I, Prospect I, or Scavenge I (3)
A Path Unfolds - Choose one of the below:
Apprentice & Journeyman Profession: [Your Choice] (3)
Apprentice Crafting: [Alchemy, (Ritual Magic AND Ritual Lore), Enchanting, or Tinkering] (3)
Note: This must be different from the choice made in Productive Equipment.
Basic Medicine (2) and one of [Bits & Pieces (1), Hearth (1), or Soothing Touch (1)]
multiclassSkills: Materials, Everywhere: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Choose a Gathering Skill: [Forage I, Prospect I, Scavenge] (3)

specializations.name: Artificer
specializations.description: The combination of human and machine is the Artificer’s specialty. Artificers are more likely to be Tinkers than other crafting specialties and make extensive use of Forgesources. The Artificer is a master of repairing armor and shields in combat, going toe-to-toe with their enemies and coming out alive, and taking up space on the battlefield. If you want to play an Artisan that can participate in combat, this is the specialization for you!

specializations.name: Crafter
specializations.description: When it comes to the productive makers, there is no equal to the Crafter. They are skilled craftsmen in their field and excel at creating items with more profound and useful benefits than their non-Crafter counterparts. Their toolkit includes many ways to alter the items that they have made, or enhance them to give situational mastery over niche encounters.

specializations.name: Mystic
specializations.description: The Mystic is a ritualist who specializes in mixing mundane crafting with magical or divine energies. While any mage or cleric can pick up a scroll and cast it with an objective understanding of what the magic contained may do, it is the Mystic who goes beyond, to enhance themself and their allies farther than that which is written on the crumbling parchments of the ritual scroll.

innate.name: Living Iron
innate.incantation: “By the power of my craft I repair what is broken” or Focus Quick 30 while miming repairing armor
innate.description: The Artisan gains a Living Iron pool equal to 3 x Artisan’s Class Level. This pool resets every Long Rest. The Artisan may subtract from their Living Iron pool when the power is used to Mend armor (Physical, Summoned, or Natural) up to the Artisan’s class-level. Example: A 4th level Artisan has 12 total points of Mending per Long Rests, and can Mend up to 4 points per use of the Power. Artificers gain additional points to their Living Iron pool based on the amounts below. Artisans that are Artificers gain bonuses based on how many tagged [Artificer] powers they have from all their classes. • 5+         [Artificer] Artisan Powers - Living Iron pool is 4 x Artisan’s Class levels • 7+         [Artificer] Artisan Powers - Living Iron pool is 5 x Artisan's Class levels • 10+         [Artificer] Artisan Powers - Living Iron pool is 6 x Artisan's Class levels • 15+         [Artificer] Artisan Powers - Living Iron pool is 7 x Artisan's Class levels

innate.name: Brilliant Thinker
innate.description: The Artisan learns an additional Basic Power from the Artisan Class Powers.

innate.name: The Learned One
innate.requirement: Artisan Level 4Skills and Options: The Artisan gains one of the following skills for free based on their Specialization when they attain level 4 Artisan (this one time skill bonus does not change if their Specialization later changes). The Artisan must have the prerequisites for this skill and if they do not then they may instead take the Apprentice or Basic level of the skill.
innate.description: • Artificer- Basic Shields (4), Basic Traps (5), Medium Armor (4) • Crafter- Journeyman Alchemy (4), Journeyman Enchanting (4), Journeyman Tinkering (4) • Mystic- Advanced Medicine (4), [Journeyman Ritual Magic (2) AND Ritual Lore (2)]

innate.name: Pragmatist
innate.description: The Artisan learns an additional Utility Power from the Artisan Class Powers.

innate.name: Grand Ritualist
innate.description: Mystics gain the ability to modify the Dark Territory of any ritual they are a Participant or Ritualist of, increasing or decreasing it by up to 1 if they are a Participant or up to 2 if they are the Ritualist. Additionally, after completing a ritual successfully, the Mystic gains 5 points of Summoned Armor that last for a Short Rest. Artisans that are of the Specialization Mystic may Grant each Participant of a ritual where they are the Primary Ritualist a number of Counters to Damage that do not interrupt the ritual when used. The Call for this Counter is “Counter, Grand Ritualist.” The Artisan may do this once per Long Rest. These Counters last until the next Short Rest, the end of the Ritual or until used whichever is first. The ritualist must explain these counters expire after the ritual has been completed. • 5+         [Mystic] Artisan Powers: Short Grant 1 Counter to Damage • 8+         [Mystic] Artisan powers: Short Grant 2 Counter to Damage • 11+         [Mystic] Artisan powers: Short Grant 3 Counter to Damage • 15+         [Mystic] Artisan Powers: Short Grant 4 Counter to Damage

innate.name: Technical Expertise
innate.description: The Artisan learns an additional Advanced Power from the Artisan Class Powers.

innate.name: Expert Craft
innate.skillsAndOptions: The Artisan gains a rank of the Fortunate Finder Character Option.
innate.description: If the Artisan meets the following criteria: • Artisan is the Crafter Specialization • At least 7 [Crafter] tagged Powers (Basic, Advanced, Veteran, Utility) • Possesses the prerequisite Journeyman Skill Then the Artisan also gains one of the following for free: • Greater Alchemy (5) • Greater Enchanting (5) • Greater Tinkering (5)

options.text: Greater Alchemy (5)
Greater Enchanting (5)
Greater Tinkering (5)

innate.name: Opus
innate.description: The Artisan gains one of: Aetheric Infusion if they are an Artificer, Artisan’s Reforge if they are a Crafter, or Eureka! If they are a Mystic. The Artisan may only have one Opus Power. If the Artisan’s Specialization changes, a different Opus Power will automatically replace their current one.

utility.name: A Spoonful of Sugar
utility.description: The Artisan gains the Apprentice Alchemy skill (3). Once per Long Rest, the Artisan can remove the “Mana Sickness” detriment from a single Alchemical item they produce during the crafting process.

utility.name: Adept Ritualist
utility.skillsAndOptions: The Artisan gains the Apprentice Ritual Magic skill.
utility.description: The Adept Ritualist gains the following benefits at various Artisan Levels: • Level 1 - The Artisan may count as two Participants in Rituals, taking up the available slots and giving benefits. • Level 3 - If the Artisan is acting as a Ritualist, reduce one Basic Resource (Bloom, Hide, Ingots, or Wealth) required for a Ritual to a minimum of 1, once per Long Rest. Place a stand in item if the Resource has a part in the Ritual • Level 7 - As above, the Artisan may count as two Ritualists. They may not perform functions of multiple Ritualists.

levelBenefits.text: The Artisan may count as two Participants in Rituals, taking up the available slots and giving benefits.

If the Artisan is acting as a Ritualist, reduce one Basic Resource (Bloom, Hide, Ingots, or Wealth) required for a Ritual to a minimum of 1, once per Long Rest. Place a stand in item if the Resource has a part in the Ritual
As above, the Artisan may count as two Ritualists. They may not perform functions of multiple Ritualists.

utility.name: Apt Assistant
utility.call: “[Name or Description] Instruction: Halve all repeated task repetitions and turns of the timer”
utility.description: The Artisan can streamline another crafter’s process by helping at a Crafting Station of any kind. RP handing tools, helping follow the Recipe, etc. Halve all repeated task repetitions and the number of turns on the timer (round up). Additionally, in specific cases, this power can reduce the time to accomplish Counted tasks. Note: This Power stacks with other time reduction skills for crafting, however, no repetitions / turns can be reduced below 1 each by this Power.

utility.name: Custom Creation
utility.skillsAndOptions: The Artisan gains the Apprentice Enchanting (3) skill.
utility.description: This Power has two uses which may be used separately or simultaneously. The Artisan spends a few seconds focusing on the item to alter its structure, allowing them to add or alter Attunement requirements. Item Requirements: • Crafted by the Artisan • Has a card • Requires Attunement Power Use #1 - Add Attunement After focusing, the Artisan may add any kind of Attunement requirement. Examples include something as specific as “Can only be Attuned to Teo Trastvara” and as generic as “Can only be Attuned to a worshiper of The Steed”. Attunement is not lost if the required trait changes (such as converting to a different Devotion). Power Use #2 - Add Limitation After focusing, the Artisan may add a specific limitation to an item’s activation and use. This limitation might be “Can only be activated by Humans” or “To activate the item, the character must yell ‘I love soup!’” Limitations requiring abusive or non-consensual behavior immediately fail. For either use of this Power, the item’s card must be updated with the information.

utility.name: Forged From Steel
utility.description: The Artisan gains 1 additional Mend Point per Artisan class-level to their Living Iron pool. Additionally, the Artisan may Sacrifice a Forgesource to gain 10 Mend Points to their Living Iron pool at-will. The Forgesource should be placed in an Ashbin as soon as is convenient.

utility.name: Forgesource Specialist
utility.description: The Artisan gains the skill Apprentice Tinkering (3). Forgesource Specialists may make Forgesources as an Apprentice Tinker, and may craft two simultaneously. Finally, the Artisan may replace any Uncommon resource (Rare Minerals, Harvest, Night Prizes) in a Tinkering Schematic with a Forgesource at-will.

utility.name: Good Timing
utility.description: The Artisan gains Prospect I, refunding as necessary. Additionally, you may take Prospect III before reaching 10th level. The Artisan may skip Prospecting during the first Long Rest period of an Event and instead Prospect twice per Mine/Rare Mineral Spring in the next Long Rest period.

utility.name: Hunter Gatherer
utility.description: The Artisan gains Forage I, refunding as necessary. Additionally, you may take Forage III before reaching 10th level. The Artisan is used to walking the land looking for Bloom and Night Prizes. Hunter Gatherers may take 1 additional Night Prize from a mushroom patch for every rank of Foraging they possess. This does not increase the total number of Night Prizes they may collect per Event overall, just from any particular patch.

utility.name: Mechanical Augmentation
utility.description: The Artisan can mechanically augment themselves, becoming a powered construct. They must have a Forgesource that is not being used for other things with them the entire time the Power is activated. While Augmented the Artisan has the Construct Qualifier (Not Type). In addition, when using a Power that targets themselves, Mechanically Augmented Artisans may use a Spike in lieu of a Forgesource once per Power. The Artisan is inherently slowed as their body is infused with mechanical features. The Artisan may spend 4 points of Living Iron to Counter Poison, Agony, and Fear Accents by calling “Counter, Augmentation”.

utility.name: Otherworldly Sight
utility.call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: What is your Lineage?” OR “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: Are you a/an [Exalted, Undead, Voidborn]?”
utility.description: The Artisan can examine an individual and try to discern their nature. Note that in mass melees and high pressure/speed situations, Discerning is hard and erroneous/no answer may occur.

utility.name: Perspicacity
utility.call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: What is one of your Vulnerabilities?”
utility.description: The Artisan can Focus on a particular foe to determine its weaknesses (must be a vulnerability Condition). Note that in mass melees and high pressure/speed situations, Discerning is hard and erroneous/no answer may occur.

utility.name: Protected Casting
utility.description: The Artisan can protect a ritual from disturbance with a small rite of consecration. First, the Artisan lays down a white rope or cord that is no longer than 100 feet long that has been laid down to make a continuous shape (usually a circle). Then the Artisan walks the perimeter of the rope, chanting and speaking words of mystic significance. During this time, the Artisan should also explain to the others who will take part in the Ritual how the Weapon and Packet resistance works (see below). This should take at least 30 seconds. Anyone not there for the explanation, will not get the benefit. At the completion of this invocation, the caster should touch the completed circle, and declare the casting complete, at which point the lain rope cannot be purposefully moved by anyone other than the caster, and anyone who attempts to do so should be informed by use of “Instruction: You may not move the rope.” or similar. This does not prevent people from crossing the threshold of the cord, rope, or the like. Should the rope be moved purposefully by the caster, the Power ends. This should be communicated to others trying to touch or manipulate it. The circle can be Dispelled by Dispel Magic and similar magic. The circle’s power ends as soon as a ritual completes inside it, or when moved by the caster, whichever comes first. While inside the cord and engaged in a Ritual casting, all Ritualists and Participants actively involved in a Ritual have resistance to Weapons and Packets from sources not involved with the ritual and taking damage from Weapons and Packets will not disrupt the Ritual.

utility.name: Smithy’s Blessing
utility.description: Tight spots and dangerous situations taught the Artisan to make resources stretch where others could not. The Artisan may Mend 1 to their own Armor when administering a Health Draught to another. The Artisan may also Mend 1 to their own Armor whenever they expend a Forgesource for any reason.

utility.name: Shield Bearer
utility.description: The Artisan gains the Basic Shield Skill (4). The Artisan may Sacrifice 2 Life Points to Counter any Disarm Effect against the shield.

utility.name: Spirit Guide
utility.description: The Artisan gains the Supernatural Perk: Medium (5).

utility.name: Tanner
utility.description: The Artisan gains Scavenge I, refunding as necessary. Additionally, you may take Scavenge III before reaching 10th level. The Artisan knows their way around a knife and hide. Tanners may instantly Scavenge a creature not already being Scavenged by another. The Artisan may call Counter, Tanner to any damage received while waiting for their token. Note: This Power ends once they receive their token, or if they take any actions other than waiting to receive their loot. The Counter does not prevent any damage taken from the Scavenging itself. The Counter does not stop Effects.

utility.name: Triage
utility.description: The Artisan gains the Basic Medicine and Diagnose Skills. The Artisan has seen their fair share of combat and served as a healer. Quickly diagnosing what is wrong and how to best handle the situation has become second nature. The Artisan has more questions they may ask when using Diagnose, honing their perception to uncover personality secrets based on physical observations. The wording need not be exact but the question must stay true to the spirit: • Are you currently in a heightened state of emotion/stress? • Are you under the effects of mind-altering substances? • Have you been in Spirit Form in the last 12 hours? • Have you ever had your memory altered? • Are you possessed or controlled? • What is your profession/class?

basic.name: Acid Soak
basic.description: Carefully, the Artisan processes a number of reagents in sinister ways, adding their lethal humours to soak a Packet full of materia in a solution that will react over a few seconds, making for a powerfully acidic projectile. The Artisan must work on the packet for a Focus Quick 100, sacrificing three Night Prizes, and may then throw it at any time. If the Packet leaves their hand or touches any object other than their hand, it will discharge. The Night Prizes should be deposited in an Ashbin when convenient.

basic.name: Aegis of the Unknown / Sundering of the Unknown
basic.call: “Short Grant Minus One Dark Territory” or “Short Grant Plus One Dark Territory”
basic.description: The Artisan has been around the block a few hundred times, and knows how to focus their energies in a way to limit or expand the potential for chaotic outcomes in their work. The artisan marks the target with a glyph personal to them, and the glyph either represents the contraction or expansion of possibility, depending on if they are granting less or more Dark Territory. Once the target is appropriately marked, the target’s Dark Territory modifier is modified as per the Artisan’s choice by one until their next Short Rest. The target may only be the recipient of this boon once per Event from any source. Enhancement: Sacrifice 3 Rare Minerals: The change in Dark Territory will be 2.

basic.name: Analysis
basic.call: “Subtle Discern by Mind: What are the properties of this item?”
basic.description: The Artisan can inspect any item that has an information card for a Quick Count of 100 and then read the information card. This will not make the character immune to any Effects or consequences from being in range of the item. If there is a Marshal available, the Marshal may be asked to give the properties of any item, including ones that do not have information cards, but the information given is up to the attending Marshal. Note: This power will reveal only the information available on the card, but not obfuscated effects.

basic.name: Antidote
basic.description: After a minute role-playing finding, preparing, and applying various ingredients to an individual, the Artisan can Cure the drained, slept, or tainted Condition. Enhancement: Expend a Bloom: The Artisan can Cure all Conditions (except for Obedient). Place the Bloom in the Ashbin when possible.

basic.name: Armor Upkeep Specialist
basic.description: The Artisan can alter a Field Patch Kit they are crafting, instead creating a Specialist Field Patch Kit with a 1 year expiration. The Specialist Field Patch Kit can mend armor to full once per Long Rest. This use does not consume the Kit if the Artisan who created it does the repair. Maintaining the Specialist Field Patch Kit requires dedicated attention, so the Artisan can only manage one Kit at a time. Furthermore, it will revert to a normal Field Patch Kit if this Power is lost or removed. The item’s card must be updated with these special features.

basic.name: Borrow Knowledge
basic.call: “Subtle Discern by Mind: What Lore Skills do you have?”
basic.description: Out of combat, the Artisan sits down with a willing creature and puts their hands near the other’s temples (remember not to touch unless consent has already been explicitly established). They should walk the target through a meditation, or explain how they are sifting through the other’s Skills and talents. When they are done with the role-play, the Artisan Discerns all the Lore Core Skills the target has, and gains all those Skills until they complete a Short Rest.

basic.name: Blood Scroll
basic.description: Normally when an Enchanter creates a scroll using the Scribe Novice Scroll formulae they must use Simple Ink to write the words of the scroll. The Artisan has gained the ability to ignore the alchemical part of this process and scribe the magics onto the hide using their own blood. Using Blood Scroll, the Artisan can ignore the need for Simple Ink for one scroll when they use Scribe Novice Scroll by Sacrificing 1 Life Point of blood.

basic.name: Combat Analysis
basic.description: Once per Short Rest the Artisan must watch an enemy or group of enemies carefully for a Quick Count of 30. Doing so allows the Artisan to Counter one Weapon attack by a member of that group. The Call is “Counter, Dodge”. If the Counter is not used before the Artisan goes out-of-combat or the Artisan completes a Short Rest, it is lost.

basic.name: Reconstitute Summon
basic.call: “Mend Summoned Armor to Full and Grant Three Barrier by Mind”
basic.description: The Artisan can Touch another and call “Mend Summoned Armor to Full and Grant Three Barrier by Mind.”

basic.name: Create Infusion
basic.description: Normally when an Enchanter pulls from the Reality Tear the Enchanter may either Soulhold what is pulled or place it in an Essence Infusion and leave the Reality Tear with it. The Artisan has gained the ability to ignore the alchemical part of this process and through sheer force of will bind the Essence into a form that is identical to an Essence Infusion. Using this Power, the Artisan can create an Essence Infusion without any other resources or alchemy when they use Delve Reality (any level) and may create an Essence Infusion card for one resource they pulled from the Tear without previously having an Essence Infusion to store it.

basic.name: Critique Construction
basic.description: The Artisan focuses, examining the shields around them, especially those of the enemy. The Artisan then explains the weakness to another allowing them to exploit that weakness (and must RP a short piece of advice to the target of this Power). The recipient of the explanation may use a single Shatter against a shield.

basic.name: Custom Brew
basic.description: The Artisan can alter the creation of any alchemical item they are crafting that has an associated item card to require 1 less Bloom or 1 less Night Prize (minimum 1).

basic.name: Dig Deep
basic.description: The Artisan can give a short speech (an Incantation) to up to six other individuals. They all gain the ability to refresh all their Spikes once before they complete a Short Rest.

basic.name: Forcefield
basic.call: Grant Protect vs Packets by Lightning
basic.description: The Artisan may focus while holding a Forgesource to Grant a Protect vs Packets. Enhancement: Expend the Forgesource: Target becomes up to four individuals and the Artisan.

basic.name: Intimidating Spines
basic.call: “[Name or Description]: Short Repel by Fear”
basic.description: The Artisan surrounds themselves with harmful blades, spikes, and whirling implements of doom. For the duration, the affected reels back in fear and refuses to approach them.

basic.name: Jury Rig
basic.description: By Focusing for a Quick 30 while fixing and readjusting a character’s armor, the Artisan can Mend Armor for 5 points. Enhancement: Expend two ingots or a Patch Kit: The Power refreshes immediately. Put the expended resources in the earliest convenient Ashbin.

basic.name: Kick
basic.description: An Artisan can take an existing potion (any quaffed liquid created with alchemy or tagged quaffed liquid found in the world) and add a little kick to it. After spending a minute or so adding to, mixing, and enhancing the potion, they may choose one of three benefits that will take place when the potion is imbibed: • Heal 1 • Grant 1 Barrier • Intoxicate No Potion can be Kicked more than once. The Kick effect should be added to the information card for the item if it has one. If there is no information card, either one must be made, or only the Artisan can get the Kick effects.

options.text: Heal 1
Grant 1 Barrier

Intoxicate No Potion can be Kicked more than once. The Kick effect should be added to the information card for the item if it has one. If there is no information card, either one must be made, or only the Artisan can get the Kick effects.

basic.name: Mesmerize
basic.call: “[Name or Description] Short Charm by Mind”
basic.description: The Mystic has learned the ways of hypnotism and beguilement. Out-of-combat, after a minute of conversation the Mystic may Call “[Name or Description] Short Charm by Mind” to the target of their words. This Power has no function for Artificer or Crafter Artisans.

basic.name: Overcharge
basic.call: “Short Grant Plus One Bonus Spike Damage”, “Short Grant Plus Two Bonus Spike Damage”
basic.description: The Artisan may focus while holding a Forgesource to Grant themselves a +1 bonus Spike Damage until their next Short Rest. Enhancement: Sacrifice the Forgesource: Instead the Artisan Grants themselves +2 bonus Spike Damage for the same duration.

basic.name: Share
basic.call: “[Name or Description] Discern by Mind: How do you feel right now?”, “[Name or Description] Cure [Condition or Accent]”
basic.description: This Power can only be used out of combat with another Individual and, ideally, should be done in private. The Artisan connects themself to the emotions of the other sitting in silence with them for at least a minute. After the minute, the Artisan should describe their own emotional state in honest, descriptive terms. They should not convey facts other than about how they feel (i.e. “I feel hurt and betrayed” rather than “Because Triss ended up taking Tymera on his quest instead of me, I feel hurt and betrayed”). When they feel they have fully described their feelings, they should Call “Discern: How do you feel right now?” The target should then describe their emotional state. After this sharing, until the end of the Event, the Artisan can, once, as a Verbal, Cure that character of dominated, charmed, obedient, or any Condition caused by the Fear accent. The Artisan can only have the power to Cure any one individual once at any one time (i.e. Share cannot be used multiple times on the same individual to stack the Curing Effect.)

basic.name: Spiritual Anchor
basic.call: “[Name or Description] Short Grant resistance to Insubstantial.”
basic.description: The Artisan can force an individual to remain corporeal. The Artisan can call out “[Name or Description] Short Grant resistance to Insubstantial.” Reminder: Inherent conditions, such as ones passively granted from having a Type, cannot be removed or prevented.

basic.name: Trap Recovery
basic.description: The Artisan can spend a Quick 100 gathering the pieces of a trap that they put down themself which has since gone off. This will allow a Tinker to remake the trap without any additional expenditure of resources. Alternatively, the Artisan can turn in the pieces to Staff to convert them into all the resources that were required to make the trap, minus one (i.e. the Artisan must choose one resource used to make the trap and sacrifice it). At 10 Artisan class-levels they can salvage any trap into its component resources in the above listed manner.

basic.name: Treat
basic.description: This Power can only be used out of combat. By expending three Bloom, the Artisan can provide a powerful curative brew for up to six other Individuals, moving from one to the other giving each a sip which will heal small wounds and injuries. No one target can be given more than one sip. The Artisan must Focus for at least 10 seconds over each character to be Treated. All targets are Healed for 1 Life Points. The Bloom should be deposited in an Ashbin when possible.

basic.name: Warming Elixir / Cooling Draught
basic.call: “Grant Protect vs Ice by Flame” or “Grant Protect vs Flame by Ice”
basic.description: The Artisan brews a potion expending 1 Bloom to receive the boon. When brewing, the Artisan chooses the effect and must choose the same effect for all who receive the benefit. The brew is only potent for a short time and must be consumed immediately after the Artisan finishes brewing it. After drinking the brew, up to 4 participants are granted “Grant Protect vs Ice by Flame” or “Grant Protect vs Flame by Ice.” The Bloom should be deposited in an Ashbin when possible.

basic.name: Weapon Upkeep Specialist
basic.description: The Artisan can alter the creation of a Sharpening Stone they are crafting. If this process is used, the resulting creation is called a Perfect Sharpening Stone with an expiration of 1 year. This one-use item provides a +1 bonus to Spike Damage until the user completes a Short Rest. On Thrown and Projectile weapons, the bonus is only on those specific weapons, and the weapons must be distinguishable in some way from the others. The item-cards must be updated with these special features.

basic.name: Weird Wanderings
basic.description: When this power is chosen the Artisan can choose one Basic-Tier Power from any other non-Artisan Base Class. This choice is permanent, and the Power will have the same Incantation, requirements, etc. as it did in the original class. The Power cannot have a Refresh of “Spell”.

advanced.name: Advanced Treatment
advanced.call: “Short Grant +1 Maximum Life Points by Poison.”
advanced.description: This Power can only be used out of combat. By expending one Bloom and one Rare Mineral, the Artisan can provide a powerful curative brew for up to six Individuals, moving from one to the other giving each a sip which will invigorate and fortify their bodies. No one target can be given more than one sip. The Artisan must Focus for at least 10 seconds over each character to be Treated. As each one is treated, the Artisan may call “Short Grant +1 Maximum Life Points by Poison.” The Resources should be deposited in an Ashbin when possible.

advanced.name: Detonate
advanced.description: The Artisan can prepare a weapon to ignite and explode, by preparing strange fuses, oils, and other ingredients. After the Artisan spends a Quick Count of 100 in deep Focus, role-playing this preparation, the character is ready to detonate their weapon. At any time the target chooses, a melee weapon can be ignited to use the Explosive Impact Power below. This should be explained to the target while the preparations are being made. Note: The Role-Playing of this Power may include props or equipment given to the target, or infusing the character’s limb with bizarre oils; the exact details are up to the Artisan.

advanced.name: Explosive Impact
advanced.call: “Wounding 8 by Flame”, “Shatter Weapon Final to Self”
advanced.description: A melee weapon can be used to deliver a Wounding Effect for 8 points of Flame damage. This will, unfortunately, also cause the weapon to Shatter. This should be explained to the target while the weapon is being prepared.

advanced.name: Efficiencies of Scale
advanced.description: The Artisan can perform any of the “collection” Plans (Ingot Sheets, Bloom Powder, Night Prize Essence, etc.) regardless of their Crafting Skills. They can perform any of those away from a Crafting Station (but out-of-combat) by spending at least a minute on each one and instead of the normal process substitute their own roleplay choices of crafting the item.

advanced.name: Empower Friends
advanced.call: “Refresh One Novice Spell Slot”, “Refresh One Adept Spell Slot”
advanced.description: The Artisan may use their knowledge of the mystical forces to refresh the spell slots of up to two other individuals. The Artisan restores 1 Novice and 1 Adept spell slot in total. These slots may be Arcane or Divine and may be from Base or Advanced Classes. The Artisan should roleplay an incantation of meaning to their character that lasts approximately 30 seconds or longer to use this Power. This Power may not be refreshed by any means outside of receiving a Long Rest.

advanced.name: Exploding Arrows
advanced.call: “Imbue Weapon with Shatter Shield by Flame”
advanced.description: Out-of-combat, the Artisan can treat a single projectile with a powerful explosive. This mixture is concocted by Sacrificing two Rare Mineral and two Harvest and treating an arrow. This preparation takes Focus of a Quick 100 Count. Note: Reminder that any one individual can only have one Imbued Power at any one time.

advanced.name: Grounded
advanced.description: The Artisan has so many small trinkets, wires, and other conductive objects, that Lightning based attacks have a significant chance of simply routing into the ground. Once per Short Rest, the Artisan may call “Counter, Grounded” against any effect with the Lightning Accent.

advanced.name: Hiatus
advanced.call: “Hiatus, Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Cure Insubstantial to Self.”
advanced.description: While in a Ritual, the Artisan may call “Grant Insubstantial to Self” and Focus for a Slow 300. Once the Count is complete, the Artisan must call “Cure Insubstantial to Self.” While Insubstantial, the Artisan can pause a ritual in progress in which they are the Primary Ritualist. If their Focus is broken, the Slow Count is completed, or at the Artisan’s discretion, whichever comes first, the Ritual resumes as normal, and the Artisan must call “Cure Insubstantial to Self.” Any actions taken during the Focus period should be ignored by the attending Ritual Marshal for the purposes of determining failure or Dark Territory. Note: This means that while the Artisan themselves are incapable of interacting with things, others may do so on their behalf.

advanced.name: Infuse
advanced.description: The Artisan unravels an Essence Infusion - Energy to alter and empower a weapon, giving it properties beyond its normal. This process must be done out-of-combat and should take Focus for a Quick Count of 100. The weapon should be given a tag that shows it having the following properties: It has a +1 bonus to Spike damage and can be used with any of the four elemental Accents (Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning) as desired. The tag should be given an expiration that matches the day after the Event finishes. The Essence Infusion should be deposited in an Ashbin when possible.

advanced.name: Perfect Practice
advanced.description: The Artisan gains additional benefits each time they use a Craft of Apprentice level or higher (ie: Supervise Armor Mending would not trigger this Power). • Each time the Artisan completes a Tinkering Plan, they gain 3 to their Living Iron pool. This has no effect if the Artisan already has more points in their Living Iron Pool than their reset number. • Each time the Artisan completes an Enchanting Ritual, they may gain a Protect vs Packets. • Each time the Artisan completes an Alchemy Craft, they gain the ability to reduce a Quick 100 Count to a Quick 30 Count once. The Artisan can only have one instance of this active at a time. • Each time the Artisan Mends at least 5 Armor at a time, they may also “Grant 2 Barrier” to the person whose armor was Mended.

advanced.name: Reinforce Shield
advanced.description: Whenever the Artisan uses the Rebuild Effect on a Shield, they can also Grant “Protection vs. Shatter Shield” to the shield’s wielder. If no one is wielding the shield, the Artisan may either choose not to Grant the Protect, or can take it themself.

advanced.name: Soulhold Survival
advanced.description: When the Artisan draws an Unravelling Essence that would normally kill them, they can choose to soulhold it. If they do so, as long as the Unravelling Essence is soulheld, the Artisan will remain alive, but the moment it ceases being soulheld, the Artisan will take the Death Final effect.

advanced.name: Spark of Doom
advanced.description: Carefully, the Artisan breaks down a Forgesource, altering it to expend its charges directly into Materia. The Artisan must work on the Forgesource for a Focus Quick 100 to complete the alterations. Until the Artisan completes a Short Rest, they can touch a Packet to the Forgesource to charge it with Lightning Power. Charging takes a Focus Quick Count of 30, and each Packet does Wounding 2 by Lightning. This damage cannot exceed a maximum of 3 from any other source. Only one Packet can be charged at a time. The same Forgesource may charge multiple packets in this way, but after the power expires the Artisan should deposit the Forgesource in the Ashbin when possible. Note: This Forgesource cannot be substituted using the Mechanical Augmentation power.

advanced.name: Spiritual Defense
advanced.description: The Artisan’s very spirit itself has been strengthened through their dabbling in Mystical forces. When targeted with a Power or attack that has the “to Living”, “to Substantial”, or “to [Artisan’s Devotion]” qualifiers the Mystic may call “Counter, Spiritual Defense” and negate the attack. If the Mystic believes they know the name of the creature that attacked the Mystic, the Mystic may also gain five Barrier when they use this Power. The Mystic being correct about the target’s name is inconsequential for this Power, only that the Mystic has a good faith belief that the name is correct.

advanced.name: Strange Token
advanced.description: Through prayers to a Divine Being, energy manipulation, or binding of minor spirits, the Mystic energizes up to six objects with power that may be gifted to others. When bestowed to another, the Mystic explains the token’s healing properties, and how they are used. The bound powers are non-transferrable, and cannot be regifted. Such power comes with a price, and the life given will take something in return, causing the recipient to be drained until they complete a Short Rest. The recipients are granted the Curious Balm Power below:

advanced.name: Curious Balm
advanced.duration: Until Used (Long Rest), Instantaneous, Short Rest
advanced.description: By using the small token that they were given by the Artisan, the character gains the ability to Heal herself for 1 point before the next time they complete a Long Rest. This healing takes a Quick Count of 100, but can be done while the character is dying. After the healing is complete, the character is drained until they complete a Short Rest.

advanced.name: Studied Focus
advanced.description: Instead of taking an Advanced Power, the Artisan can choose two Basic-tier Powers from the Artisan Base Class with the same Specialty Tag (ie: Artificer, Crafter, Mystic).

advanced.name: Uninterrupted Creativity
advanced.call: “[Name of Description] Short Obey by Mind: Do not interrupt my crafting.”
advanced.description: While touching a Crafting Station of any kind (Tinker’s Workshop, Enchanting Forge, or Hermetic Lab) and actively crafting, the Artisan can make others leave them alone to work. They can use this Power an unlimited number of times (but only once per individual), and doing so will not interrupt any Crafting they are doing. The Artisan may only target the same target once per application of this power.

advanced.name: Zenith of Knowledge
advanced.call: “Short Obey by Mind, Tell me one of your most important secrets that I do not know.”
advanced.description: After completing a Ritual where the Artisan Mystic is the Primary Ritualist, the Artisan may ask one of the following questions of the Marshal guiding the ritual, which the Marshal should attempt to answer honestly: • “What is the effect of the next card in the deck (either higher or lower)?” • “What downsides to this ritual being cast may I not have considered that you can think of?” • “Provide me a potentially helpful cryptic phrase, vision, or short story.” Or instead, the Ritualist may target one of the ritual’s Participants with a verbal Obey Effect: • Short Obey by Mind, “Tell me one of your most important secrets that I do not know.” • Long Obey by Mind, “Tell no one of this Ritual or what took place here.”

options.text: “What is the effect of the next card in the deck (either higher or lower)?”
“What downsides to this ritual being cast may I not have considered that you can think of?”
“Provide me a potentially helpful cryptic phrase, vision, or short story.” Or instead, the Ritualist may target one of the ritual’s Participants with a verbal Obey Effect:
Short Obey by Mind, “Tell me one of your most important secrets that I do not know.”
Long Obey by Mind, “Tell no one of this Ritual or what took place here.”

classSkills.name: Studied Process
classSkills.description: The Artisan has found shortcuts and efficiencies in Plans after many hours spent honing and perfecting their craft. Choose one Craft: Alchemy, Enchanting, or Tinkering. The Artisan may reduce all mentions of “turns of the hourglass” by half, and single turns of the hourglass are reduced to a Quick Count of 30. All other repeated actions are reduced by half (rounded up). This power may be purchased once for each Craft. Note: This Power stacks with other time reduction skills for crafting, however, the reductions from this Power are calculated first.

classSkills.name: Utilitarian
classSkills.description: The Artisan learns an additional Utility Power from the Artisan Class Powers.

classSkills.name: Batch Process
classSkills.description: Masters of their trade, Artisans craft some Plans so often that the patterns have become rote muscle memory, making it trivial to build several copies in parallel. Choose one Craft: Alchemy, Ritual Magic, Enchanting, or Tinkering. The Artisan may call “Batch Process” at the beginning of their crafting attempt to double the number of outputs from the crafting plan. This requires that the Artisan expend double the appropriate resources for the plan they are crafting. The Forgesource plan cannot be affected with this skill. Note: Crafting processes requiring a Dark Territory card pull must be pulled twice from the same hand. Resolve the first card before the second is pulled.

classSkills.name: Quality Control
classSkills.description: The Artisan may oversee their own or an ally’s crafting recipe once per event. The crafting resources are refunded if the process fails so long as the failure condition does not specify otherwise.

classSkills.name: It’s Alive!
classSkills.description: Once per Long Rest, an Artificer may sacrifice an Ingot to call “Revive by Lightning” to one individual.

name: Cleric
startingSkills: The Path of the Initiate: Basic Faith (4), Worship (1), Basic Martial Weapons (1)
One of the Cloth: Basic Armor (2)
The Means of Service - Choose one of the following:
Short Weapons (3)
Extended Capacity - Novice (3)
Basic Medicine (2), Diagnose (1)
Divine Study - Choose one of the following:
Extended Capacity- Novice x2 (6)
Additional Cantrip (6)
Bookcaster (1) x 3, Peacecaster (3)
Basic Shields (4), Advanced Shields (2)
multiclassSkills: A Vow to the Divine: Basic Faith (4), Worship (1)

2.bonus: Innate Bonus Cantrip: Cancel, Healing Touch

innate.name: Refreshing Prayer
innate.description: The Cleric can pray to their Divine Being or Devotion to rebuild focus and refresh their Divine Spell-slots. The Cleric can spend a Short Rest studying their spellbook, meditating, or speaking to their higher powers. The Cleric can perform this special study and refresh all of their expended Divine spell-slots. If the Cleric has used any other Innate Power from another class to refresh their Divine spell slots this long rest they may not use Refreshing Prayer. Additional benefits come if this prayer is done in an area of sacred faith (such as consecrated ground), especially an area sacred to the Cleric’s Devotion. Doing so, the Cleric gains three additional Divine Novice-tier Spell-Slots. These Spell-Slots last until the Cleric completes a Long Rest. Additional benefits may be possible (see the Skills, Perks, and Flaws document for more details).

innate.name: Healing Touch
innate.incantation: I call my faith, divine and right, to knit your wounds with healing light.
innate.description: The cleric gains a pool of healing that can be used between Long Rests called the Healing Touch Pool. This Healing Touch pool is equal to three times the Cleric’s class-level. Each time this power is used, the Cleric can heal another Individual up to the Cleric’s class-level, subtracting the healing from the Healing Touch Pool. Healing Touch will not work on the caster themself. For Example: a 4th level Cleric would have 12 total points of Healing to give out between Long Rests and could use this Power to Heal up to 4 points at a time. There is no limit to the number of points that can be in this pool; however, the pool resets to three times the Cleric’s class-level at the end of a Long Rest. Note: While this Power is an Innate Power, for purposes of Advanced Peacecaster, it counts as a Cantrip. Bonus healing provided by Advanced Peacecaster is not subtracted from the Cleric’s Healing Touch pool, however a minimum of 1 point must always be subtracted.

innate.name: Ritual Affinity
innate.description: The Cleric can use their understanding of Divine magic to help navigate the complexities of Ritual magic. They receive the Apprentice Ritual Magic skill at no cost. Once they achieve 7th level Cleric, they can purchase Journeyman Ritual Magic skill for one fewer Build Point. Once they achieve 12th level Cleric, they can purchase Greater Ritual Magic skill for one fewer Build Point. If they have already purchased either of these skills, they get one Build Point back for each.

innate.name: Shield of Faith
innate.description: The Cleric's faith protects them from those that seek to do them harm, forming a magical aegis around a shield the Cleric wields in battle. Once per Short Rest, the Cleric may call “Counter, Shield of Faith” when their shield is hit by an attack with Weapon or Packet delivery, Preventing the attack. Attacks that do not strike the Cleric's shield may not be countered in this way.

innate.name: Holy Armor
innate.description: The Cleric's Divine Being lends their power to enhance their defenses, increasing the Maximum Summoned and Physical Armor of the Cleric by 1.

innate.name: Shepherd the Faithful
innate.call: “Refresh [Power] by Divine”, “Refresh all Novice and Adept Spell Slots to Self by Divine”
innate.description: Once per Long Rest, after having an extended conversation with another member of their Devotion, the Cleric may Refresh one Power of the target with a Refresh of Short or Long Rest (Note: this cannot be used on Powers that themselves have the Refresh effect). Any individual may be the recipient of Shepherd the Faithful only once per Event. Additionally, once per Event, the Cleric may call upon the power of their Divine Being to refresh all of their Novice and Adept Divine spell slots. Alternatively, they may entreat a creature of the Exalted type for a boon. Simple prayer generally is not enough to ask for such a boon through this power, and the entity must be present with the Cleric: often the Beckon Ritual is used for this purpose. What is granted is up to the entity’s discretion and may vary greatly, as are the consequences of being found unworthy or the presumption of the request. Asking powerful entities for boons is not without risk, especially entities known to be fickle or exacting. (Note: asking a Player Character with the Exalted type for a boon may not have any particular mechanical benefit.)

classSkills.name: Path of Panic
classSkills.description: The Cleric can expend 3 points from their Healing Touch Pool to immediately call “Heal 1 to Dying”.  Note, this effect has no incant whatsoever.

classSkills.name: Mantle of Faith
classSkills.description: When taking a Short Rest, the Cleric may spend a quick moment reciting prayers or a creed specific to their Devotion. In doing so, they may grant protection against those who would use the nature of their Divine Being’s power against them.When completing a Short Rest, they may “Grant Protect vs [Devotion Accent]” to an ally and themselves.

classSkills.name: Cantrip Scholar
classSkills.description: The Cleric learns an additional Cantrip from the Cleric Class Powers or Cantrips.

classSkills.name: Expand Focus
classSkills.description: The Cleric may expend a Spike to alter the “to Undead” Qualifier and Accent on a Spell they cast to “to Exalted by Divine.” The Spell must come from the Cleric Base Class Spell List.

classSkills.name: Expansive Worship
classSkills.description: The Cleric may take abilities within a third divine domain associated with their Devotion.

classSkills.name: Greater Healing Touch
classSkills.description: The depth of the Cleric’s devotion to their cause deepens, and with it, new boons are born from the mastery of novice skills. The Cleric now gains one additional point to their healing pool per cleric level for the Healing Touch Archetype Power.

classSkills.name: Divine Substitution
classSkills.description: The Cleric has gained enough mastery over the interpretation of their doctrine that they can begin offering less… common interpretations of their Divine Domain. They may choose a single domain not within their Devotion’s standard domains, and take it as if their Devotion had access to this domain. The Cleric may not take a divine domain that is in direct opposition to their Devotion’s standard domains. See the Divine Domains document for more information. Opposed Domains: Chaos Order Destruction Creation Manipulation Expression Death Life Light Shadow Peace War

cantrips.name: Blade Blessing
cantrips.description: This Protect can be used to stop any hit that has no Call. This is always going to be an attack with no Effects and no Accent that would do 1 point of damage.

cantrips.name: Cancel
cantrips.description: This  cantrip takes the power out of a Spell-Packet (or multiple Spell-Packets) that has already been charged and returns that power to the caster. The Spell-Packet becomes inert, and the caster refreshes the Spell-Slot that was used to charge the Spell-Packet. If the Spell charges more than one Spell-Packet, all the Spell-Packets must still be charged in order to cancel the Spell.

cantrips.name: Curing Prayer
cantrips.description: This spell can only be cast out of combat. The Cleric may Cure any Condition other than tainted, obedient, resistance, and immunity. Note: During particular marshaled encounters, this spell may not be available, as the entire encounter may be considered “In-combat.”

cantrips.name: Detect Devotion
cantrips.call: “[Name or Description], Subtle Discern by Divine: Are you a follower of [Devotion]?”
cantrips.description: This divination spell will let the Cleric know if a particular creature is a follower of a specific Devotion or Divine Being, depending on how the spell is cast. The spell may not always result in information, due to the frenetic nature of combat. This spell may be cast upon a living, unconscious, spirit, or dead creature.

cantrips.name: Devotion’s Strike
cantrips.description: This Cantrip can be cast in two different ways.  Either the caster may charge a single Weapon delivered attack for “2 [Devotion Accent]”, or can Imbue another target with the power to do a single Weapon attack that is Accented with the Cleric’s Devotion Accent. The Imbue call may not be altered.

cantrips.name: Divine Bolt
cantrips.description: This minor cantrip charges a Spell-Packet with a bolt of energy tied to their Devotion’s domains, which will do 1 point of damage to those that it hits.

cantrips.name: Revivify
cantrips.description: The Cleric can Heal 1 Life Point to a Dying target. The Call may not be altered.

cantrips.name: Sense Supernatural
cantrips.call: “[Name or Description], Subtle Discern by Divine: Are you a/an [Exalted/Undead/Voidborn]?”
cantrips.description: This divination cantrip will let the Cleric know if a particular creature is Undead or Exalted. Only one of the options can be detected by any single casting of the spell. The spell may not always result in information, due to the frenetic nature of combat. Each question (Undead/Exalted) may only be asked once of any given target.

cantrips.name: Shield of Devotion
cantrips.description: The Cleric throws up a shield of divine energy to protect themself. The caster must choose their Devotion’s Accent at the time of casting.

noviceSpells.name: Armor of Faith
noviceSpells.call: “Long Grant 2 Summoned Armor by Divine” or “Long Grant +1 Physical Armor by Divine”
noviceSpells.effect: Grant Summoned Armor, Grant Physical Maximum Armor
noviceSpells.description: The caster grants themself 2 Summoned Armor Points. This is considered a new “suit” of armor and is not stackable with other sources of Summoned Armor. If the Cleric is wearing Physical Armor, they may instead grant themself +1 Armor, increasing their Maximum Armor Points.

noviceSpells.name: Augury
noviceSpells.call: “Instruction: Tell me if it is a good idea or bad idea to [suggested immediate action] by answering ‘boon’, ‘bane’ or ‘unknown’.”
noviceSpells.description: The spell lets the Cleric determine whether an action they are about to take will be, on the whole, harmful or beneficial, to them and their goals. They might, for example, ask if it is boon or bane to walk down the apparently empty hallway in front of them. Or they might ask if it is boon or bane to open the chest they are holding. If there is no good answer, the answer is unknown, or there are bad assumptions in the question (“Boon or bane to trust the emissary of The Steed?” when there is not an emissary of The Steed present) will all give “Unknown” as an answer. If the answer is Unknown, the Spell-Slot used to cast the spell is Refreshed.

noviceSpells.name: Benediction
noviceSpells.description: This spell can be cast on a group of people who are within Touch range of the caster and can only be cast out of combat. The Cleric can either Heal each target for 2 Life Points, or Mend the armor of each target for 2 points. The same option must be chosen for all targets.

noviceSpells.name: Burn
noviceSpells.description: The Cleric calls down holy flame to cause another’s equipment to burn. Any hand that is holding an item will cause that equipment to be disarmed. The caster should only call out arms that they can tell are holding items. If the caster cannot tell whether the hand is empty or not, that arm shouldn’t be called. Enhancement: Expend a Spike: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.

noviceSpells.name: Cure
noviceSpells.description: This spell charges three Spell-Packets with the ability to Cure any Condition, however, each may cure a different condition: they need not be the same condition. Casting this spell will add two points to the Cleric’s Healing Touch pool if they have one, or three if they have Peacecaster.

noviceSpells.name: Disable Undead
noviceSpells.description: With the power of their faith, the Cleric charges two Spell-Packets that can destroy the arms of an undead foe. Enhancement: Expend a Spike: Three Spell-Packets are charged, and the Cleric may target an undead’s legs, in addition to the arms.

noviceSpells.name: Faith’s Lash
noviceSpells.description: Calling on their Devotion, this spell allows the caster to add or substitute the Divine Accent on Weapon attacks.

noviceSpells.name: Fortress of Faith
noviceSpells.call: “Long Imprison to Self by Force”, “Cure Imprisoned”
noviceSpells.description: After the incantation is successfully completed, the caster gains the power to cross their hands in front of their chest once and call “Long Imprison to Self by Force.” to lock themself into a prison of power, ideally protecting themselves from harm. At any time after imprisoning themself, the Cleric can call “Cure Imprisoned” to drop the Fortress. The ability to put up the Fortress will only last until the completion of the next Long Rest once the spell is cast.

noviceSpells.name: Heal
noviceSpells.description: The caster heals the target of the spell for 3 Life Points. This spell also adds 2 points to the Cleric’s Healing Touch pool or 3 if they have Peacecaster. Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: Heals for 10.Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: Heals for 20.

noviceSpells.name: Heretics’ Brand
noviceSpells.description: Tapping into the depths of their Devotion,  the caster pulls forth a spark of power to charge two Spell-Packets to harm others, especially followers of other Devotions. The caster may add the Qualifier “to [Devotion]” and make the Call “Wounding 3 [to Devotion]”.

noviceSpells.name: Intercession
noviceSpells.call: “Grant 4 Healing Touch Pool to Cleric By Divine”
noviceSpells.description: With this Spell, the caster can Grant 4 points to a Cleric’s Healing Touch Pool if they have one. Peacecaster increases all these numbers by one. Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: It instead adds 9 points. Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: It instead adds 15 points

noviceSpells.name: Blade Guard
noviceSpells.call: “Grant Protect vs Weapons By Divine”, “Grant 3 Barrier by Divine”
noviceSpells.description: This Spell grants a single Protection from Weapons. The Cleric also gains 3 points of Barrier themselves on casting this spell.

noviceSpells.name: Prayer of Rest
noviceSpells.description: This spell can only be cast out of combat on a willing target and the Cleric cannot cast it on themself, nor can they cast it on someone they believe already has the Holy Rest Power. Once the Incantation and explanation are complete, the recipient is granted the Holy Rest Power below:

noviceSpells.name: Holy Rest
noviceSpells.description: After the character has been Dying for at least a Slow Count of 120, they can Heal themself for 1 Life Point even if unconscious or Dying. Note: This Power can be used at any time after the user has been Dying for at least 120 seconds. The character will have the Power until it is used.

noviceSpells.name: Rekindle
noviceSpells.call: “Revive to 5th level or Lower by Divine” or “Revive to [Devotion] by Divine”
noviceSpells.description: This spell will return the target to life with 1 Life Point if they are 5th level or lower. If the spell fails because the target is not fifth level or lower, the spell immediately Refreshes. Alternatively, the Spell can be used to bring a character back to life, with 1 Life Point, as long as they share the same Devotion as the Cleric. Note: The Cleric can only cast the version of this spell that returns an individual that shares their Devotion once per Long Rest.

noviceSpells.name: Spirit Speak
noviceSpells.call: “Short Root to Spirit by Mind”, “[Name or Description] Short Obey to Spirit by Mind: Converse with me for at least one minute. You are not required to answer questions.”, “[Name or Description] Cure Root to Spirit”
noviceSpells.description: The Cleric can require a Spirit to stop and talk with them. The Spirit is not required to answer questions. When the Cleric is done with their conversation, they must call “[Name or Description] Cure Root to Spirit” to end the spell and release the Spirit. Until the Spirit is released, the Cleric cannot cast additional spells.

adeptSpells.name: Bless Shield
adeptSpells.description: Bless Shield gives one shield Wielded by the target of the Spell resistance to Wounding Effects. The wielder of the shield should call “Resist” when the shield is hit with such an Effect, and should take one point of damage. This resistance lasts only until the wielder completes a Short Rest.

adeptSpells.name: Blessing
adeptSpells.call: “Short Grant +1 Maximum Spike by Divine”, “Grant 3 Barrier by Divine”
adeptSpells.target: Self and up to six Individuals
adeptSpells.description: This spell can be cast on the caster and up to six people who are within Touch range of the caster and can only be cast out of combat. This spell can be cast one of two ways: offensive or defensive. An offensive Bless will grant each beneficiary an additional Maximum Spike. A defensive Bless will grant each target three points of Barrier.

adeptSpells.name: Cleanse
adeptSpells.description: The Cleric ends all Conditions on the character, including those that might be beneficial to the target. Casting this spell will add three points to the Cleric’s Healing Touch pool if they have one. If the caster has the Peacecaster skill, instead the caster will add four points into their Healing Touch pool if they have one.

adeptSpells.name: Disrupt
adeptSpells.call: “By My Voice 3 to Undead by Divine”
adeptSpells.description: A powerful pulse of energy comes from the Cleric, washing over all nearby Undead. Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The Called damage is 5 points.

adeptSpells.name: Divine Armor
adeptSpells.call: “Long Grant 4 Summoned Armor by Divine”, “Grant Protect vs Melee” “Long Grant +2 Maximum Physical Armor by Divine”
adeptSpells.effect: Grant Summoned Armor, Grant Maximum Physical Armor, Grant Protect
adeptSpells.description: The caster grants themself 4 Summoned Armor Points. This is considered a new “suit” or armor and is not stackable with other sources of Summoned Armor. If instead of granting Summoned Armor the Cleric is wearing Physical Armor, this Spell instead grants the Cleric +2 Maximum Armor.Either way, this spell also grants a single Protect vs Melee Weapons to the Cleric. The recipient should call out “Protect” to Counter a melee weapon attack.

adeptSpells.name: Flame of Faith
adeptSpells.description: There are few spells as good for destroying the undead as Flame of Faith: by summoning righteous fire, the Cleric causes 10 points of damage to Undead. Enhancement: Spend 3 Healing Touch & a Spike: Charge an additional Spell-Packet.

adeptSpells.name: Gift of Life
adeptSpells.description: This powerful spell washes the target in life-energy. If the target is a corpse, it will bring it back to the living with 1 Life Point. This spell can only be used to bring one corpse back from the dead once per Long Rest. However, If a Power refreshes a Long Rest ability, it can target the “once per Long Rest” limitation of this spell, allowing one more individual to be brought back to life by this spell. If Gift of Life is used on an Undead, it will cause the creature to immediately discorporate and take Spirit Form.

adeptSpells.name: Judgment
adeptSpells.call: “Quick Imprison by Mind”, “[Name or Description] Heal 2 to Imprisoned by Divine”, “[Name or Description] 2 to Imprisoned by Divine”
adeptSpells.description: Wrapping their target in iron bands of willpower, the Cleric captures a creature and locks it away. While the creature is Imprisoned, the Cleric may charge harming or healing powers to use on that creature. After focusing for a Quick 30, the Cleric may either do “[Name or Description] 2 to Imprisoned by Divine” as a verbal, or may do “[Name or Description] Heal 2 to Imprisoned by Divine” as a verbal. This may be done as often as desired until the target ceases to be Imprisoned by this spell. Note: If the caster has the Peacecaster skill, instead the Call becomes “[Name or Description] Heal 3 to Imprisoned by Divine”, and if the caster has the Warcaster skill, instead the Call becomes “[Name or Description] 3 to Imprisoned by Divine.” This is an exception to normal Peacecasting / Warcasting rules.

adeptSpells.name: Minor Circle
adeptSpells.description: This spell must be cast while inside a white cord, rope, or the like that is no longer than 10 feet long that has been laid down to make a continuous shape (usually a circle). At the completion of the Incantation, the lain rope cannot be purposefully moved by anyone other than the caster, and anyone who attempts to do so should be informed by use of “Instruction: You may not move the rope.” or similar. This does not prevent people from crossing the threshold of the cord, rope, or the like. Should the rope be moved purposefully by the caster, the spell ends. This should be communicated to others trying to touch or manipulate it. As long as the caster has both feet completely inside the circle, they have immunity to all Effects and Damage. If the caster uses a Power on anyone who has both feet entirely outside the Circle, causes damage to another creature, or if the caster steps out of the Circle, the immunity granted by the Circle is removed. In addition, they must pick up the circle and end the Spell. The Circle can be Dispelled by Dispel Magic and similar magic. Note: The caster may only have one Circle active at any given time, regardless of origin.

adeptSpells.name: Punish
adeptSpells.description: Tapping into the depths of their Devotion, the caster punishes those of other Devotions. The caster may add the Qualifier “to [Devotion]” and make the Call “Wounding 8 [to Devotion]”, instead, but the Cleric may not include their own Devotion as the Qualifier. Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The damage becomes “12 Divine” and “Wounding 12 to [Devotion] by Divine” instead.

adeptSpells.name: Retributive Protection
adeptSpells.call: “Counter, Retributive Protection”, “[Name or Description] Piercing 2 by Divine”
adeptSpells.description: This spell allows the Cleric to protect themself from damage, giving two “Retributive Protection” Counters, which Prevent two damaging attacks of any kind. In order to prime the retributive part of this spell, the Cleric must point at a creature and say “I call my faith to warn you against harming me.” Only the last creature that they warned will be a valid target for retribution. When the Cleric uses a Counter defense from this spell to stop damage from the last warned creature, they may call out a Verbal Effect against that attacker: “[Name or Description], Piercing 2 by Divine.” This can be done once for each Counter defense that is used. This spell ends early if the Cleric causes harm to another with Damage or Effects other than the Damage from this spell.

adeptSpells.name: Safe Return
adeptSpells.description: After completing the incantation, the caster will become Insubstantial. They can then walk directly to a nearby area (within 10 minutes walk) which they have previously been to, that is consecrated or holy to their Devotion. When they arrive and enter they lose their insubstantial Condition and should call “Cure Insubstantial.” If they are unable to enter the area (if it is proofed against insubstantial creatures, for example) and have no other viable options, they must immediately call the “Cure Insubstantial” and end the spell, leaving them outside the area.

adeptSpells.name: Salvation
adeptSpells.call: “By My Voice, Heal 1 to Dying by Divine”
adeptSpells.description: This spell will save all those who might be dying, bringing them to 1 Life Point.

adeptSpells.name: Speak With The Dead
adeptSpells.call: “[Name or Description] Discern to Corpse by Divine: [Yes or No Question]”
adeptSpells.description: Out of combat, after completing the incantation while within Touch range of a corpse, the Cleric can cause the corpse to temporarily animate and truthfully answer questions posed by the Cleric. The Cleric may ask up to three questions. The questions posed must be yes or no questions and the call for each question is “Discern to Corpse by Divine: [Yes/No question]”. Once a corpse has been asked all of the allotted questions, the Cleric may not cast this spell on that same corpse again. The spell will end immediately if the Cleric leaves Touch range of the corpse, speaks for any reason other than to ask more Speak With Dead questions of the corpse, or enters combat. Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The Cleric may ask up to five questions..

adeptSpells.name: Spell-Warding
adeptSpells.description: When cast the spell allows the Cleric to charge two Spell-Packets with the ability to Protect individuals from attacks from Packets.

name: Druid
startingSkills: Natural Survival: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Profession - Apprentice (1), and either Forage I (3) or Scavenge I (3)
The Song of the Land: Basic Faith (4)
Budding Wisdom - Choose one of the following:
Extended Capacity - Novice (3), Lore: Nature (2)
Short Weapons (3), Two-Weapon Style (2)
Peacecaster (3), Basic Medicine (2)
multiclassSkills: The Voice of the Forest: Basic Faith (4)

2.bonus: Innate Bonus Cantrip: Cancel, Nature’s Understanding

innate.name: Commune with Nature
innate.description: The Druid can spend a Short Rest in meditation, feeling the strength of nature all around them, and drawing from its power and majesty. The Druid can perform this special meditation and refresh all of their expended Druid spell-slots. If the Druid has used any other Innate Power from another class to refresh their Divine spell slots this long rest they may not use Commune with Nature. Additional benefits come if this meditation is done in an area of special natural power, sometimes called a Natural Locus. Doing so, the Druid gains three additional Divine Novice-tier Spell-Slots. These Spell-Slots last until the Druid completes a Long Rest. Additional benefits may be possible (see the Skills, Perks, and Flaws document for more details).

innate.name: Nature’s Understanding
innate.call: “[Name or Description] Discern [to Animal/Beast] [Question] by Mind”
innate.description: This power allows the character to determine if a targeted creature is a beast of nature, and its intent. The power lasts until the Druid moves their right foot or takes a Short Rest, whichever comes first. Until it ends, the Druid can ask any of the following questions as often as desired of any number of targets that they can see, but may only ask each target each question once. • Do you have the Animal Type? • What kind of Animal are you? • Why are you here? • What are you feeling? • Do you mean me harm? • What is one of your Vulnerabilities?

innate.name: Message of the Flowers
innate.description: The Druid may leave a note locked with a Black Key marker and Has Foraging Skill on it at any Bloom or Night Prize Patch.

innate.name: Earth’s Vitality
innate.description: The Druid gains Long Grant +1 Maximum Life Points after spending at least 5 minutes communing with Nature outside. They may do this while taking a nature walk, meditating in their Grove, or other such activities.

innate.name: Natural Cycle
innate.description: Once per Event, after the Druid believes they have killed a creature (including and most often through a successful Deathblow) they remove the restriction on their ability to Revive.  This restriction to return someone to life in a particular Long Rest is common among spells that have the Revive effect. This power will only lift the restriction once, and one it has does so, will not do so again. If the Druid knows the target was not killed this power fails to refresh their ability to Revive another, however, the power is not wasted. Note: This power does not refresh the Spell-Slot used to Revive, it just removes the restriction the spell or power might have against doing so.

innate.name: Grove
innate.incantation: RP: Sanctification Rite of Druid’s Creation
innate.description: The Druid can create a special area that is their grove. To do this, the Druid should do a small sanctification rite of their choosing, role-playing creating a connection with the grove for at least a minute. The grove will last until the beginning of the next Event (even if the Druid is not in attendance). The area must either be marked off with a border of green material (rope, engineering tape, etc.) not to exceed 100 ft long, or be an obvious area (such as an actual clearing). The area may not have any permanent structures in it but may overlap with Druid groves that already exist, as long as the other Grove’s owner consents. The area should have some natural significance or beauty; just putting it on the lawn in front of the Tavern may cause Staff to claim the Grove is defunct. The Druid must provide an easily visible item card for the grove, indicating that it is a special place, its effects, the name of the Druid who created it, and any others presently attuned to it, which must be posted somewhere easily visible in the center of the grove itself. The grove has a number of properties: • The plants and vegetation there grow particularly well, as if perfectly watered with plenty of sunlight. • Plants and small animals within the grove are cheered, and generally feel safe and happy. • Plants and small animals within the grove are unusually hardy and difficult to be blighted, diseased, or harmed by outside magic. • The area is considered a Natural Locus, and should be conspicuously noted as such on the card. • Once ever, when the Druid discorporates, they may Resurrect with 1 Life Point at their Grove instead of moving to the Spirit River. • While within the Grove, the Druid may deliver the Cure effect at-will via Touch. If this is used to cure the Taint condition, this ability is suppressed until the druid takes a Short Rest.

classSkills.name: Red in Tooth and Claw
classSkills.description: Early in every Druid’s life comes the time in which they must defend themselves, whether from the natural denizens of forest, or the “civilized” of the cities. Many learn that always having a means of defense at a moment’s notice can mean the difference between life and death. The Druid may manifest up to two Short Claws as Natural weapons and can use them proficiently. These claws should be no longer than 36” long.

classSkills.name: Favored Form
classSkills.description: The character chooses one of the three Forms (Hulking Bear, Hunting Panther, Striking Serpent) and can cast one Form Spell that grants that Form without expending a Spell-Slot.

classSkills.name: Find the Path
classSkills.incantation: “By dawning growth that blooms in Spring I ask the forest for a path forward.”
classSkills.description: Upon using this power, the character may ask the trees what they have seen, and follow specific paths. They can see and follow green flags marked with a white circle (called a “Halo”) until they have finished a Short Rest. This Power must be cast while within Touch range of a tree of any size, but its benefits may be used anywhere. If the Druid later purchases the Tracking Skill, they may refund this skill.

classSkills.name: Hunter
classSkills.description: The Druid gains the Projectile Weapon Skill, but does not gain proficiency with crossbows. Anything that lists Projectile Weapon Skill as a prerequisite works normally.

classSkills.name: Wyvern’s Tooth and Claw
classSkills.requirement: Druid Level 3, Red in Tooth and Claw
classSkills.description: The Druid can manifest up to two Natural Long Weapons or one Natural Great Weapon proficiently.

classSkills.name: Cantrip Scholar
classSkills.description: The Druid learns an additional Cantrip from the Druid Class Powers or Cantrips.

classSkills.name: Flexible Form
classSkills.description: Once per Long Rest, the Druid can cast one Form Spell from their Favored Form without expending a Spell-Slot and without the “out-of-combat” restriction. This is often used to pop out of the Form, cast other Spells, and then dive right back in.

cantrips.name: Antitoxin
cantrips.description: The Druid throws calls on nature to protect themselves from poisons. This gives the caster one Protect from Poison.

cantrips.name: Cancel
cantrips.description: This minor cantrip takes the power out of a Spell-Packet (or multiple Spell-Packets) that has already been charged and returns that power to the caster. The Spell-Packet becomes inert, and the caster refreshes the spell-slot that was used to charge the Spell-Packet. If the spell charges more than one Spell-Packet, all the Spell-Packets must still be charged in order to cancel the spell.

cantrips.name: Care for the Fallen
cantrips.description: A simple trick taught by Druids of the past to prevent the transition to death, the caster can Call “Heal 1 to Dying.” This Call cannot be altered.

cantrips.name: Enchanted Thorn
cantrips.description: The Druid is able to create a wooden thorn that can be thrown as a Thrown Weapon. The caster can use any Powers or other abilities (like Spikes) on the thorn as if it were a mundane Thrown Weapon. When the Spell-Packet strikes, however, it will act as a Spell-Packet, not a Weapon and will be defended against appropriately.

cantrips.name: Festering Lash
cantrips.description: By calling the toxic wrath of nature the Druid can lash out, disarming foes that seek to harm them. Unlike other Effects, if no duration is called, the duration of Disarm is Instantaneous, and the item may be picked up immediately upon hitting the ground.

cantrips.name: Hide
cantrips.description: As long as the Druid has no points of Barrier, they can grant themself one point of Barrier. Enhancement: No Form: Grants 2 Barrier instead of one.

cantrips.name: Life Tap
cantrips.description: The Druid can heal damage to an Individual by drawing life from nearby flora, represented by their Life Tap pool. This Cantrip works normally if the Druid is outside. If the character is inside, they lose double points from their Life Tap pool for the same effect (to heal for 1 they would spend 2 Life Tap Points) unless they are touching a Plant creature (Including a character under the effects of Arboreal Transformation). The Life Tap pool is a number representing how much the Druid can heal for that Long Rest. Every time this Cantrip is used, the Druid can heal another individual up the Druid's Class-Level, subtracting the healing done from the Life Tap Pool. The Life Tap Pool resets to twice the Druid's Class-Level at the end of a Long Rest.

cantrips.name: Nature’s Rebuke
cantrips.description: The Druid is able to release a small bit of contagion or poison to harm their foes. Enhancement: No Form: Charges 2 additional Spell-Packets.

cantrips.name: Nettles and Barbs
cantrips.description: The Druid can make one attack with a Claw weapon that does “2 Poison”. If they expend a Spike while doing so, the call is altered to “Wounding 2 by Poison.”

cantrips.name: Natural Remedies
cantrips.description: From the deep lore of the woods, Druids have found ways to cure any number of poisons, toxins, and venoms with ease. Out of combat, the Druid may incant, and then call “Cure [Poison, Disease, or Taint].” Enhancement: No Form: Can be cast in combat with a Quick 30 refresh.

noviceSpells.name: Lesser Form of the Hulking Bear
noviceSpells.call: “Grant Lesser Form of the Hulking Bear to Self”
noviceSpells.description: This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the Bear Spirit. Powerful and capable of taking enormous punishment, the Bear can ride any front line. While in the Lesser Form of the Hulking Bear, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind or use physical or summoned armor, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Hide Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits: • +1 Maximum Life Points. • 4 points of Natural Armor. • May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently. The Bear has the following additional abilities: • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Counter a damaging Weapon attack. The Call is “Counter, Bearhide!”. • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Quick Provoke [No Accent/by Force]” • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Heal themself for 3 Life Points. The Call is “Heal 3”.

noviceSpells.name: Lesser Form of the Striking Serpent
noviceSpells.call: “Grant Lesser Form of the Striking Serpent to Self”
noviceSpells.description: This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the venomous snake, giving them speed and poisonous power. The serpent is a predator, striking hard and cruel. While in the Lesser Form of the Striking Serpent, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Nettles and Barbs Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits: • +1 Base Spike Damage. • May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently. • May add the “Poison” Accent to any Claw Weapon attack that does not have an Accent.
