The Serpent has the following additional abilities: • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Wounding 3 by Poison” • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Verbal attack that does “[Name or Description] Quick Slow by Fear” • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Disable [Left/Right Arm] by Poison”

noviceSpells.name: Lesser Form of the Hunting Panther
noviceSpells.call: “Grant Lesser Form of the Hunting Panther to Self”
noviceSpells.description: This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the panther, the perfect predator. The panther is darkness and shadow, slipping in for the kill. While in the Lesser Form of the Hunting Panther, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Enchanted Thorn Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits: • +1 Base Spike Damage. • May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently. • May Counter the Damage and Effects caused by a fall by Sacrificing 2 Life Points and Calling “Counter, Catfall”. The Panther has the following additional abilities: • Expend a Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack from behind that does 5 per Tier expended. • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Short Slow by Agony” • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Root”

noviceSpells.name: Arboreal Transformation
noviceSpells.description: While this spell is active, and the Druid has their feet planted, the Druid gains the Plant Type and may count for any tree size requirements for spells for the caster and other character’s requirements. Enhancement: No Form: The Druid may move as if Slowed without ending this spell in combat, or move normally outside of combat.

noviceSpells.name: Balm
noviceSpells.description: The Druid heals the target of the spell for 3 Life Points or Cures the target of one Condition. Enhancement: No Form: The Druid may heal and Cure the target.

noviceSpells.name: Barkskin
noviceSpells.description: Pulling the protection of the forest around them, the Druid hardens their skin with bark, giving them two points of Natural Armor.

noviceSpells.name: Blessing of the Oak
noviceSpells.description: The Druid may bestow the blessings of the mighty oak to bolster their allies' fortitude granting a Protect against Poison and Disease. Additionally, the caster gains 3 points to their Life Tap pool if the Druid has not been under the effects of a Form spell this event. Enhancement: Spike: The Druid may expend a Spike to add one additional Target.

noviceSpells.name: Bounty of the Land
noviceSpells.description: The Druid knows how to take from the land without ruining its natural beauty or despoiling its resources permanently. In return for this treatment, the land is kinder to the Druid. Until the Druid's next Long Rest, the next time the Druid would suffer negative consequences of Gathering, the character may Counter those consequences for themself. The character can Counter one negative effect of Scavenging a creature, Prospecting, or Foraging as long as it happens before they complete a Long Rest. The appropriate Call for preventing this negative effect is “Counter, Land's Bounty.” Enhancement: No Form: In addition to the above, once during the duration of the Spell, the Druid may put back a card and re-draw from the deck when Prospecting.

noviceSpells.name: Corrosive Bolt
noviceSpells.description: Casting this spell, the Druid infuses materia with the destructive power of Acid. Enhancement: No Form: The Druid gains 1 additional charge.

noviceSpells.name: Ensnare
noviceSpells.description: When this spell is cast, the Druid calls the icy tendrils of the earth to reach up and grab a hold of their enemy, charging two Spell-Packets with “Short Root by Ice.” Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: The Delivery becomes Verbal and the Call is “[Name or Description] Short Root by Ice”.

noviceSpells.name: Entangling Vines
noviceSpells.call: “[Name or Description] Quick Disable [Left/Right Leg] by Poison”
noviceSpells.description: Venomous thorns reach up from the underbrush, or through the cracks in the stones, striking at the legs and feet of the Druid’s enemies to disable their foe’s leg.

noviceSpells.name: Eyes of the Crow
noviceSpells.description: With this Spell up, Instead of searching a target, the Druid can quickly size up a helpless or willing creature and determine where to search. Please note that, like searching, this should not include any actual touching without express pre-negotiated consent. The caster can search instantaneously, by calling “Instruction: Give me your loot”, rather than searching for the Quick 100. Although this can be done any number of times while the Spell is active, the caster must wait until they either have the Loot, or are told there is none, before it can be used again.

noviceSpells.name: Frostbite
noviceSpells.description: The Druid is able to call forth the freezing cold of winter to hinder their enemy. After casting they may throw three Spell-Packets for “Short Slow by Ice.”

noviceSpells.name: Nature’s Embrace
noviceSpells.description: The Druid is able to cloak themselves from those that wish to find them. By calling for nature’s aid this spell will protect them from the Expose effect. While touching a tree, laying in grass, or cloaked in darkness (such as night time or deep shadow), the Druid is Obfuscated vs Expose.

noviceSpells.name: Purifying Flame
noviceSpells.description: When casting this spell, the Druid infuses their touch with the burning purity of the sun. They may call “Cure [Condition] and 1 by Flame”. The Druid may do this up to three times to the same Individual consecutively, each one Curing an additional Condition, but causing additional damage. Enhancement: No Form: The 3 charges no longer have to target the same individual.

noviceSpells.name: Quell Beast
noviceSpells.call: “[Name or Description] Short Charm to Animal by Mind”
noviceSpells.description: The Druid is at home with the creatures of the forest, and is able to soothe even the most dangerous of natural beasts. They may make a verbal call to a creature known to be an Animal and call “[Name or Description] Short Charm to Animal by Mind.” Enhancement: Expend a Spike: The Call can be changed to “[Name or Description] Short Sleep to Animal by Mind.”

noviceSpells.name: Sap of Life
noviceSpells.call: “[Name or Description] 1 Final”, “Grant [X] Life Tap Pool”
noviceSpells.target: Individuals (up to eight and the caster)
noviceSpells.description: This Spell can be cast on up to eight other individuals (and the Druid), but must be cast out-of-combat. The Druid must explain that each person in the casting will lose one Life Point if they consent. Once the Incantation is complete, the Druid asks each target (other than themself) in turn whether they are willing to give up life for this Spell. This can be “Are you willing to sacrifice your blood for the good of the wilds?” or “May I have a sip of you?” or any similar question. As each one says they consent, the Druid will say “[Name or Description] 1 Final”. For each Life Point given up, the Druid adds 1 point to their Life Tap Pool. If they Sacrifice a Life Point themself, they add 2 more. This can add up to 10 points back into the Druid’s Life Tap Pool.

noviceSpells.name: Shape Metal
noviceSpells.description: The Druid can give another the power to shape metal, molding armor over itself to repair rents and tears. The Druid must sacrifice an Ingot during the casting of the Spell, infusing it into the target. The target will be able to do a Mend 4 once by Touch.

noviceSpells.name: Submerge
noviceSpells.description: This spell charges a Spell-Packet to curse the victim with lungs full of freezing water causing “Short Silence by Ice.”

adeptSpells.name: Form of the Hulking Bear
adeptSpells.call: “Grant Form of the Hulking Bear to Self”
adeptSpells.description: This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the Bear Spirit. Powerful and capable of taking enormous punishment, the Bear can ride any front line. While in the Form of the Hulking Bear, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind or use physical or summoned armor, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Hide Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits: • +3 Life Points • 5 points of Natural Armor • May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short or Long weapons proficiently. • May add the “Force” Accent to any Claw Weapon attack that does not have an Accent. The Bear has the following additional abilities: • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Counter a damaging Weapon attack. The Call is “Counter: Bearhide!”. • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Heal themself for 3 Life Points. The Call is “Heal 3”. • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Quick Provoke [No Accent/by Force]” • Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to Grant themself +1 Bonus Spike Damage until they leave the Form. • Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to Cure themself of one Condition. The Call for this is Cure [Condition] and the Bear can do this while unable to speak, but not while unable to use Powers.

adeptSpells.name: Form of the Striking Serpent
adeptSpells.call: “Grant Form of the Striking Serpent to Self”
adeptSpells.description: This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the venomous snake, giving them speed and poisonous power. The serpent is a predator, striking hard and cruel. While in the Form of the Striking Serpent, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Nettles and Barbs Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits: • +1 Base Spike Damage. • +2 Base Maximum Spikes. • May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently. • May add the Serpent Accents to any Claw Weapon attack that does not have an Accent. • Serpent Accents: [Poison, Fear] The Serpent has the following additional abilities: • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Wounding 4 by [Serpent Accent].” • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Verbal attack that does “Quick Slow by [Serpent Accent].” • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Disable [Arm] by [Serpent Accent].” • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to Refresh one Spike by Calling “Refresh Spike.” • Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Taint by [Serpent Accent].” • Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Quick Drain by [Serpent Accent].”

adeptSpells.name: Form of the Hunting Panther
adeptSpells.call: “Grant Form of the Hunting Panther to Self”
adeptSpells.description: This Spell can only be cast while out of combat. The Druid is inhabited by the essence of the panther, the perfect predator. The panther is darkness and shadow, slipping in for the kill. While in the Form of the Hunting Panther, the character cannot cast Spells of any kind, but can activate the Powers of items and can cast the Enchanted Thorn Cantrip, but no others. The Druid has the following benefits: • +2 Base Spike Damage. • May manifest and Wield up to two Natural Short weapons proficiently. • May Counter the Damage and Effects caused by a fall by sacrificing 1 Life Point and Calling “Counter, Catfall.” • Gain the benefits of the Adept Druid Spell Eyes of the Crow. • May do a Weapon attack at-will that does “Death to Helpless.” • May add the Panther Accents to any Claw Weapon attack Accent or substitute another existing accent on a Claw Weapon attack. • Panther Accents: [Disease, Agony] The Panther has the following additional abilities: • Expend a Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack from behind that does 5 per Tier expended. • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Slow by [Panther Accent]” • Expend a Novice Spell-Slot to make a Weapon attack that does “Quick Root” • Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to do a Weapon attack that does “Silence by [Panther Accent]” • Expend an Adept Spell-Slot to Counter a Weapon or Packet attack by Calling “Counter, Dodge”

adeptSpells.name: Curse the Despoiler
adeptSpells.call: “Long Obey by Fear: Do not [Prospect/Forage/Scavenge] without my permission.”
adeptSpells.description: Woe unto the enemy of the Druid who takes from the land in a way unsustainable. The Druid may charge a Spell-Packet with a powerful curse that prevents the individual from gathering from the land and using either their Prospecting, Foraging, or Scavenging skill until the next Long Rest. During the Call, the Druid may prevent the other from using any or all of the Gathering Skills.

adeptSpells.name: Dart Frog’s Skin
adeptSpells.call: “Counter, Poison Skin” and “[Name or Description] 4 Poison”
adeptSpells.description: The Druid gains three Counters against melee attacks. Each time the Counter is used, the Druid may call a Verbal “4 Poison” against the attacker. The skin is delicate, however, and will slough away and prematurely end the Spell if the caster throws Materia or makes a Weapon attack.

adeptSpells.name: Feral Strength
adeptSpells.description: The Druid is able to call upon the feral nature of the wild beasts of the wood. Upon casting this spell the Druid will be able to pick up and carry an individual in each hand while running even while holding Natural Weapons (See pickup and carry rules). In addition, while the Spell is active, the Druid is considered to have proficiency with Great Weapons and all Shields.

adeptSpells.name: Health of the World
adeptSpells.description: The Druid may cast this spell to add 5 points to their Life Tap pool or the Life Tap pool of another Druid.Enhancement: No Form: The Druid may choose to add 10 points to their Life Tap pool or the Life Tap pool of another Druid.

adeptSpells.name: Putrid Blade
adeptSpells.description: The Druid can summon the powers of rot and decay, infusing them into an individual, tainting them, stopping healing from being effective and possibly killing the victim of the attack.

adeptSpells.name: Phoenix Reborn
adeptSpells.description: Upon casting this spell the Druid must Sacrifice 10 Life Points (or however many they currently have if it is less). This powerful spell washes the target in flame and magical life. If the target is a corpse, it will bring it back to the living with 1 Life Point. This spell can only be used to bring one corpse back from the dead once per Long Rest. If a power refreshes a Long Rest ability, it can target the “one per Long Rest” limitation of this spell, allowing one more individual to be brought back to life by this spell. If used on an Undead, it will cause the creature to immediately discorporate and take Spirit Form. Enhancement: No Form: The Druid may deliver “Heal to Full by Flame” to the target affected by Revive

adeptSpells.name: Hide of the Rhino
adeptSpells.description: The Druid is able to tap into some of the deeper energies of the forest to become more bestial in nature. This spell will give the Druid three points of Natural Armor.

adeptSpells.name: Lifethrive
adeptSpells.description: The Druid can cause life all around to infuse an individual, healing them even at a distance. If the Druid is standing in their Grove when the Spell is cast, they are also Granted 4 points into their Life Tap pool.

adeptSpells.name: Nature’s Blessing
adeptSpells.description: This spell augments an ally, increasing the life essence within, increasing their Maximum Life Points by one. If the Druid has not been under the effects of a Form spell this event, they also add 4 points to their Life Tap pool.

adeptSpells.name: Spitting Venom
adeptSpells.description: This Spell infuses two Spell-Packets with highly potent poison: anyone struck will become weakened.

adeptSpells.name: Spirit Strike
adeptSpells.description: The Druid can use this spell to give the power to strike through the veil and harm the incorporeal. The imbued target can inflict one attack that does 6 damage to Insubstantial creatures. Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The spell can, instead, do “Short Imbue Weapon with [Cure Insubstantial to Insubstantial]”.

adeptSpells.name: Thorns of Ruin
adeptSpells.description: Vines with deadly thorns wrap around and extend from the Druid’s hands, ready to infuse materia with deadly power. While this spell is active, the Druid can expend a Spike and Sacrifice a Life Point to call “Thorn of Ruin!” to charge a Spell-Packet with “Wounding 4.”

adeptSpells.name: Sprout Wings
adeptSpells.description: The character sprouts wings and gains the ability to take flight for a short amount of time. The call to take flight is “Grant Flying,” at which point the character gains the flying condition. After taking flight, the character must begin moving at a minimum of a walking pace, taking up to 50 steps, at which point they must land and end the flying condition. If the character ever stops moving, voluntarily or involuntarily, while in flight, they also must end the flying condition. After landing, the character may not take flight again using this ability until they perform a Focus Quick 100. The character may not take any other actions, besides making Defense Calls, while under the flying condition.

adeptSpells.name: Weasel’s Agility
adeptSpells.description: The Druid is able to tap into some of the deeper energies of the forest to become more bestial in nature. This Spell will give the Druid the ability to resist the snares and traps that would slow or pin them down. The Druid may spend a Spike to Counter any Root or Slow Effects.

name: Fighter
startingSkills: The Most Basic Training: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Basic Shields (4)
The Second Skin: Basic Armor (2), Light Armor (3)
The Path of Combat - Choose one of the following:
Projectile Weapons (3), Lore: Historical (2)
Short Weapons (3), Advanced Shields (2) or Two Weapon Style (2)
Great Weapons (5)
multiclassSkills: Combat for the Uninitiated: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Short Weapons (3)

10.bonus: Warrior Spirit *+1 Armor point as long as the Fighter is wearing at least 1 point of Physical Armor

innate.name: Deadly Remnants
innate.description: The Fighter can use shattered weapons to block and do 1 point of damage. Using a shattered weapon will never do more than 1 point, regardless of other modifiers and enhancements. When a Shatter is taken to a wielded weapon that the Fighter will continue to use with this Power, they should call “Altered, Deadly Remnant,” to convey that the Shatter was taken, and altered. In addition, when picking up a shield from the ground that the character does not know the status of, they can treat the shield as if it were intact unless they are informed otherwise.

innate.name: Parry Blow
innate.description: While wielding a weapon or shield, a character may use Parry Blow to Counter any weapon delivered attack, by calling “Counter, Parry.”

innate.name: Fighting Instructor
innate.incantation: RP: Slow 300 Instructing a character in how to use the weapon.
innate.description: After spending at least 5 uninterrupted minutes instructing another character in how to fight using specific weapons or styles, the Instructor may grant the target a temporary Skill. Regardless of the Instructor's own skills, they may grant the following Skills to the target until their next Long Rest: Basic Martial Weapons, Short Weapons, Great Weapons, Projectile Weapons, Thrown Weapons, Basic Shields, Advanced Shields, or Two Weapon Style. If the Instructor has proficiency with Long Weapons or Great Shields, they may teach one of those instead.

innate.name: Mountain of Steel
innate.description: The Fighter may Sacrifice 5 Points of Physical Armor (not Barrier, Summoned Armor, or Natural armors) to Refresh the Parry Blow Power instantly. The character should call “Refresh, Parry Blow” when they use this power. This ability may be used a number of times equal to half the character’s Fighter class-levels per Short Rest.

innate.name: Warrior Spirit
innate.description: At the beginning of an Event, and after finishing a Long Rest, the Fighter may choose one of the following bonuses that last until the next Long Rest: • While wielding a Melee Weapon with one hand, you may add the Piercing effect to any Spike attack once per Short Rest and increase the Base Spike Damage of that attack by 3. • While Wielding a Great Weapon in both hands, you may add +1 to your Melee Base Damage. • While Wielding a Projectile Weapon, you may add the Wounding Effect to your normal shots that utilize the Aiming mechanic, these shots also gain +1 Base Damage. • While Wielding a Shield, you gain a Counter vs Shatter after completing a Short Rest. The Call is “Counter, Warrior Spirit.” Additionally, the Fighter gains +1 Maximum Health, physical Armor Point, and Natural Armor Point.

options.text: While wielding a Melee Weapon with one hand, you may add the Piercing effect to any Spike attack once per Short Rest and increase the Base Spike Damage of that attack by 3.
While Wielding a Great Weapon in both hands, you may add +1 to your Melee Base Damage.
While Wielding a Projectile Weapon, you may add the Wounding Effect to your normal shots that utilize the Aiming mechanic, these shots also gain +1 Base Damage.

While Wielding a Shield, you gain a Counter vs Shatter after completing a Short Rest. The Call is “Counter, Warrior Spirit.” Additionally, the Fighter gains +1 Maximum Health, physical Armor Point, and Natural Armor Point.

utility.name: Armored Shell
utility.description: Whenever the Fighter is physically representing and using at least 4 Armor Points of physical armor, they are Granted +1 Armor, increasing their Base Maximum Armor Points.

utility.name: Armored Shell II
utility.description: Once the Fighter reaches 10th level, they gain +1 to their Armor Point total as long as they are wearing at least one point of Physical Armor.

utility.name: Bowyer
utility.description: The Fighter can spend a Focus Quick 100 Rebuilding a projectile weapon (such as a bow or crossbow) that has been Shattered. In addition, out of combat, the Fighter may prepare ahead of time tending to their projectile weapons and ammunition, spending a Focus Quick 100 to give themselves a +1 Bonus Spike Damage on their first Spike used with their projectile weapon.

utility.name: Execute
utility.description: The Fighter gains the Hard to Kill (1) and Carnal Creature (1) Perks. Instead of doing a Death Blow, the character can instead use Execute to instantly kill a Helpless creature.

utility.name: Fortitude
utility.description: Fortitude can be used to Counter any Disable or Disarm Effect. Alternatively, the Fighter can Cure the disabled Condition on themself.

utility.name: Knight’s Strength
utility.description: The Fighter can gain a short burst of strength. This will last long enough to complete one carry, pull one person out of a trap or murk, open one portcullis, etc. If the Fighter picks up an ally while under this effect, they may move at any speed while carrying a body and only needs one hand to start the carry. Alternatively, the Fighter may use this power to gain +1 Bonus Spike Damage on their next spike attack with a weapon.

utility.name: Lessons from Scars
utility.call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: What is your Maximum Life Points?”
utility.description: The Fighter gains Lore: Historical (2), Lore: Noble (2) Perks. As a veteran of many campaigns, the fighter can gauge a rough level of toughness in those around them by sight. Choose one target that you can see, and look them over, then make the appropriate call to determine the target’s Maximum Life Points.

utility.name: Linked Armor
utility.description: The Fighter gains Medium Armor Skill. As long as the character is physically representing and using at least 5 points of physical armor, the character may Grant 1 Barrier to themself at the end of each Short Rest after roleplaying patching their own armor.

utility.name: Reload
utility.description: The Fighter can only use this ability after moving off to a location removed from combat. They then go insubstantial and can return to the battle and collect projectile ammunition or thrown weapons. Once they have collected a maximum of six, they must return to their original location and Cure their insubstantial Condition by calling Cure Insubstantial. While insubstantial the fighter can do nothing besides collect projectile ammunition or thrown weapons and return.

utility.name: Renowned Soldier
utility.description: Having seen the worst that war has to offer, there’s little that can truly shake your will. The Fighter gains Minor Fame (1) and the Apprentice Profession (1) as bonus Skills. The character may choose a Profession suitable to describe their particular niche, but should reflect the actualities of being from a military background, examples include: Soldier, Mercenary, Gladiator, and Bounty Hunter. In addition, the fighter may Counter one Effect with the Fear Accent once per Short Rest.

utility.name: Scales of the Serpent
utility.description: The Fighter gains the Heavy Armor Skill. The flexibility of Heavy armor is often overlooked, but veterans used to wearing these sorts of armor know that each scale is part of a whole, greater than its pieces. As long as the character is physically representing and using at least 7 points of physical armor, and has at least one point of armor remaining, they may, once per Short Rest, choose to Counter one Wounding attack.

utility.name: Shoulder
utility.description: By taking a running start and putting their shoulder into a door, wall, or other immobile object, the Fighter can cause a Shatter Effect against it by force of pure strength. Care should be taken by the player not to damage any actual property. This power has no effect against held objects. If a door is shattered successfully in this way, it should be propped open.

utility.name: Wall of Steel
utility.description: The Fighter gains the Ironclad Armor (2) Skill. After years of living in the heaviest of armors, the Fighter has learned to depend on their armor’s durability, especially in the thickest of battles. Whenever the Fighter is physically representing at least 10 points of armor on both the Upper Torso and Lower Torso locations, once per Short Rest, if the Fighter is using Physical Armor and has at least one Armor Point remaining, they may “Counter” one Piercing attack that they receive.

basic.name: Battlemind
basic.call: “Short Grant +2 Bonus Maximum Spikes by Mind”
basic.description: By spending a Focus Slow Count of 60 concentrating and meditating, the Fighter can put themselves into a mental state ready for combat. Until they take a Short Rest, their Maximum (and current) Spikes increases by two.

basic.name: Disarming Strike
basic.description: While striking an opponent or their equipment with a weapon, the fighter may expend a Spike to call out “Quick Disarm [Item] by Force”.

basic.name: Disengage
basic.call: “Quick Repel by Mind” or “[Name or Description] Quick Repel by Mind”
basic.description: The Fighter can either make a weapon attack and cause the target to stay away from them for a short time or if they are wielding a projectile weapon in hand when the Power is used, can use this Power with a Verbal delivery instead, indicating the target’s name or description to cause them to be repelled.

basic.name: Flaming Arrow
basic.description: While wielding a projectile weapon, the fighter may set their ammunition aflame. For up to two shots with a projectile weapon, the archer may strike a foe and then call “Wounding 5 by Flame”. These shots need not be consecutive, but they expire when the archer takes a Short Rest.If the Fighter has at least 11 levels of Fighter they may instead fire 3 shots instead of 2.

basic.name: Grit
basic.description: The Fighter, through pure dedication, toughness, or sometimes concentrated hatred, can Counter one damaging attack delivered by Packet. Using this ability will leave the Fighter drained for a Quick 100 as they shake off the devastating damage, however, the Fighter may also expend a Spike and Sacrifice one Life Point to Counter the Drain Effect if they choose.

basic.name: Harry
basic.description: The Fighter is adept at keeping the attention of their foes, by word, deed, or well placed blow. The Fighter can deliver “Quick Provoke by Mind” with a single melee weapon attack. If the Fighter expends a Spike this power refreshes immediately.

basic.name: Heroic Save
basic.description: Seeing one of their comrades has fallen in battle, the Fighter heroically dashes in to save them, gaining a temporary surge of heroic inspiration allowing them to shrug off blows and deflect attacks. The fighter may call Grant Insubstantial to Self” and walk towards a downed ally. Once at the ally, they must call Cure Insubstantial to Self, they then gain resistance to Damage and Effects (the appropriate call is Resist, Heroic Save) and must pick up the ally. They may move at any speed while carrying the ally and only need one hand to start the carry. These effects last while carrying the ally, ending when they are dropped, conscious, or dissipate. While carrying the ally, the fighter can't take any other actions but moving to safety and putting the ally down. In no case will any effect from this power persist beyond a Slow 60.

basic.name: Patchwork Defense
basic.description: Once per Long Rest, after having their physical armor repaired with a Patch Kit, the Fighter may refresh Parry Blow.

basic.name: Raging Blows
basic.description: If wielding a great weapon with two hands, the character may Call “Shatter Shield” after doing the Incantation (remember while doing Incantations a foot must be planted). Enhancement: You may expend a spike to ignore the Incantation.

basic.name: Resolve
basic.description: After being affected by an Effect or Damage that would take the character to 0 Life Points or kill the character, the character may call “Altered, Resolve” and instead be reduced to 1 Life Point.

basic.name: Shake It Off I
basic.description: The Fighter can reduce one Effect with a duration of “Quick 100” or “Short” to a Quick Count of 30. When the duration ends, the character must let out a bellow, yell, or cry before taking any other actions. This can reduce the duration of Effects caused by the Fighter’s own Powers, such as Grit.

basic.name: Shot Placement
basic.description: The Fighter can make one melee attack that will do 4 points of damage. If the Character performs the attack with a Great Weapon, the damage is 8 instead.

basic.name: Smite
basic.description: The Fighter can make one melee attack that will do Wounding damage equal to their Base Damage plus two. If the Character is wielding a Great Weapon and no other weapons or shields, the damage is equal to their Base Damage plus three.

basic.name: Snap Shot
basic.description: While wielding a projectile weapon, the Fighter may fire a shot so quick that many people don't see them even draw the string, causing them to pin a foe to the ground with a call of “[Name or description], Quick Root.”

basic.name: Stifle
basic.description: While wielding a projectile weapon, the Fighter may make a projectile attack and call “Short Silence” against an opponent, as the projectile neatly pierces the opponent’s neck. The strike should not actually target the player’s neck.

basic.name: Twin Shot
basic.description: The character can fire two projectiles in succession that are identical. If the archer lands two consecutive shots, they may immediately use the Call from the first shot on the second.

advanced.name: Archer’s Eye
advanced.description: Once activated, while wielding a projectile weapon, the Fighter may choose to aim for a Quick Count of 30, rather than a Quick count of 10, and add the Wounding Effect to their next shot. The Fighter may use this power at-will until they take a Short Rest or leave combat. This Effect cannot alter the calls of other Powers.

advanced.name: Challenging Shout
advanced.call: [Name or Description] Quick Provoke by Mind
advanced.description: The Fighter might not always be able to reach ranged combatants with their blades, but their words can sometimes assault a heart even from far away. After issuing a challenge, insult, or raging bellow, the Fighter can verbally deliver a “Quick Provoke by Mind” to an opponent. If the Provoke is Prevented, the Fighter gains a +1 Bonus to their Spike Damage until they complete a Short Rest.

advanced.name: Expert Parry
advanced.description: Once per Long Rest, the Fighter may refresh Parry Blow.

advanced.name: Hammer Down
advanced.description: Many fighters find that a good solid shield is the key to a sturdy defense. However, it can become a liability once destroyed, leaving the warrior both unbalanced and scrambling. The Fighter may call “Shatter [Item] by Force” once per Short Rest. If the Fighter is wielding a Great Weapon and no other weapons or shields, they may immediately Refresh this power by expending a Spike.

advanced.name: Heartseeker Shot
advanced.description: The Fighter lets loose a deadly shot killing in a single shot powerful enemies. This Power, which only may be used with a projectile weapon deals 15 or may instead deal 10 Wounding.This call may not be modified, though it may be copied with Twin Shot.

advanced.name: Improved Blows
advanced.description: When this power is used, it refreshes two Powers that do the Wounding Effect from the Basic Fighter Powers.

advanced.name: Lock Down
advanced.description: The Fighter can make melee attacks that Root those they hit. As long as they keep their right foot planted, as if rooted, they can swing “Quick Root” at-will.

advanced.name: Rally the Troops
advanced.incantation: RP: About 5 seconds of encouraging an ally
advanced.call: “[Name or Description] Refresh One Spike and Grant 2 Barrier by Mind”
advanced.description: Shouting a few words of encouragement, tactics, or sometimes even a simple look out behind you, the Fighter encourages one of their comrades to greatness. The Fighter must provide instruction and encouragement to an ally for about 5 seconds and should then follow it with the call immediately to provide one individual a Refreshed Spike and 2 Barrier by Mind. The Fighter may not target themselves with this ability. This instruction and encouragement may be tactical in nature, for example: Brick, shift left. Okay, now with me. Good. Now we push around the flank. Nice. would be an acceptable type of RP to engage in as long as it is approximately 5 seconds worth.

advanced.name: Rapid Shot
advanced.description: While wielding a projectile weapon, if the Fighter lands (hits the target or their equipment, even if Prevented) two consecutive damaging shots on the same target, they may expend a Spike to make the second shot Wounding [X] where X is the damage called for the first shot.

advanced.name: Refocus
advanced.description: The Fighter refreshes 3 of their Spikes to no higher than their current Maximum Spikes.

advanced.name: Rest
advanced.call: “Grant Resistance to Death and Deathblows to Self by Mind”, “Heal 1 to Self by Mind”
advanced.description: Once per Long Rest, the character may choose to activate this power while Dying. When activated, the character, while dying, gains resistance to Death Blows and Death Effects. In addition, after the character has been dying for a Slow Count of 120, they may heal themselves for 1 Life Point. The character need not be conscious to use this Power.

advanced.name: Riposte
advanced.description: Whenever the Fighter uses a power that uses the call “Counter, Parry”, they may Refresh an expended Spike.

advanced.name: Shake It Off II
advanced.description: The Fighter can reduce one Effect with a duration of “Quick” or “Short” to a Quick 30. When the duration ends, the character must let out a bellow, yell, or cry before taking any other actions. This can reduce the duration of Effects caused by the Fighter’s own Powers, such as Grit. Shake It Off II can be used while drained, imprisoned, or while affected by any Condition that normally stops a character from using Powers.

advanced.name: Skewer
advanced.description: While wielding a projectile weapon, the fighter may target their opponent’s limbs, striking vital nerve centers and joints. The Fighter may expend a Spike to immediately Refresh this Power.

advanced.name: Stand Ground
advanced.description: Boots firmly digging into the dirt, the Fighter is adept at staying in the fray and may Counter one Repel, Slow, or Root Effect.

advanced.name: Throat Shot
advanced.description: The fighter strikes the target with skill and precision, disrupting their ability to speak. This Silence lasts until the creature Short Rests. The strike should not actually target the player’s throat.

advanced.name: Thick Skin
advanced.description: The Fighter may grant themself a Protect vs. Melee Weapons upon the completion of a Short Rest.

classSkills.name: Reinforce
classSkills.description: Whenever the Fighter's physical armor is repaired by any type of crafted Patch Kit they may Refresh all powers with the [Reinforce] tag. The Fighter can benefit from this ability a number of times equal to their Fighter Class-Levels per Long Rest.

classSkills.name: Accuracy
classSkills.description: Whenever they are using Thrown or Projectile Weapons, the Fighter’s Base Damage increases by 1.

classSkills.name: Field Maintenance
classSkills.description: Knowing how to properly maintain one’s equipment can mean the difference between life and death. The Fighter gains a pool of Maintenance Points equal to 10 plus their Fighter Class-Level that refreshes upon the completion of a Long Rest. They may spend a Focus Quick 100 roleplaying patching their own armor and use any amount of Points to apply them as Mending to their worn armor. When the character uses the Reinforce Skill, they gain two Maintenance Points.

classSkills.name: Healthy
classSkills.description: The Fighter’s health is paramount, and there are those who take it as a matter of personal pride. The Fighter’s Base Maximum Life Points is increased by one. This is a Base change, and does not interact with Stacking rules.

classSkills.name: Utilitarian
classSkills.description: The Fighter learns an additional Utility Power from the Fighter Class Powers.

classSkills.name: Flesh of the Titan
classSkills.description: Whenever the Fighter is Healed by another, they are Healed an additional Life Point. Thus, two points of Healing would heal the Fighter three Life Points.

name: Mage
startingSkills: The Arcane Unveiled: Basic Arcane (4)
Dutiful Scholar: Choose a Lore Skill (2), Library Use (1), Bookcaster (1) x 2
A Magical Specialty - Choose one of the following:
Extended Capacity - Novice (3) x 2
Advanced Recharge (2), Lore: Arcane (2), Bookcaster (1) x 2
Bookcaster (1) x 6
Additional Cantrip (6)
multiclassSkills: Dabbling in the Arcane: Basic Arcane (4)

1.bonus: Arcane Study, Innate Bonus Cantrip: Cancel

innate.name: Arcane Study
innate.description: The Mage can meditate and realign their theoretical constructs to rebuild focus and refresh their Arcane Spell-slots. The Mage can spend a Short Rest studying their spellbook, making notes on spells, or practicing their incants quietly. The Mage can perform this special study and refresh all of their expended Arcane spell-slots. If the Mage has used any other Innate Power from another class to refresh their Arcane spell slots this long rest they may not use Arcane Study. Additional benefits come if this study is done in an area of powerful arcane magic (such as an Arcane Locus), and performs a personal rite (determined by the Mage). Doing so, the Mage gains three additional Arcane Novice-tier Spell-Slots. These Spell-Slots last until the Mage completes a Long Rest. Additional benefits may be possible (see the Skills, Perks, and Flaws document for more details).

innate.name: Replicate Enhancement
innate.description: When casting a non-damaging touch Mage spell, the number of targets is increased by one. The spell must have a target of Individual, shield, or armor, and the listed Delivery must be Touch (Packet Spells cast as Touch do not get this benefit). Once they achieve 7th level Mage they may add one additional target per cast. At 12th, they gain a third additional target.

innate.name: Ritual Affinity
innate.description: The Mage can use their understanding of Arcane magic to help navigate the complexities of Ritual magic. They gain the Apprentice Ritual Magic skill at no cost. Once they achieve 7th level Mage, they can purchase Journeyman Ritual Magic skill for one fewer Build Point. Once they achieve 12th level Mage, they can purchase Greater Ritual Magic skill for one fewer Build Point. If the Mage has already purchased either of these skills, they get one Build Point back for each.

innate.name: Arcane Barrier
innate.incantation: “I conjure words of arcane might, from studied tomes in candlelight to grant you the protection of the Arcane.
innate.description: The Mage can call upon the powers of the Arcane to grant an individual one Barrier by Touch. This Power refreshes whenever the Mage casts a Spell (not Cantrip) from their list of Mage spells or after a Long Rest. Once they achieve 10th level Mage this Power instead grants two Barrier. At 15th level Mage it instead grants three Barrier.

innate.name: Cantrip Mastery
innate.description: All damaging Cantrips cast by the Mage do one additional point of damage.

innate.name: Practiced Sight
innate.description: When the Mage is a Primary Ritualist in a Ritual, when they pull their Final Card from the Dark Territory Hand, they may also pull one additional card from the Hand. When the second card is revealed, the Marshal will give them a premonition of the outcome of the second card. This premonition is given by the attending Marshal as “Weal (Success), Woe (Failure), or Alteration.“ This does not replace the first drawn card, merely suggests what the outcome of the second card might have been.

innate.name: Channel the Arcane
innate.call: “Refresh all Novice and Adept Mage Spell Slots”
innate.description: This Mage power gives them incredible versatility and ability to continue their arcane assistance in battle in exchange for some obvious vulnerability during the channeling process. Once per Event, without access to their book or the normal meditation process Mage may engage in an incredibly obvious and loud magical gathering of essence for about 1 minute and then announce loudly “Beginning to Channel Arcane Energies” and begin a Focus for a Quick 100. At the end of this entire process the Mage instantaneously Refreshes all their Arcane Novice and Adept Spell Slots. If interrupted during this Focus Quick count the power is considered expended so the Mage should do this in an area they are certain they will not be interrupted. Upon attaining the 15th level in Mage this Innate Power also refreshes Arcane Greater spell slots.

classSkills.name: Empowered Mage Bolt
classSkills.description: While before, the nuances of the arcane might have been foreign to the Mage, now, it’s become second nature. The four arcane affinities: Flame, Ice, Lightning, and Acid, are now always close to the Mage’s fingertips. When casting the Mage Bolt Cantrip, the Mage may choose to use the Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning Accent instead of Force.

classSkills.name: Arcane Charge
classSkills.description: When the Replicate Enhancement Innate Power is used, the Mage may choose to reduce the number of targets by one and instead place the additional charge granted into a Spell-Packet. This allows the Mage to retain the charge for later use, or deliver the charge at range.

classSkills.name: Cantrip Scholar
classSkills.description: The Mage learns an additional Cantrip from the Mage Class Powers or Cantrips.

cantrips.name: Cancel
cantrips.description: This minor cantrip takes the power out of a Spell-Packet (or multiple Spell-Packets) that has already been charged and returns that power to the caster. The Spell-Packet becomes inert, and the caster refreshes the Spell-Slot that was used to charge the Spell-Packet. If the Spell charges more than one Spell-Packet, all the Spell-Packets must still be charged in order to cancel the Spell.

cantrips.name: Force Shield
cantrips.description: The Mage throws up a shield of energy to protect themself from Force or Mind.

cantrips.name: Fumble
cantrips.description: A creature stuck by the spell has the named item or item held by the named limb forcibly removed and dropped to the ground. If the specified equipment is a shield (or the limb is wearing a shield) the arm must be straightened so that it is pointing directly at the ground to allow the shield to slide off the arm if possible. Unlike other Effects, if no duration is called, the duration of Disarm is Instantaneous, and the item may be picked up immediately upon hitting the ground.

cantrips.name: Ghost Hand
cantrips.description: The caster must immediately drop everything they are holding and will go insubstantial. They then have a Slow Count of 15 to perform one action and return to the approximate spot where they completed the incant. At the end of the Slow Count of 15 the caster must immediately return to where they were at the start of the spell, and cure their insubstantial Condition. The action must be one of the following: pushing a spot with one finger, picking up a small object (no larger than two fists) and placing it/throwing it, pulling on something with one hand, gesturing to another individual with one hand, or tapping a character on the shoulder (if the character has prior explicit consent only). If this movement triggers a trap, the Mage will be considered to have tripped the trap while momentarily physical, and therefore take the effects of the trap per the normal Insubstantial rules. This cantrip can only be cast out of combat. Note: This spell offers interaction normally unavailable to Insubstantial creatures.

cantrips.name: Identify
cantrips.description: By using this Cantrip on an item while at an Arcane Locus, the Mage may learn all of the magical properties of an item. Although it is easy for anyone to determine if an item is magical or special in nature and read the information card to discover obvious properties, this cantrip will allow the player to look at an item’s folded information card (even if they don’t meet the requirements on the outside) to know its exact properties. Only the caster will be allowed to read the properties, although if they choose to, they can open the item card to make the properties discoverable to anyone. If the card says “Divine Relic” on the public portion, this spell has no effect. Note: Items identified with this spell may still be Obfuscated or otherwise masked, and Identification may not reveal all its properties. Identify may have other specific uses.

cantrips.name: Mage Bolt
cantrips.description: This minor cantrip charges a Spell-Packet with an explosive burst which will do 1 point of Force damage to those it hits.

cantrips.name: See Magic
cantrips.description: See Magic gives the Mage the ability to detect magical emanations from enchantments, active spells (including summoned armor), or magical items. Once cast, the Mage can ask any or all of the following questions of as many targets as desired. The Mage can do this until they cast another spell, says anything that is not one of the questions, takes damage, takes an Effect, or completes a Short Rest: • “What non-Inherent Conditions do you have?” • “How many points of summoned armor do you have?” • “What items are you carrying that are magical?” • “What was the last spell you cast?” • “Are you a Construct?”

options.text: “What non-Inherent Conditions do you have?”
“How many points of summoned armor do you have?”
“What items are you carrying that are magical?”
“What was the last spell you cast?”
“Are you a Construct?”

cantrips.name: Stop
cantrips.description: A creature struck by the spell has their feet bound to the ground with momentary shackles of pure mental power.

noviceSpells.name: Arcane Barrage
noviceSpells.duration: Short Rest or Until Expended, Instantaneous
noviceSpells.description: After this spell is cast, the caster gains the ability to charge a Spell-Packet with power by simply holding it and calling Spell-Packet Charged! Each Spell-Packet charged in this way will do Wounding 1 when it strikes. Only one Spell-Packet can be charged at a time, and Arcane Barrage goes down if the caster completes a Short Rest, casts another Spell, or charges a fifth Spell-Packet using Arcane Barrage. Enhancement: Adept Slot: The caster can charge 10 Spell-Packets with Wounding 2. Enhancement: Greater Slot: The caster can charge 15 Spell-Packets with Wounding 3 and the caster may replace the Accent with the Mind, Acid, Flame, Ice, or Lightning Accent, changing the accent on each attack.

noviceSpells.name: Bolster Shield
noviceSpells.call: “Grant Protect vs. Shatter to Shield by Force” x3
noviceSpells.description: Bolster Shield adds a sheath of magical power around up to three shields, giving it a Protect vs. Shatter.

noviceSpells.name: Carnate
noviceSpells.call: “Cure Insubstantial to Insubstantial by Mind”, “[Name or Description], Piercing 5 to Insubstantial by Mind.”
noviceSpells.description: The Mage can use this spell to shove an incorporeal creature or object fully onto their plane, making it vulnerable to many normal attacks. If the Carnate Spell-Packet is Prevented, one more Spell-Packet can be charged with this same spell by calling “Spell-Packet Charged!”. If the second Carnate Spell-Packet is also Prevented the Mage may call “[Name or Description], Piercing 5 to Insubstantial by Mind.”

noviceSpells.name: Cripple
noviceSpells.description: The target of this spell feels their limb twisted and crushed by an invisible force. An arm will become useless and will be forced to drop whatever that hand is holding. A disabled leg is unable to hold weight. When the Spell-Packet strikes the target, the caster must choose a limb and make the Call. If the Spell-Packet appears to hit a specific limb, or if it hits a weapon or equipment held by a specific limb, that limb must be called, even if it is already disabled. Enhancement: Expend a Spike during Incantation: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.

noviceSpells.name: Flameburst
noviceSpells.description: Fireburst charges a Spell-Packet with a dreadful arcane flame. This Spell-Packet will do “4 Flame”. Enhancement: Expend a Spike during Incantation: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.

noviceSpells.name: Forcebolt
noviceSpells.description: Forcebolt charges a Spell-Packet with a pure magical energy, doing Wounding 4 by Force. Enhancement: Expend all current points of Summoned Armor during Incantation (minimum 1): An additional two Spell-Packets are charged (Total 3).

noviceSpells.name: Freeze
noviceSpells.description: When this spell is cast, it will charge two Spell-Packets at the same time. The Spell-Packets must be thrown separately. When struck by this spell, the target is immediately encased in ice. For the duration, the block of ice will protect them, but will also stop all movement and use of Powers (as normal for imprisoned). Enhancement: Expend a Spike during Incantation: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.

noviceSpells.name: Mage Armor
noviceSpells.call: “Long Grant 3 Summoned Armor to Self by Force”
noviceSpells.description: The caster grants themself 3 Summoned Armor Points. This is considered a new “suit” or armor and is not stackable with other sources of summoned armor. The Mage may instead change the duration of the spell to be Short and grant themselves 4 points of Summoned Armor instead.

noviceSpells.name: Mage Mend
noviceSpells.description: The target of this spell will Mend 5 Armor Points. If the Mage chooses Touch Delivery it may be enhanced by Replicate Enhancement.

noviceSpells.name: Mage Power
noviceSpells.call: “Refresh Basic Short Rest Base Class Martial Power”
noviceSpells.description: The Mage reinvigorates a martial target transforming Arcane energy into strength for the target to power through. The target of this spell immediately refreshes one Basic Base Class Martial Power with a recovery period of Short Rest. The same target may only be affected by any particular Mage’s Mage Power spell once per Long Rest.

noviceSpells.name: Mageskin
noviceSpells.call: “Grant 2 Barrier and Protect vs Weapon by Force”
noviceSpells.description: Mageskin summons two points of Barrier for the individual and one Protect against Weapons for the individual.

noviceSpells.name: Mask Aura
noviceSpells.call: “Short Grant Counter vs Discerns by Mind”
noviceSpells.description: The Mage may put a protective bubble around one individual, making the character difficult to Discern. Whenever the recipient is targeted by a Discern by Mind, they must answer “Counter, Masked”. This protection lasts until the recipient completes or otherwise receives the effect of a Short (or Long) Rest, regardless of the number of Discerns they Counter. Mask Aura can only be cast out of combat in a quiet and generally peaceful area and the effects of the Spell must be explained clearly to the recipient.

noviceSpells.name: Revulsion
noviceSpells.description: The Mage reaches out with their will, putting into others a deep compulsion to stay away. Upon completion of the incantation, two Spell-Packets will be charged with the ability to Repel others. Enhancement: Adept Slot: Four packets are charged instead of two. Enhancement: Greater Slot: The Spell charges a single Spell-Packet, but gives the Mage the ability to continue casting the spell as if it were a cantrip until the next Short Rest. Although multiple Spell-Packets are charged at once, they must be thrown one at a time.

noviceSpells.name: Magekey
noviceSpells.description: Magekey allows the caster to end the imprisoned Condition on one Individual. This can be to save a friend or to end the protection on a foe. This cannot be used while the Mage themself is imprisoned.

noviceSpells.name: Ruinous Curse
noviceSpells.call: “Quick Slow by Mind”, “Quick Weakness by Mind”
noviceSpells.description: Ruinous Curse charges two Spell-Packets simultaneously. One will Slow its target, the other will Weaken its target, each for a Quick Count of 100.

noviceSpells.name: Shred
noviceSpells.description: When this spell is cast, Shred Shatters an item the Mage is holding. If the item is successfully Shattered, and the item was tagged, or otherwise had provable in-game value, like a weapon, shield, or other discarded item on the battlefield, the Mage refreshes one Spike, or gains two Barrier, their choice.

noviceSpells.name: Town Portal
noviceSpells.call: Town Portal, Insubstantial!, “Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Instruction: [Explanation]”, “[Name or Description] Cure Insubstantial to Insubstantial”
noviceSpells.description: This spell must be cast Out Of Combat and the Mage may only have one active Town Portal trigger and sigil at any one time. Upon casting this spell, the Mage inscribes a location, outside, within touch range, with a symbol of their choosing. The Symbol must be at least 6” by 6” and must be visible from at least one angle by a person standing on the ground. At any time until the Mage finishes a Long Rest, they may trigger this power in order to “Grant Insubstantial to Self” and are compelled to walk directly to their inscribed symbol and touch it. The Mage should call Town Portal, Insubstantial! when this occurs. If the Spell is Enhanced, all individuals must all be within Touch range when the Power is triggered. The caster should make it clear to the targets who are being affected and how. The Mage may use Instruction to tell the players that their character is going to be Insubstantial, can only walk, and should Call Town Portal, Insubstantial. In addition, all affected characters must attempt to remain within melee distance of the caster until they have reached the Sigil, or have been cured of Insubstantial. When the travelers reach the sigil's original location and the Mage touches the sigil, the caster calls “[Name or Description] Cure Insubstantial to Insubstantial” on all the travelers. If, instead, the Symbol is no longer there or otherwise defaced, the caster immediately dies. Should the caster die, or the spell ends, all Effects and Conditions related to this spell end. Note: This means the caster is Dead, but no longer Insubstantial, as all Conditions are removed when dead. Removing the Insubstantial Condition will not remove any other part of this Spell's effects: the Mage (and other Targets) will still need to walk back to the original location and will still die if the sigil has been moved or defaced. Enhancement: Adept Slot: Two additional passengers can be taken.Enhancement: Greater Slot: Four additional passengers can be taken.

adeptSpells.name: Adept Mage Power
adeptSpells.call: “Refresh Advanced Short Rest Base Class Power or Novice Spell Slot”
adeptSpells.description: The Mage reinvigorates a target, transforming Arcane energy into strength for the target to power through. The target of this spell immediately refreshes one Advanced Tier (or lower) Base Class Martial Power with a recovery period of Short Rest or one Novice Spell slot of any type. The same target may only be affected by any particular Mage’s Adept Mage Power spell once per Long Rest.

adeptSpells.name: Arcane Bridge
adeptSpells.call: “It Can Be Seen, An Arcane Bridge is Built!”, “It Can Be Seen, The Arcane Bridge Collapses!”
adeptSpells.description: This spell must be cast upon a Spell-Packet that has been marked with a symbol that is special to the caster. The Spell-Packet is then thrown and once it comes to rest, the Mage can briefly touch the ground to create an ethereal, translucent bridge of pure magical power that extends from that spot to the thrown Spell-Packet. The bridge is taxing to maintain, and once six Individuals have passed over it, the magical force will dissipate. The bridge will likewise dissipate if the anchoring Spell-Packet is moved in any way. Severe changes in level between the two ends cause the bridge to dissipate immediately, so this spell cannot be used to help climb or descend more than about four stair-steps up or down. Enhancement: Greater Slot: Ten individuals can cross the bridge before it ends.

adeptSpells.name: Betrayal
adeptSpells.description: The Mage charges two Spell-Packets with the energy of madness and pain. Each charge will cause an individual to strike out at those around it, attempting to harm anyone they can reach. A Berserked creature will attack the nearest creature until it is slept or drops to 0 Life Points. They will not use Powers, and will stop attacking a creature once it falls. If the caster expends a Spike an additional Spell-Packet is charged.

adeptSpells.name: Disintegrate Item
adeptSpells.description: When cast, this spell charges two Spell-Packets that cause the items struck to be Shattered. If the Spell-Packet strikes a shield or weapon held by an individual, then that shield or weapon must be targeted. If the Spell-Packet strikes the individual, the caster can choose from a held item, physical armor, or some other visible possession. The target may be an unattended physical item. Magic items are generally immune and their holder may call out “No Effect” in these cases.

adeptSpells.name: Dispel Magic
adeptSpells.description: This powerful spell can be used to end the duration of many Spells or Powers, including Spells like Elemental Node or protective circles. It can be used in one of the following ways: • Harm Constructs: “Piercing 3 to Construct.” If the caster expends a Spike an additional Spell-Packet is charged. • Dispel any of the following: “Dispel Protects”, “Dispel Summoned Armor”, “Dispel Barrier”, or a specific Condition: “Dispel [Condition].” • Dispel all Conditions caused by a specific Accent: “Dispel [Accent].” • Break any circle represented by a cord, rope, or the like: “Dispel Circle.” Note: Dispel Magic may have other specific uses.

options.text: Harm Constructs: “Piercing 3 to Construct.” If the caster expends a Spike an additional Spell-Packet is charged.
Dispel any of the following: “Dispel Protects”, “Dispel Summoned Armor”, “Dispel Barrier”, or a specific Condition: “Dispel [Condition].”
Dispel all Conditions caused by a specific Accent: “Dispel [Accent].”
Break any circle represented by a cord, rope, or the like: “Dispel Circle.” Note: Dispel Magic may have other specific uses.

adeptSpells.name: Dominate Creature
adeptSpells.description: The target of the spell will feel their attitude change and shift, becoming compliant to the caster’s will. The target is Dominated to the caster for a Short Rest, or until the target is reduced to zero Life Points. This spell is exhausting to the caster, and thus the caster may only have a single target dominated with this spell at any one time, and, in addition, this spell may only be cast once per Long Rest.

adeptSpells.name: Elemental Node
adeptSpells.description: This spell must be cast while inside a white cord, rope, or the like that is no longer than 10 feet long that has been laid down to make a continuous shape (usually a circle). At the completion of the Incantation, the lain rope cannot be purposefully moved by anyone other than the caster, and anyone who attempts to do so should be informed by use of “Instruction: You may not move the rope.” or similar. This does not prevent people from crossing the threshold of the cord, rope, or the like. Should the rope be moved purposefully by the caster, the spell ends. This should be communicated to others trying to touch or manipulate it. As long as the caster is completely inside the circle (regardless of whether they have remained in the circle), they can charge a Spell-Packet with elemental energy by calling “Spell-Packet Charged” Only one Spell-Packet may be charged at a time with this spell. The Spell-Packet will cause “Wounding 3 by [Acid/Flame/Ice/Lightning] Damage. The Mage may charge a number of Spell-Packets with this spell equal to their Mage class-level. The Mage can continue to do this until they complete a Short Rest or until the Circle is moved, broken, or Dispelled. Incidental/accidental movement of the circle will not disturb the spell (such as a foot moving the cord accidentally). The Circle can be Dispelled by Dispel Magic and similar magic. Note: The caster may only have one Circle active at any given time, regardless of origin.

adeptSpells.name: Elemental Survival
adeptSpells.description: Elemental Survival must be cast focusing on one of the Elemental Cornerstones (Fire, Air, Water, Earth). The caster designates 3 targets to gain traits based on the plane chosen. The character cannot be affected by this spell more than once at a time, even if a different plane is chosen. • Air: The character is unaffected by high winds, including natural tornadoes and the like. The character is unaffected by natural lightning strikes and can see normally through clouds and the dust caused by weather. The character has resistance to Lightning and resistance to falling damage a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points. • Fire: The character takes no damage from normal fire or heat, and can breathe and see normally in smoky environments. The character has resistance to Flame a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points. • Earth: The character can pass through some rock and stone (the player can touch stone for a Focus Quick 10 then meld into or out of it, becoming Insubstantial and inherently Slowed while in this state). The character has resistance to Acid a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points. • Water: The character can walk normally on icy surfaces and can breathe normally underwater. The character will suffer no ill effects from being in deep water and can see normally underwater, even in almost total darkness. The character has resistance to Ice a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points. Enhancement: Greater Slot: The Spell’s Duration changes to be Long Rest.

options.text: Fire: The character takes no damage from normal fire or heat, and can breathe and see normally in smoky environments. The character has resistance to Flame a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points.

Earth: The character can pass through some rock and stone (the player can touch stone for a Focus Quick 10 then meld into or out of it, becoming Insubstantial and inherently Slowed while in this state). The character has resistance to Acid a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points.

Water: The character can walk normally on icy surfaces and can breathe normally underwater. The character will suffer no ill effects from being in deep water and can see normally underwater, even in almost total darkness. The character has resistance to Ice a number of times equal to their Maximum Life Points. Enhancement: Greater Slot: The Spell’s Duration changes to be Long Rest.

adeptSpells.name: Gift of Solitude
adeptSpells.description: The caster may identify up to three individuals who are given the Gift of Solitude may, up to three times before completing a Short Rest, verbally repel nearby attackers, giving them room to breathe. The target receives the Alone But Not Lonely Power below:

adeptSpells.name: Alone But Not Lonely
adeptSpells.call: “[Name or Description]: Quick Repel by Force”
adeptSpells.description: Through manipulations of Force, the targets of this power are kept at bay for a short while. “Quick Repel by Force.” This power may be used up to three times before being expended, however, all remaining charges are lost if the wielder of this power completes a Short Rest.

adeptSpells.name: Harden Gear
adeptSpells.call: ”Grant Protect vs Shatter by Force”, “Grant 5 Barrier by Force”
adeptSpells.description: The target individual’s gear gains a Protect against Shatter effects. Additionally, the individual gains 5 Barrier.

adeptSpells.name: Hush
adeptSpells.description: When this spell is cast, Hush charges two Spell-Packets with “Short Silence by Mind.” The target of this spell is rendered unable to make vocal noises for the duration, including casting (but not including out of game calls or responses). Enhancement: If the caster expends a Spike an additional Spell-Packet is charged.

adeptSpells.name: Improved Identification
adeptSpells.description: The Mage may learn all of the magical properties of all designated non Divine Relic items. Although it is easy for anyone to determine if an item is magical or special in nature and read the information card to discover obvious properties, this spell will allow the player to look at the folded information card (even if they don’t meet the requirements on the outside) to know its exact properties. Only the caster will be allowed to read the properties, although if they choose to, they can open each item card to make the properties discoverable to anyone. Note: Items identified with this spell may still be Obfuscated or otherwise masked, and Identification may not reveal all its properties. Improved Identify may have other specific uses.

adeptSpells.name: Mageblast
adeptSpells.description: This powerful bolt does 10 points of Force damage to those it hits.

adeptSpells.name: Rebuild
adeptSpells.description: The Mage can bring back to usefulness an item that has been Shattered or otherwise broken. The majority of the item is required, so an item lost into lava, misplaced, etc., will not be rebuildable.

adeptSpells.name: Spell Absorption
adeptSpells.call: “Grant Protect vs Damage from Packets”, “Wounding [X] by Force”
adeptSpells.description: This spell gives the Mage a limited ability to absorb magical power and use it themself. They gain a Protect against damaging Packets. When the Protect is used, it will also absorb the magic of the attack. The amount of Power that can be used depends on the damage called with the Power. If the Mage does not have a Spell-Packet in hand, the Mage can Refresh one expended Novice Spell-Slot. If the Mage has a Spell-Packet in hand, they can call “Spell-Packet Charged!” and give the Spell-Packet the ability to do the same damage as the original Power, although whatever Accent that Power had will be changed to Force. The call for the charged Spell-Packet is “Wounding [Damage] by Force.”

adeptSpells.name: Succumb
adeptSpells.call: “Short Grant Vulnerability to [Force, Flame, Ice, Lightning, Acid]”
adeptSpells.description: Twisting fate and warping reality, the Mage lays bare their opponent’s weakness for all to exploit. A single Spell-Packet is charged with this spell.

adeptSpells.name: Wrest Control
adeptSpells.call: “[Name or Description] Short Dominate to Construct by Force”
adeptSpells.description: The Mage can intuit the pathways used in the generation of constructs, and attempt to override them, gaining control. The Mage weaves mystic sigils in the air binding the strands of energy animating the golem, verbalizing their intent, and the construct is tethered to their will… for a time.

name: Rogue
startingSkills: To the Point: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Thrown Weapons (3)
Trust is Rare: Basic Armor (2), Light Armor (3)
Up to No Good - Choose One of the Following:
Short Weapons (3), Scavenge (3)
Fence (3), Lore [Shadow] (2), Profession - Apprentice [Specific Profession] (1)
Basic Lock Skill (5) or Basic Trap Skill (5), Poisoner (1)
Connections (2), Lore: Shadow (2), and Contact (2)
multiclassSkills: The Tools of the Trade: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Thrown Weapons (3)

innate.name: Deflect Projectile
innate.description: The Rogue can Counter any thrown weapon or projectile attack made from another character as long as the Rogue catches or deflects with their hands the Thrown Weapon or Projectile before it hits a part of the Rogue that isn’t their hands, ground, or comes to rest.

innate.name: Backstab
innate.description: Whenever the Rogue performs a Spike attack from behind, the attack does 1 additional point of Base Damage. Certain additional levels of Rogue cause the Backstab to do 1 additional point of Base Damage. (+2 points at 5th level, +3 points at 10th level, +4 points at 15th level). This damage is increased by an additional +1 Base Damage if the only weapon Wielded is a Hand weapon.

innate.name: Loot
innate.call: “Instruction to Helpless: Give me your valuables.”
innate.description: Instead of searching a target, the Rogue can quickly size up a helpless or willing creature and determine where to search. Please note that, like searching, this should not include any actual touching without express pre-negotiated consent. The Rogue can search instantaneously, rather than waiting the Quick 100. This ability refreshes when loot is collected or the target indicates they have none.

innate.name: Dodge
innate.description: Dodge is a Counter to any attack made with a Weapon or Packet delivery. Upon being struck with such an attack the player should call “Counter, Dodge” and consider the attack Prevented.

innate.name: Escape Plan
innate.description: The character can escape from a space they would otherwise have difficulty escaping from. Once per Event, they can go insubstantial by calling “Insubstantial to Self” and must then immediately walk slowly out of the space. Once outside the space, they no longer need to walk, and can remain insubstantial for a Slow 60 before calling “Dispel Insubstantial to Self.”

innate.name: Sinister Strike
innate.description: Once per Short Rest when they would be able to perform a Backstab, the Rogue instead performs a Sinister Strike which doubles the damage the attack normally would do. This damage bonus is calculated after the bonus granted by Backstab.  If this attack is Prevented, the Refresh is changed to Focus Quick 30.

utility.name: Acquired Tolerance
utility.description: The Rogue has spent time taking micro-doses of poison in order to build up a mild resistance. Once per Short Rest, the Rogue may Counter one Poison-accented Attack or Effect.

utility.name: Back Alley Deals
utility.description: The Rogue gains the Fence Skill (3). In addition, the Rogue has a number of shady underworld contacts who can provide various favors and services. Once per Event, the Rogue can go to Staff, and ask for one of each the following favors, pay the required cost, and get what they are looking for: Bard - Spread rumor information: 6 Wealth. Barkeep - Get rumor information on one named individual: 10 Wealth. Priest - Heal to full: 4 Wealth. The Rogue may pay this price for each character that comes with them to perform their Back Alley Deals up to 3 additional Characters) Fence - Sell an item at 100% of standard value, minus any duration or charges used: 2 Wealth. Medium - Get a prophetic answer to a question: 8 Wealth. Pusher - Get one dose of an Apprentice level Alchemy preparation: 8 Wealth. Smuggler - Get 3 of any one Basic Resource: 6 Wealth. If the Rogue uses this skill as part of their Pre-Registration, the costs are halved.

utility.name: Catfall
utility.description: The Rogue gains the Agility Character Perk. In addition, the Rogue may Counter the Damage and Effects caused by a fall by Sacrificing 1 Life Point and Calling “Counter, Catfall”. For Example: Avana, climbing across a narrow ledge, slips and falls forty feet to the sharp rocks below, normally taking 10 damage and a Disable to both legs. Instead, they sacrifice one Life Point to call “Counter, Catfall” and negate the effects of the environmental damage from the fall.

utility.name: Contract Killing
utility.description: The Rogue may place a contract on a named individual by going to the Plot Staff and paying a bounty of 20 Wealth. This bounty will be held by Plot Staff, and may be added to by characters and NPCs as outlined below. Until the end of the Event, any character who knows about the bounty may provide proof to the Plot Staff of the death of the target, and collect the bounty. Until the end of the Event, the Rogue gains +1 Base Damage against the Target. They may only have one bonus against a single target at any one time. For this bounty to be active, the Rogue must post the individual’s name, likeness, and description in at least three conspicuous locations, as well as giving instructions for collecting the bounty and the requirement for proof. If members of Staff notice that the posters have been gone for a reasonable amount of time without being replaced, they can decide to cancel the bounty, and refund half the Wealth. If a Rogue has this Power, and finds a Bounty poster, they may add 10 Wealth to the bounty (by going to Plot) to gain the same Bonus without expending their use of this Power.

utility.name: Keen Hearing
utility.description: The Rogue can listen intently for a moment and then pinpoint living creatures around them, even those hidden in the shadows or bushes.

utility.name: Mine
utility.call: “[Name or Description] 8” or “[Name or Description] 12 by Poison”
utility.description: The Rogue can booby trap themself so that they are the only one who can access their equipment and pouches. The Rogue should spend a Slow Count of 120 preparing their pouches, pockets and garb with the trap. They may decide whether to build a mechanical or chemical trap at the time of creation. If the Rogue is searched before the next Long Rest, a mechanical trap will do 8 points of damage, while a chemical trap will do 12 points of Poison damage to the searcher. Either trap should be called as soon as someone starts a search of the Rogue (whether conscious or not).

utility.name: Rogue’s Touch
utility.description: While attempting a mundane lock, the character can call “Rogue’s Touch” if there is some negative effect to failing the lock, or failing to pick it in a certain amount of time. This will not Counter the negative effect, but will allow the character to try again from the beginning. So if the Rogue had to pick a lock within one minute or take a Death Effect, they could take the minute, and as soon as the fact that there was a consequence for failure announced, they could call “Rogue’s Touch” and take another minute. If they do not immediately re-start the attempt or if they totally disengage from the lock, the consequence occurs. At 10th level, the Rogue may do this twice on the same lock.

utility.name: Studded Leather Armor
utility.description: Whenever the Rogue is physically representing and using 4 or fewer Armor Points of physical armor, they gain one additional point to their Base Maximum Armor Points. This bonus becomes +2 when the character reaches 15th Level Rogue.

utility.name: Size Up
utility.call: “[Name or Description], Subtle Discern by Mind: Do you have [X] or more Wealth on you right now?”
utility.description: The Rogue has a practiced eye in finding “big fish” and can, with careful observation, tell a lot about how much wealth a person is carrying. They use various clues and all their senses to tell if someone is carrying more than [X] Wealth with them, where [X] is a number of their choice. They must observe the target carefully for a Focus Quick 100 before making the Call. This skill may only be used once per Short Rest on any given target.

utility.name: Reload
utility.description: The Rogue can only use this ability after moving off to a location removed from combat. They then go insubstantial and can return to the battle and collect their personally owned projectile ammunition, thrown weapons, or Spell-Packets. Once they have collected a maximum of six, they must return to their original location and Cure their insubstantial Condition by calling Cure Insubstantial. While insubstantial the Rogue can do nothing besides collect their personal objects listed above.

utility.name: Throat Slit
utility.description: The Rogue gains the Deathgrip (1) and Holding On (1) Physical Perks. Instead of doing a Deathblow, the character can instead use Throat Slit to instantly kill a Helpless creature.

utility.name: What We Do
utility.description: The Rogue can take either the Basic Lock Skill or the Basic Trap Skill at no cost.

utility.name: Way of the Blade
utility.description: The Rogue gains the Two Weapon Style (2) and Weapon Specialization (4) in one of the following weapons: Swords, Thrown Weapons, or Daggers.

basic.name: Blunt End
basic.description: The Rogue is pretty handy with their Thrown Weapons, and knows just how to handle them to make the flat of the blade strike their opponent. While not damaging, the strike is still effective, weakening their foe for a short time.

basic.name: Concealed Dart
basic.call: “Wounding [Life Point Maximum]” or “Quick Sleep”, or “Short Silence”
basic.description: This Power can only be used with a Thrown weapon and it can only be used out of combat, generally to start a fight. After preparing with a Focus Quick 30, the attack can be used to do one of three effects. It can do a Wounding attack that does damage equal to the Rogue’s Maximum Life Point total, it can do a Quick Sleep Effect, or it can cause a Short Silence Effect.

basic.name: Expert Deflect
basic.description: The Rogue can Counter any thrown weapon or projectile attack as long as the Rogue catches or deflects with their hands the Thrown Weapon or Projectile before it hits a part of the Rogue that isn’t their hands.

basic.name: Evasion
basic.description: While the Rogue has no Maximum Armor and no Barrier, the Rogue may, once per Short Rest, Counter a Weapon or Packet attack that hits them.

basic.name: Flash Attack
basic.description: The Rogue can land a blow slicing the tendons of an enemy in an attempt to weaken the target.

basic.name: Hamstring
