basic.description: The Rogue can land a blow of force and surprising fury, pinning the target so that the Rogue can escape.

basic.name: Improvised Weapon
basic.description: In the right hands, anything is a weapon - a pencil, a cup, or even a coin. The Rogue may use a Spike to “Throw” a non-weapon object as a weapon once to call “[Name or Description] [X],” where X is the Rogue’s current Spike Damage. The player should generally mime throwing an object and be careful not to throw anything that can cause actual harm.

basic.name: Keen Eye
basic.description: The Rogue looks over the chest or container, picking up on any potential subtleties, Focusing for a Quick Count of 100. The Rogue should then go to the Item information card and read the appropriate information, or ask a nearby Marshal if there is no associated information card. The Rogue can use this Power once per Long Rest.

basic.name: Precision Strike
basic.description: While making an attack from behind, the Rogue can expend a Spike to call “Piercing 3,” this call cannot be altered. If this Power is Prevented, this Power immediately Refreshes, but must be used on a different target.

basic.name: Quick Nap
basic.description: Anytime after the character has been Dying or Slept for a Slow Count of 30, they may Heal themself for 1 Life Point (and thus is no longer considered Dying), and the Slept Condition is Cured. The character need not be conscious to use this Power.

basic.name: Reflexive Strike
basic.description: Whenever the Rogue uses a defense that has the call “Dodge”, they may immediately make an attack with the call “Wounding 2.” This attack must be the next attack the Rogue makes, or this Power is wasted.

basic.name: Rogue’s Aim
basic.description: The Rogue may add the Wounding effect to one thrown weapon once per Short Rest.

basic.name: Scarper
basic.description: The Rogue can Cure one Condition that keeps them captive. This can be imprisoned, slowed, or rooted. This can also be used to escape from one set of physical bonds. This includes tied ropes, shackles, the chain attaching the Rogue to the wall, or other bonds, but not enclosures like prisons or cages. This will not free the Rogue from Serious Knots.

basic.name: Scavenge Armor
basic.description: If the Rogue searches or Scavenges a creature and finds a Scavenging Token or sees that the creature is wearing physical armor, they can immediately use parts of the armor or Scavenging Token to “Mend 2” points of their own armor. When the Rogue finds a Token and uses this Power, it expends the Token, and that Token should be taken to the Ashbin when possible.

basic.name: Unburdened Speed
basic.description: The Rogue may Counter one Packet or Weapon attack that strikes them so long as they are not using any type of shield.

basic.name: Uncanny Perception
basic.description: The Rogue can Counter a Weapon or Packet attack that hits them from behind.

basic.name: Waylay
basic.description: This Power may only be used out of combat. The Rogue can expend a Spike to use a Hand Weapon to make an attack from behind which will knock out the target, causing the Sleep Effect. If the Rogue places their weapon against their target (while validly “from behind”) and leaves it there, they can Call the attack at any time, including the initiation and first moments of combat. Note: The weapon should be placed against a valid hit location, not against the throat or head.

advanced.name: Ambush
advanced.description: When attacking from behind, the Rogue can spend the effort to ensure their blow is particularly effective at removing their opponent’s abilities. When the Rogue makes an attack from behind, they may call “Short Drain by Agony.”

advanced.name: Bloodthirst
advanced.description: Whenever the Rogue Deathblows a Helpless creature or uses the Throat Slit Power, and it is not Prevented, they may Refresh one Spike. This Power refreshes after either a Short Rest, or after the Rogue makes a from behind attack against an opponent.

advanced.name: Breach
advanced.description: After a moment’s focus (Focus Quick 30) the character can cause themself to go insubstantial for a Slow Count of 30. During this time, although insubstantial, the character is also slowed. The Power is often used to get past enemy lines, especially through tight, guarded doorways. While the character is insubstantial, they need to be very careful not to push past others, especially given the limited duration of the Power. At the end of the count they must call “Cure Insubstantial.” The Rogue may end the count early by making the “Cure Insubstantial” call at-will.

advanced.name: Elude
advanced.description: Elude may counter any attack that is delivered by a Weapon, Packet, Verbal, or “in this Room.”

advanced.name: Fan of Blades
advanced.description: The Rogue may throw several weapons in rapid succession with exceptional accuracy. The Rogue may make up to three consecutive attacks with Thrown Weapons with the call “Wounding 4”.

advanced.name: Flat Catch
advanced.description: As long as the Rogue has nothing in hand and no shield attached to the arm, they can Counter any melee damage that hits that lower arm (from the elbow to the wrist). They can only Counter attacks that do damage; any weapon-delivered Effect including Wounding and Piercing cannot be Countered with this Power. The Rogue should call “Counter, Flat Catch” when countering an attack with this.A player using Flat Catch must have adequate protection on their arm, such as a bracer, cloth wraps or otherwise to ensure that the player is not injured by repeated blows to the same area over the course of an event. Such padding should be checked in with a Safety Marshal, however, Staff has final say on appropriate protection for this skill.

advanced.name: Gut Shot
advanced.description: The Rogue can throw a weapon into the innards and offal of an opponent, causing it to be weakened.

advanced.name: Hidden Soul
advanced.description: The Rogue may expend a Spike to become Obfuscated vs Discern to a single Discern.

advanced.name: Incapacitate
advanced.description: When attacking from behind, the Rogue may make an attack that will knock out an enemy. If this Power is Prevented, it immediately Refreshes, but must be used on a different target.

advanced.name: Piercing Strike
advanced.description: The Rogue can expend a Spike to do an attack from behind that does Piercing 8.

advanced.name: Precision Throws
advanced.description: Once used, this Power grants the Rogue the ability to, at-will, imbue their Thrown Weapon attacks with the Wounding Effect, however, their damage on that attack is then limited to a maximum of 2. This power will only last until the Rogue receives or completes a Short Rest or until the character becomes Helpless. Note: The Rogue may choose to forgo the Imbue on any given attack, which also removes the damage restriction for that single attack.

advanced.name: Refocus
advanced.description: The Rogue refreshes their Spikes to their current Maximum.

advanced.name: Reversal
advanced.description: Whenever the Rogue uses a defense that has the call “Counter, Dodge”, they may Refresh a Spike.

advanced.name: Slice
advanced.description: The character can do one melee attack that will do Wounding damage equal to their Spike Damage. If this attack is done from behind the Rogue may drop the Wounding Effect to make an attack that does double their Spike Damage.

advanced.name: Toxic Blade
advanced.description: The Rogue may coat their blades, either Thrown or Melee with a poison they’ve made from the local flora. By Sacrificing a Night Prize, the Rogue, while this Power is active, may add or change an attack to use the Poison Accent.

classSkills.name: Serious Knots
classSkills.incantation: RP Slow 60: Tying a set of Bindings
classSkills.call: “[Name or Description] Short Instruction: Serious Knots, You may not get out of these bindings without another creature’s help.”
classSkills.description: The Rogue can tie people up so well that it is almost impossible for them to escape on their own, this may only be done on someone that is Helpless. The bindings can be shackles, ropes, or similar, but the person tied up will not be able to get out without outside aid - remember that the character should be restrained, and not the player.

classSkills.name: Mask of Deception
classSkills.description: If your form is hidden by appropriate clothing, you may circumvent attempts to expose your location. You must be masked and hooded to use this skill, and you cannot have any skin showing except for the eye holes or eye slit in your mask or cowl. While you are dressed this way, you may expend a Spike to gain Obfuscate vs Expose once. As with all Obfuscate Powers, you do not need to announce when you are using this Power.

classSkills.name: Trap Sense
classSkills.description: Any time the Rogue is affected by a trap, they can use this Power to reduce the effects of the trap. If the trap does enough damage that the Rogue would drop to 0 or fewer Life Points, instead they are reduced to one Life Point. If the Rogue takes a Death Effect, they take a Drain Effect and drop to one Life Point instead. Any other Effects and damage are taken normally. A Final trap effect cannot be stopped by Trap Sense.

classSkills.name: Utilitarian
classSkills.description: The Rogue learns an additional Utility Power from the Rogue Class Powers.

classSkills.name: Retreat
classSkills.description: After the character has recovered from a Dying state (not Dead), they may immediately go insubstantial for a Slow 30. They must move towards a location that is outside melee range of any perceived enemies. At the end of thirty seconds, they must Cure their insubstantial Condition by calling Cure Insubstantial. Recall that when insubstantial, the player has a duty to inform anyone that inspects, closely interacts with, or plans to attack their character.

name: Socialite
startingSkills: Forewarned is Forearmed: Basic Martial Weapons (1), Library Use (1), Poisoner (1)
The Presentation: Basic Armor (2)
Old Secrets: Choose a Lore Skill (2)
A Means to an End: Profession: Apprentice & Journeyman [Your Choice] (3)
Opportunities Abound: Choose one of the following:
Fence (3) & Contact (2)
Title (4) & Minor Fame (1)
Short Weapons (3) & Connections (2)
multiclassSkills: Finishing School: Choose a Lore Skill (2), Library Use (1), Poisoner (1)

innate.name: Practiced Manner
innate.description: The Socialite learns an additional Basic Power from the Socialite Class Powers.

innate.name: Heart of the Group
innate.call: “Grant Inherent Type: Member of [Group Name]”, “Counter, Heart of the Group”
innate.description: Socialites tend to find themselves at the center of social cliques. Acting as the cog of a wheel can confer many benefits to its members, including organization, information disbursement, and even mental health. A Socialite may extend a formal invitation after spending 5-10 minutes discussing the group with the potential new member. Invitees must accept of their own free will, and this Power cannot be used if the Socialite knows the invited individual is compelled in any way. The new member gains the Inherent Type: Member of [Group Name] (the Socialite should feel free to name the group). A member may leave at any time by informing the Socialite or by notifying Staff between games. If the Socialite is asked about leaving, or informed that the member was under a compulsion when they accepted, the Socialite must volunteer information about leaving. The Socialite gains a number of Counters equal to their levels in Socialite usable against any attack or Power targeting them that originated from a member of their group. The Socialite must inform Staff when a group is created (before any recruiting happens), disbanded, and when a new member is inducted (immediacy is not necessary). Without Powers or items to create an exception, the Socialite may have up to their Socialite level in members, and may only be the Heart of a single group. A Socialite's bodyguard, and the Socialite themselves, may be included in the group without counting against the total limit.

innate.name: Stern Willed
innate.description: The mind of a Socialite is known for its ability to keep its secrets and push past the lesser wills that would hinder it. The Socialite may call “Counter, Stern Will” to Counter a Berserk, Charm, or Dominate Effect used on them. Once the character has 10 levels of Socialite, they can also Counter the Obey Effect with this Power.

innate.name: Smuggler’s Powdercase
innate.description: The Socialite may designate one pouch (no more than 8” in any one dimension) as their Powdercase. The Powdercase can only contain Wealth, Hide, Harvest, Ore, Rare Minerals, Bloom, and Night Prizes. Once marked with a White Halo Marker, the contents of the pouch are immune to searches and Powers and are excluded when answering Discern questions. The Power does not need to be announced, and can be ignored by the Socialite. The Socialite may only hold a maximum of 200 wealth in this pouch until they reach Socialite level 10 in Socialite and then there is no limit. Any amount of Hide, Harvest, Ingots, Rare Minerals, Bloom, and Night Prizes may be contained in this pouch up to the physical space limits of the pouch. Once the character has 15 levels of Socialite, Mithril Ore, Golden Blossom, and Raw Scales may be held in the Smuggler’s Powdercase as well.

innate.name: Of Sound Mind
innate.description: The Socialite may Counter an Obey effect once per Long Rest. To do so, they must call out the nerve of the offender with a retort of at least ten words immediately after calling the counter.

innate.name: Sharp Dagger, Sharper Tongue
innate.call: “[Name or Description] Quick Provoke by Mind”
innate.description: Social butterflies with bright garish colors make for excellent distractions, especially for the Bodyguard with a sharp, poisoned knife. By using this power, the Socialite may draw the attention, briefly, of an opponent while their Bodyguard sneaks in for the kill. This ability requires a roleplay of at least ten words audibly taunting the target.

innate.name: The Last Toast
innate.description: Across the lands, the Socialite is well versed in appearing hospitable, but always with an escape plan or hidden agenda. Among the most feared and lethal tools at their disposal is the wide array of poisons easily procured in even the most out-of-the-way locations. Beware a proffered drink from a Socialite, as that sickly sweet taste might be your last. All ingested poisons the Socialite places into drinks are modified to add the “Final” tag. Additionally, once per Event, the Socialite may poison a drink with “Heal to Full and Cure all Conditions by Poison Final”

innate.name: Enduring Inspiration
innate.call: “Heal to Full”, “Cure All Conditions”, “Refresh All Spikes”, Explanation
innate.description: After a pep talk of thirty seconds or more, the encouraging words of the Socialite lift the target's spirit, mend their wounds, and cure their ailments. The target receives all the following benefits: • Heal to Full • Cure All Conditions • Refresh Spikes • Refresh one Power with a Refresh of Short or Long Rest (Note: this cannot be used on Powers that themselves have the Refresh effect).

utility.name: Aesthetic Suggestion
utility.description: The Socialite bestows a few words of encouragement and some tips on how to improve the quality during a Crafter’s process. The Crafter (who cannot be the Socialite) is able to improve their Plan with a minor boon from the list below. Each ability can only be used once per craft, and the effect only lasts til the end of the Event. As part of the RP, the exact benefit and its duration must be explained. • Ritual/Enchantment: As a Participant, add or remove one Dark Territory • Weapon:  +1 Bonus Spike Damage to one attack per Short Rest. Ability is lost if the weapon changes hands • Armor/Shield: The wielder may call “Resist Shatter” once • Trap/Damage Dealing Alchemy: The damage of the item(s) is doubled • Healing Item: The healing effect of the crafted item is increased by +3

utility.name: Headliner
utility.description: The Socialite gains the Profession - Master skill. The Socialite has mastered their trade and expanded their business throughout the region. They have connections in many places, who can offer means of transportation and services that may be difficult to find otherwise. This has no mechanical advantage on its own, but should be used when you believe it may benefit a scene. Finally, the caravan often comes with odd stories and tales from the outside world, which they gladly share with the Socialite. The Socialite gains the ability to refresh Bits and Pieces if they have the Perk, once per event.

utility.name: Implicit Truths
utility.description: By book education or more practical means, the Socialite is familiar with the ways of many puzzles. The Socialite gains the Insight Perk. In addition, whenever the Socialite uses Insight, they can ask these two specific additional questions of a nearby Marshal: • “Is there a timer that resets their work, time limit on the puzzle, or other practical timed aspect to the situation?” • “Is an action we are about to take likely to end disastrously?”

utility.name: It’s Who You Know
utility.description: When people are your business, you learn the tell-tale signs of who is dealing in what. In this case, you know someone who specializes in moving goods with questionable sources. The Socialite may exchange Basic or Uncommon Resources with their off-stage business contacts for a small fee. They send up to 15 Basic (Hide/Ingot/Bloom) or 7 Uncommon (Rare Minerals/Harvest/Night Prizes) and 2 Wealth (their fee) and get an equal number of the same rank (Basic/Uncommon) in a denomination of the Socialite’s choice. Players pass the Resources and Wealth to Staff with a note detailing their return request. Staff will make the trades return to the Socialite at their convenience. The Resources and Wealth will be refunded and the ability refreshed if Staff cannot make the trade for logistical or plot reasons.

utility.name: Never Stop Learning
utility.call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern: What in-game Skill are you demonstrating and how does it work?”
utility.description: After engaging another in interaction for a minute or two, the Socialite can use a Discern to determine if the other character is demonstrating an in-game Core Skill.  A Core Skill is a Skill from the “Skills, Perks, and Flaws” document, therefore Class Skills may not be duplicated. This might be showing the Socialite how to use a Long weapon, or might be expounding on a particular type of Lore. If, after the interaction, and the Discern, the target gives the name of a Skill, the Socialite can “have” that Skill until they complete a Long Rest. This may require further questions on the part of the Socialite to determine how the Skill actually works mechanically and these can be asked as Discerns or the Socialite may give an “Instruction: Tell me how that Skill works.” The Skill is gained at whatever Rank is explained by the target. If the answer that comes back from the Discern is not an in-game Skill (the name need not match perfectly), no Skill is gained, but this Power can be Refreshed by completing a Short Rest. The Socialite may need to direct the mind of the target to thinking of the Skill in question in order to most effectively use this Power.

utility.name: Pit Master
utility.description: The Socialite gains 4 Wealth per Event at Logistics check-in. Additionally, the Socialite can serve as an intermediary between two combatants who wish to beat each other to a pulp. The Socialite declares the fight and ensures everyone knows the rules. Once the two duelists accept the duel, the Pit Master has a few Instructions to give. This need not be verbatim: “Instruction to [Names of Duelists]: You will be unable to use Powers or make attacks on anyone but each other until this duel ends. The duel ends when I declare a winner, when one of you takes damage or an Effect from someone not in this duel, or one of you admit defeat to the other. When the duel ends, you will have all the same Powers, Life Points, and Armor Points you have right now, regardless of what is used during the duel; only expended Resources will remain expended.” The duel is declared by the Socialite with the following example calls at thematically appropriate moments: • “I declare this duel honorable and just!” • “Duelists, are you ready?” • “Fight!” • “Winner!” If the Socialite knows that the duel has ended, they must inform the duelists.

utility.name: Safebox
utility.description: After a few moments of tinkering the Socialite can trigger a container to become a Safebox the next time it is closed. A Safebox’s contents are generally unreachable unless all parties involved in its creation agree that it should be opened. Certain Powers (Item Disjunction, etc) will get past the safeguards. The Safebox’s maximum dimensions are 2’x2’x3’. The Safebox needs to be created with an Item Info Card containing the following: • The Safebox becomes a box if Item Disjunction is used on it. • The Safebox gains Inherent Shatter immunity. • Two people (one of them may be the Socialite) present during its creation must be listed on the card • Add a Black Key marker and write “Only opens once all listed parties touch the box at the same time and say ‘Open.’”

utility.name: Sources
utility.description: The Socialite has access to closed markets before gatherings via business partners and associates. A Sources List containing prices and quantities will be available at Check-In. Basic Resources, otherwise unavailable items, information, or other curiosities and oddities Plot deems fit will rotate through this list.

utility.name: Tax Evasion
utility.description: The Socialite has developed a network of discrete trade associates and maintains an underground network. Their friends in low places have taught them how to avoid paying tribute to those who would cut into their profits. The Socialite receives an additional 3 Wealth for every rank of Profession, and an additional 2 Wealth for Manse and Income.

utility.name: Traveling Merchant
utility.description: With a few favors called the Socialite can add an additional stop to a merchant’s route. Correspondence must be sent in advance to give time for the merchant to adjust their travels accordingly. The Socialite must write the request before the end of an Event to arrange for the stop at the next Event. The letter must contain one request from the following list: General Goods, Ritual Supplies, Tinker Supplies, Alchemical Supplies, Enchanting Supplies, Raw Materials, and Specialty Goods. The merchant will arrive at the following Event and seek out a common place of commerce, offering the Socialite who called them right of first refusal on the first round of sales. If, for whatever reason, the merchant is unable to show, the Socialite may exercise their opportunity to make purchases at Check-In by themselves. Finally, the Traveling Merchant may be commissioned to sell goods on behalf of the Socialite as a consignment sale, negotiated at the time the Merchant arrives in town. The Socialite should be aware that particularly good sales by the merchant might incline them to tip the Socialite, but that’s up to individual roleplay.

basic.name: Calming Tone
basic.call: “[Name or Description] Cure [provoked, berserk, repelled, intoxicated]”, Explanation
basic.description: The Socialite must start by grabbing the target’s attention. Then the Call is made, Curing the Condition, and the Socialite must try to spend another ten seconds or so speaking in a calm, soothing tone, trying to help the target find themself again. Alternatively, out of combat, this Power can be used after talking soothingly to the target for a minute or so (and explaining the effects of the Power), to cure the negative effects of the Nightmares Flaw.

basic.name: Curiosity Killed the Cat
basic.description: The Socialite can booby trap a personal container, but can only have one trapped container at a time. The character should spend at least two minutes roleplaying the process of setting up the device and making the information card. The Socialite decides whether it is a mechanical or chemical trap. The trap is considered active and must be disabled normally if the container is searched before the next Long Rest. The info card should follow all the normal guidelines for trap information cards. The trap does not harm the Socialite if it goes off, and the Socialite can open the box and use it as long as the snap doesn’t go off; this can be used for adjusting and re-setting the trap. A triggered trap will make a snap and deal 10 (Mechanical) or 16 (Chemical) damage to the one who sets it off. Chemical traps will also destroy all flammable objects in the container, and it should say this on the information card. Like the Tinkering Power “Triggered Base Trap”, the trap is represented by a mousetrap, party popper, and/or snaps that should be rigged to make noise if the box is opened.

basic.name: Deducing the Apparent
basic.call: “[Name of Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: [Question]”
basic.description: Keen observations about individuals reveal hidden information to a Socialite. When out of combat and within conversational distance, the Socialite may Subtly Discern a number of the following questions equal to their Class Levels in Socialite per Long Rest: • “Have you killed a living creature in the last hour?” • “Do you think of yourself as a generally honest person?” • “Where did you most recently come from?” • “In which Base Class do you have the most levels?” • “What are your Professions?” • “What Civilization are you from?” • “What Devotion do you follow?”

basic.name: Diplomatic Immunity
basic.description: Upon raising hands while holding nothing but a white flag (optional), the Socialite may call “Short Grant Insubstantial.” They must call “Cure Insubstantial” on themselves upon lowering and/or obscuring their hands or moving their feet. The character cannot benefit from Rests while this Power is active.

basic.name: Don’t You Know Who I Am?
basic.description: Known across social circles, a Socialite’s reputation precedes them… even if sometimes their audience might need a gentle reminder. The Socialite may include a single sentence in the “It has been told…” format when the audience seems to doubt their credentials, status, or station. The sentence must be factually true (ie: from an approved backstory, or a situation that occurred in-game), but may be reasonably embellished for good roleplay.

basic.name: Enthrall
basic.call: “[Name or Description] Short Charm by Mind”
basic.description: Who doesn’t love a bit of flattery? A Socialite’s honeyed words are the stuff of legend. After showering a mark with compliments for a minute the Socialite may call “Short Charm by Mind” to the target of their wiles.

basic.name: Inspiration
basic.description: A group trusts their Heart and can be emboldened by their words. The Heart may speak to each individual in their Group once per Long Rest and give them a special boon. Each individual can only be given the boon once per Long Rest. The Socialite may choose from the following three options: • “Imbue Weapon with Wounding 3, and Refresh one Basic or Advanced Long Rest Power.” (Note: this cannot be used on Powers that themselves have the Refresh effect). • “Imbue Packet with Wounding 5, and Refresh one Novice or Adept Spell-Slot.” • “Grant 3 Barrier” The accompanying roleplay must be a suitably motivating activity such as a rousing speech, back and forth discussion of battle tactics, a sermon, an assurance of purpose, a hug (remember physical consent rules), or a heart to heart where the Socialite gushes about how much the person means to them.

basic.name: Intoxicating Aura
basic.call: “By My Voice to those who watched my Performance: Short Intoxicate”
basic.description: The Socialite has an intoxicating effect on those around them. After performing for an audience, the Socialite may gather their will and lull the audience into an intoxicated state. The roleplay of that state is up to the affected, and may be ended at any time. The call should be just loud enough to cover those who were paying attention to the performance, and no louder.

basic.name: Mithridatization
basic.description: Thin lace gloves do very little to protect against exposure to the poisons sprinkled into drinks and foodstuffs. To one end, this exposure can be quite deadly, but some Socialites have acquired a taste for the almondy brush of death. The Socialite may gain a Protect vs. Poison by Poison when applying toxin to a weapon, or a Protect vs Ingested Poison by Poison when applying it to food/drink.

basic.name: Plausible Deniability
basic.call: “[Name or Description] Short Subtle Obey by Mind: Believe the reason I gave you until I leave your line of sight.”
basic.description: Even the wittiest interrogator can be dazzled by a Socialite’s confident words… if only for a short while. It’s not about outrunning the bear, it’s about outrunning your friend who’s slower than you. Socialites have mastered the tactic of getting out when the coins are down. After explaining they could not have possibly done the thing (that they likely did) for at least 10 seconds, and providing a somewhat believable excuse (“Couldn't have been me, I was blind that day. Eyes got eaten by birds. It was the one armed man!”), the Socialite may force their victims to believe their lie long enough for them to escape unharmed.

basic.name: Pocket Sand
basic.description: Sometimes things don’t go as planned, and sometimes things really don’t go as planned. In such scenarios the Socialite must out think, out maneuver, and outperform the opposition… but if that fails, then Pocket Sand!

basic.name: Poison Dart
basic.incantation: Quick Count of 30 preparing a poison dart (thrown weapon in the form of a dart or dagger)
basic.description: The only difference between murder and an assassination is the intended target and their perceived importance. When timelines get short, Socialites sometimes find they cannot simply hire it out and need to do things themselves… even though their preferred subtleties might not be applicable. Poison Darts in the form of thrown weapons do not require the Thrown Weapons Skill to prepare or throw. This Power can only be prepared outside of combat on a Packet or Thrown Weapon. Once the dart is prepared, it will remain “charged” until it is put down or thrown, or until the Socialite completes a Rest.

basic.name: Rotten Tomato
basic.description: Sticks and stones may break bones, but words still hurt my feelings. The Socialite has learned to weave insults to make even the most stoic enraged. By spending a Spike, the Socialite may call “Quick Berserk by Mind.” If the materia is completely red (or looks like a tomato), this Power refreshes on a Short Rest, otherwise it refreshes on a Long Rest. Note that the insult is the Incantation, so the character’s foot must be planted, one hand free, etc. just like a normal Incantation.

basic.name: Soothe
basic.call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: [Question]”, “[Name or Description] Heal 5”, “[Name or Description] Cure All Conditions”
basic.description: A Heart may engage a member of their Group in a long conversation for a few minutes, after which they may inquire into their wellbeing and determine how to be a supportive force in their life. The target must engage with the Socialite, otherwise this Power cannot be used, otherwise they may be asked any or all of the questions below: • Are you under the effects of any Charm, Dominate, or Obey Effects, and if so which ones? • “Have you lied to me in the last five minutes?” • Is this your true form? • “Are you under the effects of any curses, if so which ones? • “How are you feeling generally? • Is there anything you wish you could tell me about, but haven't out of fear, social pressure, or desire to not be a burden to me and if so what is it?” The Socialite may choose to either Heal the target for 5 or Cure all Conditions on the target at the conclusion of their talk, at which point the Power is exhausted until the character’s next long rest.

basic.name: Sizing Up the Opposition
basic.call: [Name or Description], Subtle Discern by Mind: [Question]
basic.description: “Our waitress is left-handed and the Legionnaire sitting up at the counter weighs two hundred fifteen pounds and knows how to handle himself.” Socialites rely on their wit and observation to survive, and have learned to uncover threats. Once an opponent has been identified they can be negotiated with. Socialites may ask one of the below questions once per Individual per Short Rest after the Focus is completed: • Do you intend to harm us? • Are you willing to negotiate a peaceful resolution? • Will you accept a bribe?

options.text: Do you intend to harm us?
Are you willing to negotiate a peaceful resolution?
Will you accept a bribe?

basic.name: Time to Go
basic.call: “Cure [Slow, Root, or Disable Leg] by Mind”
basic.description: Getting stabbed is usually fairly low on a Socialite’s agenda, and magical bonds yanking at their heels typically only help those trying to stab them. The Socialite may twist their will to end a Slow, Root, or Disable condition on a target. If this power targets the Socialite’s Bodyguard, the Delivery may be Verbal and the Socialite also receives the same Cure (use the same call).

basic.name: To Dance with Death
basic.call: “[Name or Description] Piercing [X] by Poison, Short Taint by Poison”
basic.description: Dancing can be such an intimate time, with so many opportunities to subtly betray one so close. The Socialite is not one to sleep on such an opportunity. After dancing for at least a full minute, the Socialite may conclude the dance by calling “[Name or Description] Piercing [X] by Poison, Short Taint by Poison,” where X is equal to the character’s levels in Socialite. This power cannot be used in combat, must be used on their most recent dance partner, and that character must have engaged you in dance back. Remember to get consent before touching other players.

advanced.name: Battlefield Commander
advanced.description: When pressed into combat, the Socialite is best able to serve while heavily guarded and calling the shots from the back. Their quick wit and observant nature are cornerstones of the Socialite’s battlefield prowess. Weaving inspiring words to benefit their allies is what they do best. The Socialite may use the following abilities to grant boons to their allies. The Socialite must Focus for a Quick Count of 100 to grant each one and the Socialite cannot grant themself boons. • “Imbue Weapon with 3” • “Mend 3 Armor” • “Heal 1” • “Grant Protect vs Packets” • “Grant Protect vs [Mind/Fear]” Once the first boon is granted, the Socialite may grant additional boons at-will (with a Focus Quick 100 Count) until they take a Rest or  become Helpless.

advanced.name: Enticing Opulence
advanced.call: “[Name or Description] Charm by Mind”, Explanation
advanced.description: Fine jewelry and expensive silks are the staple of the Socialite, and often, these items are carried in abundance by them. Gift giving is a Socialite favorite, and the giving of expensive gifts even more-so. When the Socialite uses this power, they must show their target an item worth 10 Wealth. When they do, they may offer it out as a gift to their target, and, should their target take the item, the Socialite may immediately call “[Name or Description] Charm by Mind.” The Socialite must explain that the target is under the effects of this Charm until they are no longer in possession of the item given to them, or the end of the Event, whichever comes first.

advanced.name: Fortress of the Mind
advanced.description: The Socialite can keep a certain part of their mind separate and inviolate. This part will watch over the character’s personality, even if that personality is suborned by enchantment magic. While under the effect of a Condition using the Mind accent, the character may at any time call “Dispel Mind by Mind”. This can be done even if the character has been ordered not to.

advanced.name: Gathering of the Moot
advanced.call: “By My Voice, Grant 3 Barrier by Mind to each person who told a story at this gathering.”
advanced.description: Out of combat, the Socialite gathers together at least ten people in a public area, each telling stories of their recent trials and tribulations. The storytellers gather these tales and lessons, reflect on them, and offer wisdom to those gathered. Each person who told a story and heard the collective wisdom shared may benefit. Those who did not tell a story or left before the end gain no benefit.

advanced.name: Grace Under Duress
advanced.description: The Socialite is the master of their mind and is able to steel themself against mental trials. Choose one of the following Accents when this power is used: Fear or Mind. The Socialite may then Focus for a Quick 100 to Grant themselves a Protect vs the chosen Accent. They may re-apply this Protect by Focusing for another Quick 100 until they complete a Short Rest.

advanced.name: Hard to Read
advanced.call: “Subtle Short Grant Obfuscated vs Discern by Mind”
advanced.description: The fine art of masking one’s true feelings is a long and practiced skill for every charming Socialite, as it could easily mean the difference between a struck trade agreement, or a diplomatic parlay falling through. The Socialite is able to curtail the reading of their features and tells for a while, leaving those who would learn their inner turmoil in the dark. The Socialite becomes Obfuscated vs Discern effects with the Mind accent. This Condition ends when the Socialite has used it a number of times equal to their Socialite class level, or they take a Short Rest, whichever comes first.

advanced.name: Inspiring Performance
advanced.call: “By My Voice to my audience: Refresh Novice Spell or Long Rest Basic Power by Mind,” Explanation
advanced.description: By presenting a work of art, trinket, or performance, the Socialite can boost the energy and strength of some of the attendees. The performance should be substantive and not a perfunctory show to enable the use of the power. Those who are in the Socialite’s audience get to Refresh a Novice Spell-Slot or Long Rest Basic Power. The Socialite cannot benefit from their own Inspiring Performance as they were not in the audience.

advanced.name: Liquid Courage
advanced.call: “[Name or Description] Grant Protect vs Fear and Poison by Poison”
advanced.description: As a master of poisons, the Socialite knows the best defense against their own tools. Having developed their own potent brew to counter toxic effects, the Socialite may line up a round of shots for up to 6 people. Each person who consumes the brew gains a Protect vs Fear, and Poison by Poison as the liquid coats the inside of their stomach and sturdies their mind.

advanced.name: Let’s You and Them Fight
advanced.call: “[Name or Description] Quick Dominate by Mind: Attack someone else.”
advanced.description: Socialites are quite adept at diverting the focus of their foes when the need arises. With a bit of flair, this can take the focus off the Socialite and place it on someone else more suitable to violence. The Socialite may, after being struck in melee combat, target their attacker and utter a quick diversionary phrase (ex, “Look over there!”), and then cause the target to seek vengeance elsewhere with a “[Name or Description] Quick Dominate by Mind.” The only command the Socialite may give the dominated foe is “Attack [description]”.

advanced.name: Mark for Death
advanced.description: The Socialite may place a contract on a named individual by going to the Plot Staff and paying a bounty of 25 Wealth to make a 50 Wealth bounty (Staff will provide the other 25). This bounty will be held in escrow by Plot Staff, and may be added to by characters and NPCs as outlined below. Until the end of the Event, any character who knows about the bounty may provide proof to the Plot Staff of the death of the target, and collect the bounty. • The Socialite may call “Counter, Death Mark” if they are attacked (damage or Effects) by the bounty’s target. They can do this once per Short Rest. For this bounty to be active, the Socialite must post the individual’s name, likeness, and description in at least three conspicuous locations, as well as giving instructions for collecting the bounty and the requirement for proof. If members of Staff notice that the posters have been gone for a reasonable amount of time without being replaced, they can decide to cancel the bounty, and the Socialite may return to Staff to collect the original ante’d wealth. Another Socialite with Mark for Death may pay 5 Wealth to add an additional 10 to a Bounty other than their own, without expending their power by visiting Plot and naming the specific Bounty they wish to add their name to. However, they must also conspicuously add their name to each of the three posters of the original Bounty before doing so.

advanced.name: Method Acting
advanced.call: “[Name or Description] Long Obey by Mind: Trust me when I say that I belong to [Name of Organization].”
advanced.description: The Socialite is a master of mingling between different social circles and forging strong bonds. Some circles are outside of their grasp, but they are, to their credit, usually able to ‘blend in’ with the best of them.

advanced.name: Mostly Harmless
advanced.call: “[Name or Description] Short Repel by Mind”
advanced.description: Nobody wants to stomp the flowers… usually. The Socialite is adept at convincing combatants in a bar fight or a theater of war that there are bigger fish to fry. While not brandishing any Melee weapons, the Socialite may Call “[Name or Description] Short Repel by Mind” to a creature who they believe is about to attack them.

advanced.name: Party Planner
advanced.description: After spending at least twenty minutes preparing for hosting a party in a specific location, the Socialite gains special powers for the event that will go away only when the party ends or the Socialite leaves the party location. The Socialite can use any of the following at-will (all effects are “by Mind”): • “It can be believed that your drink is now alcoholic.” • Touch: One beverage. • “It can be believed that you are now totally sober.” • Touch: One individual. • “[Name or Description] Obey by Mind: Leave this venue and do not return for one hour.” • Verbal: One Individual • An Individual may only be targeted by this effect once per party. • “By My Voice, Expose Intoxicated” • Intoxication is up to the player and could be in any form of inebriation. • Voice: All who can hear. • “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: Do you mean imminent harm to anyone here?” • Verbal: One Individual • “[Name or Description] Long Obey by Mind: If you have had any food or drink from this party, you may not initiate harm to anyone at this party while you are here.” • Verbal: One Individual The Socialite can also do a number of effects from the following list equal to their Socialite-level per party: • “[Name or Description] Short Sleep by Mind” • Verbal: Individual. • “[Name or Description] Heal to Full by Mind” • Verbal: Individual. • “By My Voice, Heal 1 to Dying by Mind” • Verbal: By My Voice. • “[Name or Description] Grant Protect vs Weapon Damage” • Verbal: Individual • “[Name or Description] Grant Protect vs Packet Effect” • Verbal: Individual • “[Name or Description] Grant Protect vs Verbal or Voice” • Verbal: Individual As long as the party has lasted at least 45 minutes, the Socialite can do the following once: • “By My Voice to Party Guests: Heal to Full by Mind” As long as the party has lasted at least 20 minutes, and is then interrupted, the Socialite can do the following once: • “By My Voice to Party Guests: Grant Three Barrier by Mind”

advanced.name: Peacebind
advanced.call: “Long Obey by Mind: Use no weapons nor offensive spells in this building until attacked.”
advanced.description: Peacebind encourages a safe location. It cannot shut down all attacks, but it does give some measure of security. Peacebind can only be used on a Helpless or Willing target because it is a Touch Power, and occasionally situations will make it clear Peacebinding is a requirement to enter a specific meeting or area. The wording of the Call can be adjusted from “building” to “tent”, “structure”, “corral”, etc. for clarity, so long as it is a contained area with obviously marked physical borders.

advanced.name: Sense Duplicity
advanced.call: “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: Do you believe the last thing you said was a lie?”or “[Name or Description] Subtle Discern by Mind: Have you knowingly told a lie in the past five minutes”
advanced.description: By listening to subtle cues in the speaker’s voice, a Socialite may determine if a spoken statement was a lie (according to the speaker).

advanced.name: Squirreled Away
advanced.description: The Socialite knows the best hiding places for coins. A Socialite with this Power treats all Wealth on their person as having a Black Key marker, and does not have to hand over any during a search.

classSkills.name: Gambler’s Visage
classSkills.description: The Socialite may always choose to answer “Unreadable” to any Discern that attempts to determine their emotional state, whether they intend harm to another, or whether they are cheating at a game.

classSkills.name: The Right Hand
classSkills.description: The Socialite is a creature of beauty, a flower in a field, and a damsel in distress… when they want to be. Otherwise they have a Bodyguard. After a detailed conversation and a written contract, a Socialite may bind their target into an agreement to protect them from harm. The contract must stipulate that the Bodyguard will only provide that service for one individual: the Socialite. This contract is valid for at least a Long Rest, but may be extended automatically and indefinitely. A Socialite may only retain one Bodyguard at a time, unless they have purchased a Power such as “Additional Bodyguard” which overrides this limitation. The Power can be ended at any time either by the stipulations of the contract or by mutual agreement of Bodyguard and Socialite.Socialites that have purchased The Right Hand may purchase one Power from the Right Hand Powers list for Build Points, and may purchase another every 3 levels (4th, 7th, 10th, 13th, etc.) of Socialite they have. For Example: Lord Kendrynn takes The Right Hand at Socialite level 2. He immediately may purchase one class power for which he meets the prerequisites and which has Requirement: The Right Hand. When Lord Kendrynn takes his 4th level in Socialite, he will pick an additional class power for which he meets the requirements and which has Requirement: The Right Hand. This is true for level 7, 10, 13, etc.

classSkills.name: Additional Bodyguard
classSkills.description: Requirements: The Right Hand, Socialite Level 4 Incantation: RPCall: None Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate The Socialite has mastered the arts of social manipulation such that they may have an additional Bodyguard. The Socialite may use The Right Hand power to designate one other individual as their Bodyguard. Purchasing this Power also grants the Socialite the privilege of purchasing another Right Hand Power.

classSkills.name: Inspirational Leader
classSkills.incantation: Inspirational speech of at least two minutes.
classSkills.description: Whether walking the lines before a battle, delving into a dungeon, or starting off a council meeting, Socialites know how to inspire allies. The Socialite should give a speech lasting at least two minutes. It may include tactical or strategic information, but it must also attempt to inspire and motivate. This speech is an Incantation, so the Socialite cannot move their foot, must have one hand free, and cannot be disturbed while giving it (or else begin again). Once that rousing speech is completed, the Socialite can inspire individuals who listened to the speech. For each individual who the Socialite believes was motivated by the speech, the Socialite can give them a few individual words of encouragement, and give one of the following boons by Touch: • “Short Grant 1 Bonus Maximum Spike” • “Short Grant 5 Barrier by Mind” • “Grant Protect vs Packets by Mind” The Socialite may give themself any or all of these boons upon completion of the speech.

options.text: “Short Grant 1 Bonus Maximum Spike”
“Short Grant 5 Barrier by Mind”
“Grant Protect vs Packets by Mind” The Socialite may give themself any or all of these boons upon completion of the speech.

classSkills.name: Utilitarian
classSkills.description: The Socialite learns an additional Utility Power from the Socialite Class Powers.

rightHandPowers.name: Commission of Protection
rightHandPowers.description: A Bodyguard wearing the favor of their charge feels the weight of responsibility and can shrug off wounds in the name of duty. A trinket, token, necklace or similar adornment needs to be gifted to the Bodyguard during a time of peace (out of combat) so they may be reminded of what they fight for later. At this same time, the Bodyguard should be reminded about the effects of this Power. The Socialite may, “Grant One Barrier to Bodyguard” at-will (after the Refresh) while the Bodyguard is visibly displaying the object of their ward’s favor. Out-of-combat, the Socialite can Grant 3 Barrier all at once by Calling “Grant Three Barrier to Bodyguard”. Stacking limits will still apply.

rightHandPowers.name: Holding Out For a Hero
rightHandPowers.call: “Short Grant Power: Save the Day”, Explanation
rightHandPowers.description: After speaking for a few moments with their Bodyguard, the Socialite may ask them “Subtle Discern by Mind: Would you save me if I were in danger?” If the response is yes, the Socialite may Grant them the Save the Day Power below by explaining the Power to them.

rightHandPowers.name: Save the Day
rightHandPowers.call: “[Name or Description] Quick Provoke by Fear”
rightHandPowers.description: The recollection of their oath fuels the Bodyguard with strength to protect the Socialite, albeit in their own way. The Bodyguard may Call “[Name or Description], Quick Provoke by Fear” on any Target who seems to be within melee range of the Socialite.

rightHandPowers.name: It's Not Safe to Go Alone
rightHandPowers.call: “[Bodyguard’s Name or Description] Grant Protect vs Damage”
rightHandPowers.description: Socialites don’t make a habit of wandering around on their own, and seeing to their Bodyguard’s safety is mutually beneficial. Once per Short Rest the Socialite may bolster a Bodyguard. The Socialite grants a Protect vs. Damage. The Socialite may not give this Protect to the same Bodyguard more than once per Long Rest.

rightHandPowers.name: Magical Empowerment
rightHandPowers.description: Bodyguards may be inspired and call upon their inner reserves to accomplish more than they thought possible. The Bodyguard may Refresh 1 Novice or Adept Spell Slot after a quick pep talk with the Socialite.

rightHandPowers.name: Rise Above This
rightHandPowers.call: “[Bodyguard’s Name or Description] Heal 1 to Dying”
rightHandPowers.description: The Socialite knows how to rouse their companions, and their words can keep even the most desperate from teetering from the ledge. After a word or two of motivation or encouragement, they may call “[Bodyguard’s Name or Description] Heal 1 to Dying.”

rightHandPowers.name: Steel Yourself
rightHandPowers.call: “[Bodyguard’s Name or Description] Grant Protect vs Mind”
rightHandPowers.description: The Socialite knows how to convince their Bodyguard that everything will be alright, granting them one Protect vs Mind with a short pep talk.

rightHandPowers.name: Training Regiment
rightHandPowers.description: Through training with their Bodyguard, the Socialite learns their potential and drives them to excel martially even when the Bodyguard is at a breaking point. The Bodyguard may Refresh 1 Short Rest martial power after a quick pep talk with the Socialite.

rightHandPowers.name: Hidden Weapon
rightHandPowers.description: Bodyguards aren’t particularly useful if they cannot defend the Socialite. The pair are used to taking advantage of the Socialite’s quick diversions to sneak weapons into places they are normally forbidden. To use this Power, the Socialite must give the Bodyguard a brightly colored cloth to attach or tie to a weapon. At this time, the Power should be explained to the Bodyguard.

rightHandPowers.name: Concealed Weapon
rightHandPowers.call: “By My Voice Long Obey by Mind: Ignore the [color] flagged weapon.”
rightHandPowers.effect: ObeyThe Bodyguard may use “By My Voice Long Obey by Mind: Ignore the [color] flagged weapon.” The Bodyguard cannot use, or allow others to use, the weapon in combat until the flag has been removed.

rightHandPowers.name: Rousing Inspiration
rightHandPowers.call: “[Bodyguard’s Name or Description] Refresh Two Spikes”
rightHandPowers.description: When muscles tire and the will to continue flags, a voice of encouragement can be the best thing at your back. Most Socialites are experts in knowing exactly what to say to keep a stalwart soldier fighting… or goading them on into cleaning up a fight, at least. The Socialite Refreshes two of their Bodyguard’s Spikes with quick verbal encouragement.

rightHandPowers.name: Third Hand
rightHandPowers.description: After a brief inspection of the Bodyguard’s materia, and an explanation of this Power, the Socialite becomes able to hold charged Packets that their Bodyguard has cast. The Socialite and the Bodyguard can pass Packets back and forth without them being discharged. The Socialite cannot use the Packets, only hold them. Packets held by the Socialite will not discharge or lose their charge if the Bodyguard casts other Spells. The Socialite is subject to all the rules for holding the Packet, but once the Socialite completes a Short Rest and this Power ends, any Packets still being held will also become inert. When the Packet is passed back and forth, the two should remind each other of the Call held within the Packet.For Example: Trobriand is the Bodyguard for Jheshyra, who has activated Third Hand. Trobriand, an Artificer, creates a charged Packet with Acid-Soak. He hands it to Jheshyra and says “Reminder: Wounding 8 by Acid”. He then does some other things, including creating other Packets and throwing them, before asking for the Packet back. Jheshyra hands him the Packet and says “Reminder: Wounding 8 by Acid”.

rightHandPowers.name: Guard and Savior
rightHandPowers.description: After the Socialite gives the Bodyguard instructions on when to use this Power, they should give the Call and any additional explanation needed. This lets the Bodyguard Heal the Socialite for 5 Points by Touch. Once used, the Socialite can give the Power again as long as they have completed a Short Rest. Remember that one can not have more than one Imbued Power at a time.

rightHandPowers.name: This Is My Bodyguard
rightHandPowers.incantation: RP: Explaining that your Bodyguard is going with you wherever you’re going
rightHandPowers.call: “[Name or Description] Obey by Fear: Let my Bodyguard in with me if I am allowed in.”
rightHandPowers.description: The Socialite may make allowances for their Bodyguard in any space they themselves are welcome. If a venue would try to prohibit the Bodyguard from entering with the Socialite, the Socialite may pull the contract to ensure their passage.

name: Sourcerer
startingSkills: The Ritual of Beginnings: Basic Arcane (4), Warcaster (5)
The Contract With the Other - Choose one of the following:
Extended Capacity - Novice x2 (6)
Additional Cantrip (6)
Patron [Character’s Choice] (4), Gift of Hateful Retribution I (2)

2.bonus: Innate Bonus Cantrip: Cancel, Astride the Weave

innate.name: Internal Battery
innate.description: The Sourcerer can meditate and recenter their connection to magic’s undercurrents to rebuild focus and refresh their Arcane Spell-slots. The Sourcerer can spend a Short Rest communing with themselves, contemplating their Source, or practicing their incantations quietly. The Sourcerer can perform this special study and refresh all of their expended Arcane spell-slots. If the Sourcerer has used any other Innate Power from another class to refresh their Arcane spell slots this long rest they may not use Internal Battery. Additional benefits come if this study is done in an area of powerful arcane magic (such as an Arcane Locus), and performs a personal rite (determined by the Sourcerer). Doing so, the Sourcerer gains three additional Arcane Novice-tier Spell-Slots. These Spell-Slots last until the Sourcerer completes a Long Rest. Additional benefits may be possible (see the Skills, Perks, and Flaws document for more details).Finally, if the Sourcerer has not worn non-Barrier armor at any point prior to the use of this Power this event they may once per Event refresh Internal Battery immediately. In doing so they lose the ability to wear non-Barrier armor for the remainder of the Event.

innate.name: Arcane Source
innate.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 1 Incantation: None Call: None Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate The Sourcerer has an item (See Arcane Source, above) that is special to them. The character may begin with an object of their choice empowered as a Source. Arcane Sources must be a solid durable object at least 5” in one dimension and 1” in the others. After completing a Long Rest, a Sourcerer may empower a new object as their Source (the other one loses its Source properties). This Source must follow all rules defining an appropriate object. If that object is not an Empowered Source, it is attuned to one of the following Accents: Acid, Ice, Flame, or Lightning, chosen at the time of designation as a Source. This Chosen Accent is called their Arcane Accent. The Sorcerer may only have one Source at any one time, and this Source will only work for the Sourcerer who designated it. However, the Source comes with some risk: should the Sourcerer find themself without an Arcane Source, they will lose access to all Sourcerer Spell-Slots and Known Spells until the Arcane Source is back in hand or a new object is designated (at a Long Rest, see above). Once per Short Rest, the Sourcerer may re-Empower a broken or shattered Arcane Source by performing a personal rite over the Source. If it was Shattered, the Source must first be Rebuilt. Once the Sourcerer has reached 10th Sourcerer level, when empowering an Arcane Source, the Sourcerer may choose an additional Arcane Accent for the Source. While wielding the enhanced Source, the caster may use either Accent when the [Arcane Accent] is noted. When choosing this Arcane Accent for a given Power, all instances of the [Arcane Accent] must be the same Accent. Note: The Sourcerer’s Arcane Source takes the place of the Basic Arcane Spellbook. The Sourcerer cannot use Powers or Skills that rely on a Spellbook for their Sourcerer Spells.

innate.name: Astride the Weave
innate.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 2 Incantation: None Call: None Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate The Sourcerer’s channeling of magic through the Arcane Source unbinds them from the traditional need to remain still while gathering power, allowing the arcane theurgist to move while incanting their Base Class: Sourcerer Cantrips and Spells. This delicate balance is lost if the character is wearing any non-Barrier Armor and only applies to Base Class Sourcerer Cantrips and Spells. This ability does not override the specific requirements of the spell being cast, thus, if the specific spell itself calls for the caster to remain in place, or pivot on one foot, or any other requirement, the caster must abide by those restrictions.

innate.name: Enduring Source
innate.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 3 Incantation: RP: Focus Quick 100 miming rebuilding the source. Call: “Rebuild Source” Target: Self                                         Duration: Instantaneous Delivery: None                                          Refresh: Short Rest Accent: None                                        Effect: Rebuild Once per Short Rest, the Sourcerer may Rebuild a broken or shattered Arcane Source.

innate.name: Dissipate Curse
innate.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 5 Incantation: Focus Quick 30 Call: “Cure [Disarm, Disable, Root, Bind]” Target: Self                                         Duration: Instantaneous Delivery: None                                         Refresh: Short Rest Accent: None                                        Effect: Cure Once per Short Rest, the Sourcerer may Cure Condition [Disarm, Disable, Root, Bind] on themselves by Focusing for a Quick 30.

innate.name: Power of Magic
innate.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 7 Incantation: None Call: None Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest The Sourcerer’s channeling of magic through the Arcane Source allows them to make sacrifices other more traditional arcane casters cannot. Upon completion of a Long Rest, the Sourcerer may Sacrifice a Cantrip that they know, other than Cancel, losing the ability to cast it until their next Long Rest. In exchange, the Sourcerer may select an Accent they have a Source for and add +1 damage to any spell (not Cantrip) cast with that accent, for a Long Rest. Note: In all cases, the normal stacking rules apply to these damage bonuses.

innate.name: Rote Invocation
innate.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 10 Incantation: None Call: None Target: Self                                        Duration: Long Rest Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Long Rest Accent: None                                                            Effect: Grant Signature Spell The Sourcerer’s time harnessing the arcane has led to their ability to gradually intake mana from their environment, fuelling the spellwork with which they are most familiar. Once per Long Rest, you may designate a Sourcerer spell you know as a Signature Spell by expending a spell slot that could be used to cast that spell. Until your next Long Rest, you may cast your Signature Spell once per Short Rest as if you had spent the spell slot used to designate the spell. The spell may not have the Duration of Long Rest.

classSkills.name: Additional Source
classSkills.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 4 Incantation: RP Short Rest to Create; Focus Quick 100 while altering your binding to a different Arcane Source. Call: None Target: Self                                        Duration: Permanent Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Short Rest, Immediate The Sourcerer has mastered the arts of Source manipulation such that they may have an additional Arcane Source bound. After finishing a Short Rest linked to an object, the Sourcerer may designate a new item to become their Source in addition to their first. The Source must be aligned with one of the normal Accents that the Sourcerer can choose for Sources. While a character must hold an Arcane Source in their hand to use its Accent, the character may switch between two different Arcane Sources that they are bound to with a Focus Quick 100.

classSkills.name: Accent Substantiation
classSkills.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 4 Incantation: None Call: None Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None The Sourcerer can bend the elemental forces of reality itself to mold to their purpose. Many cantrips and spells have optional modes or bonuses that only apply when the accent matches a specific requirement. This skill allows the Sourcerer to gain the benefits of these options with another Accent. This skill may be purchased multiple times, each time allowing the Sourcerer to choose from the substitution list below. Arcane Accent Substituted Effect Agony Acid Shadow Acid Force Flame Radiant Flame Fear Ice Void Ice Divine Lightning Mind Lightning For Example: Trivian the Sourcerer is wielding his Arcane Source with the Agony accent. When casting a spell that has a special effect that benefits from the Acid Accent, the Agony Accent counts as the Acid Accent for purposes of that spell. Note that this does not change the accent of that spell.

classSkills.name: Unsuppressable
classSkills.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 5 Incantation: None Call: “Concentration” Target: Self                                        Duration: Passive Delivery: None                                        Refresh: None The Sourceror can not be interrupted while incanting by strikes or Effects, or defending from strikes or Effects. The Sourceror can still be interrupted by conditions that would prevent being able to incant, such as being Silenced, being rendered Helpless by damage taken, being Imprisoned, etc. Additionally, the Sourceror may call defenses retroactively after completion of the incantation, by calling “Concentration” followed by any relevant defenses. Note that while this Power affects Incantations, it does not stop non-Incantation Focus requirements such as for Refresh or the cooldown time on various Spells.

classSkills.name: Interlaced Shell
classSkills.description: Requirements: Sourcerer Level 6 Incantation: None Call: “Grant 2 Barrier to Self” Target: Self                                        Duration: Instantaneous Delivery: None                                        Refresh: Immediate Accent: None                                        Effect: Grant Barrier On the completion of a Short Rest the Sourcerer may grant themselves 2 Barrier by calling “Grant 2 Barrier to Self.”

classSkills.name: Cantrip Scholar
classSkills.description: The Sourcerer learns an additional Cantrip from the Sourcerer Class Powers or Cantrips.

classSkills.name: Faded Slumber
classSkills.description: The Sourcerer may post an information card on their bunk or bed, stating to others that they are insubstantial while in that bed. This Power can only be activated by Focusing while in the bed for a Slow Count of 60. It then lasts until the Sourcerer says “Cure Insubstantial” and takes down the card, or leaves the bed.

cantrips.name: Cancel
cantrips.description: This minor cantrip takes the power out of a Spell-Packet (or multiple Spell-Packets) that has already been charged and returns that power to the caster. The Spell-Packet becomes inert, and the caster refreshes the spell-slot that was used to charge the Spell-Packet. If the spell charges more than one Spell-Packet, all the Spell-Packets must still be charged in order to cancel the spell.

cantrips.name: Charge
cantrips.description: The first packet of the next Cantrip or Spell that the Sourcerer casts deals 1 more damage. Enhancement: If the Sourcerer has Warcaster and expends a Spike, all Spell-Packets charged from the next Cantrip or Spell the Sourcerer casts are affected by Charge instead of just the next one. (Reminder: Warcaster does not increase the damage of Cantrips.)

cantrips.name: Circle of Four
cantrips.description: The Sourcerer calls forth a ring of elemental power that wreaths their hand in visible crackling energy. The caster charges four Spell-Packets, one with each of the four elemental Accents (Acid/Flame/Ice/Lightning). These can be thrown in any order. If the Sourcerer expends a Spike when throwing a charged Spell-Packet from this Cantrip, the Call is changed to “Wounding 2 by [Whichever Element is being Thrown]” for that charged Spell-Packet.

cantrips.name: Wild Bolt
cantrips.description: This minor cantrip charges a Spell-Packet of materia with the Element of the Arcane Source’s Accent which will do 1 point of that Accent’s damage to those it hits. Enhancement: Expend a Spike: The Cantrip charges additional Spell-Packets equal to the Sourcerer’s Class-Level.

cantrips.name: Energy Shield
cantrips.description: The Sourcerer throws up a shield of energy to protect themself from the elements. This gives the caster one Protect from the Accent of their Arcane Source. If the Sourcerer has more than one Arcane Accent, they may have a Protect for each of those Accents.

cantrips.name: Infuriate
cantrips.description: A creature struck by this Spell-Packet suddenly sees the caster as an arch-enemy and hated nemesis.

cantrips.name: Mass Flux
cantrips.description: When the Sourcerer casts this Cantrip and expends a Spike, it changes the mass of the struck shield, pulling it to the ground for a moment. Unlike other Effects, if no duration is called, the duration of Disarm is Instantaneous, and the item may be picked up immediately upon hitting the ground.

cantrips.name: Synergistic Transfer
cantrips.description: The Sourcerer is able to twist the Weave to their needs when pressed. By Sacrificing two Barrier, the Sourcerer is able to activate an optional power of another Cantrip, as if they expended a Spike.

cantrips.name: Whispers of the Unseen
cantrips.description: This power taps into the innate understanding of the flows of the magic common to Sourcerers. The Sourcerer may ask each of these questions of a target per cast, but must recast to repeat a question. • What is [another] one of your Vulnerabilities? • What is [another] one of your Resistances? • What is your Spike Damage?

noviceSpells.name: Aether Lance
noviceSpells.description: When this spell is cast it charges two Spell-Packets with “Piercing 3 to Insubstantial by [Arcane Accent].” Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: Three Spell-Packets are charged that do “Piercing 5 to Insubstantial by [Arcane Accent].”

noviceSpells.name: Coalesce Scales
noviceSpells.description: This spell Grants 3 Barrier by [Arcane Accent] to the caster. Enhancement: If the Sourcerer’s Arcane Accent is Lightning, double the Barrier received. Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: Grants 5 Barrier. Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: Grants 7 Barrier.

noviceSpells.name: Destructive Resonance
noviceSpells.description: For the duration of this Spell, the Sourcerer is able to set up deadly resonances within a Helpless, Imprisoned, or Insubstantial target by Touch, causing the victim to literally shake apart under the ever increasing vibrations. The caster may call “Piercing 1 to [Helpless/Imprisoned\Insubstantial] by [Arcane Accent]” and can then repeat that after Focusing for a Quick Count of 30.

noviceSpells.name: Deteriorate
noviceSpells.call: “Dispel Summoned Armor by Acid” “Shatter Armor by Force”
noviceSpells.description: When this Spell is cast, Deteriorate charges one Spell-Packet with “Dispel Summoned Armor by Acid.” Enhancement: If the Sourcerer’s Arcane Accent of their Source is Acid, this charges two Spell-Packets and they may call “Shatter Armor by Force” instead.

noviceSpells.name: Galvanic Shield
noviceSpells.description: Until the Sourcerer’s next Short Rest, each time the caster lands a Lightning-Accented Cantrip or Spell on a foe, the caster may Call “Grant 1 Barrier by Lightning” to themself. Note: Recall that the Stacking Limit for Barrier from different sources is 3, including separate applications from this spell.

noviceSpells.name: Flee
noviceSpells.call: “Quick Obey by [Arcane Accent]: Move away from me.”
noviceSpells.description: Upon striking a target with a Spell-Packet, the target is compelled to remove themselves from the company of the caster. The Sourcerer is able to charge a Spell-Packet with “Quick Obey by [Arcane Accent]: Move away from me.”

noviceSpells.name: Icy Veins
noviceSpells.description: Once cast, the Sourcerer gains the Icy Blood Power until their next Short Rest.

noviceSpells.name: Iron Maiden
noviceSpells.call: “Quick Disable [Left Arm/Right Arm] by [Arcane Accent]”, “Quick Disable Both Arms by [Arcane Accent]”
noviceSpells.description: When this spell is cast, the Sourcerer drains the strength from a foe’s arm. Enhancement: Expend a Spike: Both arms may be targeted. “Quick Disable Both Arms by [Arcane Accent]”

noviceSpells.name: Melt
noviceSpells.call: “Dispel All Barrier by Acid”, Dispel All Protects by Acid”, “Wounding 3 by Acid”
noviceSpells.description: The Sourcerer charges two Spell-Packets that can do one of three things. The Spell-Packets need not have the same Call: • “Dispel All Barrier by Acid” • “Dispel All Protects by Acid” • “Wounding 3 by Acid” Enhancement: Expend a Spike: The caster may also choose “Shatter Physical Armor by Acid”

options.text: “Dispel All Barrier by Acid”
“Dispel All Protects by Acid”
“Wounding 3 by Acid” Enhancement: Expend a Spike: The caster may also choose “Shatter Physical Armor by Acid”

noviceSpells.name: Primal Ray
noviceSpells.description: Upon casting this spell, the Sourcerer charges two Spell-Packets with “4 [Arcane Accent].” Enhancement: Expend a Spike to charge an additional Spell-Packet. This enhancement may be used multiple times on the same cast, but no more than a total of ⅓ of the Sourcerer’s class level (rounded down). Enhancement: Expend a Spike to increase the damage by +1. This enhancement may be used multiple times on the same cast, up to the normal stacking cap of +3 damage. Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: Charges 3 Spell-Packets for 6 [Arcane Accent]. Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: Charges 4 Spell-Packets for 8 [Arcane Accent].

noviceSpells.name: Soulknife
noviceSpells.description: When this spell is cast, Soulknife charges a Spell-Packet with Piercing 1 by [Arcane Accent]. Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: An additional two Spell-Packets are charged and all Spell-Packets charged do Piercing 2 by [Arcane Accent].

noviceSpells.name: Spirit Link
noviceSpells.call: “Grant Protect vs [Disable, Root, and Slow]”, “Counter, Spirit Link”
noviceSpells.description: This spell weaves the Sourcerer’s power over them, locking their Arcane Source to them. Until their next Short Rest, any time they are affected by any Disarm, Disable, or Obey Effect that would cause them to drop or give away their Source, they may expend a Spike to Counter it by calling “Counter, Spirit Link”. In addition, when the Spell is cast, the Sourcerer gains one Protect against Disable, Root, and Slow.

noviceSpells.name: Startle
noviceSpells.description: The Sourcerer charges three Spell-Packets with the power to briefly disarm individuals.  A creature stuck by the spell has the named item or item held by the named limb warped to appear as a dangerous object, forcing them to shed the item as quickly as possible, and avoid picking it up for the duration.

noviceSpells.name: Aethereal Fade
noviceSpells.description: When this Spell is cast, the Sourcerer may enter a shadowed area, and become Insubstantial. While the character remains in shadow, they may walk or stand still as they please. They may not cross an unshadowed area or jump across unshadowed areas. If the caster allows themselves to be struck by sunlight, moonlight, torchlight, or other sources of light, the Spell immediately ends. At any time, the caster may call “Cure Insubstantial to Self” to end the effect.

noviceSpells.name: Warpfire
noviceSpells.call: “Wounding 4 by [Arcane Accent]”, “Wounding 6 by Flame”, “Piercing 4 by Force”
noviceSpells.description: When this spell is cast, Warpfire charges a Spell-Packet with Wounding 4 by [Arcane Accent]. If the Arcane Accent is Flame, the Spell does Wounding 6 instead. If the Arcane Accent is Force, the Spell does Piercing 4 instead. Enhancement: Adept Spell-Slot: Two additional Spell-Packets are charged.

adeptSpells.name: Bonesplinter
adeptSpells.description: When this spell is cast, Bonesplinter charges two Spell-Packets with “Wounding 6 by [Arcane Accent]”. As long as either Spell-Packet lands on a target and is not Prevented, the Sourcerer may deliver one additional verbal Call of “Weakness by Agony” to that target.

adeptSpells.name: Breach Teleport
adeptSpells.call: “Short Grant Insubstantial to Self”, “Cure Insubstantial to Self”
adeptSpells.description: This spell must be cast upon a Spell-Packet that has been marked with a symbol that is special to the caster. The incantation charges the Spell-Packet which is then thrown. As soon as it comes to rest, the Sourcerer will become insubstantial and must either move as quickly and directly as possible to the spot where the Spell-Packet came to rest, or end the spell immediately. They may not walk part way and then end the spell. Once see the Sourcerer arrives at the appropriate spot, they should call loudly “Cure Insubstantial to Self”. The Sourcerer can do nothing besides move to the Spell-Packet’s location while Insubstantial. Note that if the Spell-Packet hits an individual, the caster should inform the struck person that the Spell-Packet has no effect on them.

adeptSpells.name: Crimson Bargain
adeptSpells.call: “Short Grant Crimson Bargain”, “Refresh 1 Spike by Agony”
adeptSpells.description: Once cast, the Sourcerer may roleplay for a Quick 30 drawing their power from their blood and Sacrifice two Life Points to Refresh one Spike by Agony. This can be done as often as desired until the Sourcerer completes a Short Rest and ends the Spell.

adeptSpells.name: Devastating Eruption
adeptSpells.description: After finishing the Call for this Spell, the Sourcerer must hold up their Source with both hands. After a Focus Quick 100 of role-playing unraveling the arcane energies stored within it, the Sourcerer may overload the source, destroying it and calling “By My Voice, 4 by [Arcane Accent].” The Sourcerer takes the damage as Final. This Spell’s effects cannot be altered other than as listed in this Spell’s description. Enhancement: Expend up to four Spikes: Increase the damage of this Spell by two per Spike expended.Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: The Piercing Effect is added to the damage. Note: The Source is irrevocably destroyed in the process of casting this spell, and should be turned into the Ash Bin as soon as possible. It may not be Rebuilt.

adeptSpells.name: Enduring Corrosion
adeptSpells.description: For the duration of this spell, the Sourceror may follow up Acid accented attacks with additional damage.  Upon landing another Weapon or Packet attack with the Acid Accent, they may then verbally deliver one Call of “[Name or Description], 1 Acid” to the target hit. Enhancement: If the Sourcerer has Warcaster, they may instead Call “[Name or Description], 2 Acid”.

adeptSpells.name: Everflowing Shield
adeptSpells.description: While the Sourcerer has this enchantment active, they may Focus for Quick 100 and gain three Barrier at-will.Enhancement: If the Sourcerer’s Arcane Accent on the source they are using when they use the Focus Q100 is Ice, they instead may gain four Barrier.

adeptSpells.name: Immortal Focus
adeptSpells.description: When this Spell is cast, it invigorates the Sourcerer, heightening their potential. For the duration of the Spell, the Sourcerer gains +1 Bonus Maximum Spikes.

adeptSpells.name: Incandescent Spire
adeptSpells.description: Until the Sorcerer completes a Short Rest, the Sourcerer may at-will roleplay setting a particular materia alight for a Focus Quick 30 and then add their Spike Damage Bonus to the next damaging Spell-Packet they throw. Only Spell-Packets that will cause Flame-Accented damage will gain this damage bonus.

adeptSpells.name: Arcane Backlash
adeptSpells.description: The caster charges their aura with violent points of concussive force. Whenever the Caster is hit by a melee attack that deals damage, they may immediately Call “[Name or Description] Wounding 3 by [Arcane Accent]” to the person who struck them once per strike, up to 3 times before the spell is expended. Enhancement: If the Sourcerer’s Arcane Accent is Lightning, they instead do Wounding 4. Enhancement: Greater Spell-Slot: Ten charges are gained instead of three.

adeptSpells.name: Mystic Mirror
adeptSpells.description: This spell grants a Protection vs Packets to the Sourcerer. They may charge a Spell-Packet with the exact Call that the Prevented attack had, although when the Sourcerer throws their Spell-Packet, its Accent will be one of their Arcane Accents.

adeptSpells.name: Restful Prison
adeptSpells.call: “Short Imprison by Force to Self”, “Grant Short Rest”, “Cure Imprisoned”
adeptSpells.description: When this Spell is cast, the Sourcerer places themselves in a protective shell and rapidly enters a meditative trance. The Sourcerer is immediately imprisoned and should Call “Short Imprison by Force to Self”. They may, while imprisoned, Focus for a Slow Count of 60 to Call “Grant Short Rest.” The Sourcerer must then call “Cure Imprisoned” to exit. The Sourcerer may cast this Spell only once per Long Rest.

adeptSpells.name: Smothering Ash
adeptSpells.description: The Sourcerer can summon ash into the very lungs of their foes. When this spell is cast, Smothering Ash charges two Spell-Packets with “Wounding 6 by Flame”. As long as either Spell-Packet lands on a target and is not Prevented, the Sourcerer may deliver one additional verbal Call of “Short Silence by Flame” to that target.

adeptSpells.name: Spirit Chalice
adeptSpells.call: “Grant Spirit Chalice”, “Death to Helpless by [Arcane Accent]”, “Refresh 1 Spike”
adeptSpells.description: Until the end of the Sourcerer’s next Short Rest, they have the ability to use the death of others to power their magic. For the duration, the Sourcerer can touch a creature who is Helpless and cause it to die. The call for this death-touch is “Death to Helpless by [Arcane Accent].” If this is not Prevented, the Sourcerer may Refresh one Spike. This can be done as often as desired during the duration of the spell. Note: This is a Touch Spell and thus can only be used on the Helpless. It may be necessary to let the player/portrayer of the target know that they are being targeted by a Touch Spell if their eyes are closed or they are otherwise insensible.

adeptSpells.name: Splinter Shield
adeptSpells.call: “Shatter Shield by [Arcane Accent]”, “Grant 5 Barrier to Self”
adeptSpells.description: When this spell is cast, Splinter Shield charges a Spell-Packet with “Shatter Shield by [Arcane Accent]”. As long as the attack hits and was not Prevented, the Sourcerer may immediately grant themself 5 Barrier.

adeptSpells.name: Vaporize
adeptSpells.description: When this spell is cast, Vaporize charges two Spell-Packets with “Shatter [Item] by Acid”. The target may be an unattended physical item. Magic items are generally immune and their holder may call out “No Effect” in these cases. Enhancement: Expend a Spike during Incantation: An additional Spell-Packet is charged.

adeptSpells.name: Wrack
adeptSpells.description: When this spell is cast, Wrack charges a Spell-Packet with Short Drain by [Arcane Accent]. Enhancement: If cast with a Greater spell slot, three Spell-Packets are charged.
